§ 210.020 DEPARTMENT OF MENTAL HEALTH
210.020 Qualifications of commissioner
and other personnel ; compensation . ( 1 )
The Commissioner of Mental Health shall
be a qualified physician with approved
training in psychiatry and with a mini-
mum of five years ' successful experience
in clinical psychiatry and administrative
psychiatry . The assistant commissioner
shall have the same qualifications , except
that the minimum experience shall be
three years . Professional personnel of the
department shall possess such qualifica-
tions as are prescribed by the commis-
sioner .
( 2 ) The compensation of the commis-
sioner shall be fixed by the Governor ,
without regard to any limitation imposed
by KRS 64.640 . The compensation of
other personnel of the department shall
be fixed by the commissioner in accord-
ance with KRS 64.640 . ( 1952 , c . 50 , § 2 ;
1960 , с . 64 , § 1 )
Employment and compensation of employes of
statutory administrative departments ,
KRS 12.050 , 12.060

210.030 Advisory Council of Mental
Health . There hereby is created an Ad-
visory Council of Mental Health , within
the Department of Mental Health , which
shall consist of the Commissioner of Men-
tal Health , ex officio , and twelve mem-
bers , including physicians, psychiatrists
and laymen , appointed by the Governor ,
who shall serve for terms of four years ,
and until their successors are appointed ,
except that the members first appointed
shall be so appointed that the terms of
three members will expire each year . The
appointed members shall receive their ac-
tual and necessary expenses in attending
meetings of the council . The Governor
shall each year appoint a chairman from
among the psychiatrist members of the
council . The council shall serve in an
advisory capacity to the Commissioner of
Mental Health . (1952, c. 50 , § 3 )

tardation . ( 1 ) There shall be established
an Advisory Council on Mental Retarda-
tion attached to the Department of Men-
tal Health .
(2) The Advisory Council on Mental
Retardation shall be composed of five
members , appointed by the Governor for
terms of four years . Members shall serve
until their successors are appointed . The
board shall elect from its membership a
chairman . Members of the board shall
serve without compensation but shall be
reimbursed for necessary expenses in-
curred in the performance of their duties .
(3 ) The Advisory Council on Mental
Retardation shall act in an advisory ca-
Health in regard to the mental retardation
program . ( 1962 , c . 106 , Art . XI , § 5 )
Note : KRS 210.075 takes effect July 1, 1962 .

DEPARTMENT OF MENTAL HEALTH § 210.235
Commissioner of Mental Health may utilize
the services of the workers of the Depart-
ment of Health , and the social workers of
the Department of Welfare and the De-
partment of Economic Security , when au-
thorized by the commissioners of these
departments . The Commissioner of Mental
Health may authorize payment of the ac-
tual traveling expenses of the workers so
utilized . ( 1952 , с. 50 , § 19 ; 1954 , c . 14, § 2 ;
effective July 1, 1954)
210.200 Inspection visits to institutions .
At least four times in each calendar year ,
the Commissioner of Mental Health shall
visit or cause to be visited by the assist-
ant commissioner , or a physician of the
staff of one of the institutions , every state
institution over which the Department of
Mental Health has control . The visits
shall be made on such days and at such
hours of the day or night , and for such
length of time , as the inspecting officer
may choose . In addition to these regular
visits , the inspecting officer may make
such other visits as the commissioner
deems necessary . Every visit shall in-
clude an inspection of every part of the
institution , including the buildings and
grounds in connection therewith , an ex-
amination of all records and the methods
of administration the general and special
dietary , and of every patient confined
therein , especially those admitted since
the preceding visit, giving them suitable
opportunity to converse with the inspect-
ing officer , apart from the officers and
attendants of the institution . The inspect-
ing officer shall examine the officers , at-
tendants and other employes , and make
inquiries to determine their fitness for
their respective duties . ( 1952 , c . 50 , § 20 ;
effective July 1 , 1952)
210.210 Boards of visitors for institu-
tions . ( 1 ) The Commissioner of Mental
Health may appoint , for each of the in-
stitutions operated by his department , a
board of visitors consisting of not less
than three nor more than ten citizens hav-
ing an interest in the problems of mental
health. The members of each board shall
serve during the pleasure of the commis-
sioner, and without compensation .
(2 ) Each board of visitors shall visit
at least once a year the institution for
which it was appointed , and shall report
to the commissioner the conditions found
to exist in the institution , together with
recommendations for improvements in
the administration of the institution . The
board shall be given free access to the in-
stitution and its patients for the purpose
of such visits . ( 1952 , c . 50 , § 24 ; effec-
tive July 1 , 1952 )
210.220 Correspondence by patients
with commissioner or attorneys . No pa-
tient of any of the institutions operated
by the Department of Mental Health shall
be denied the right to correspond with an
attorney or with the commissioner . ( 1952 ,
c . 50 , § 21 ; effective July 1 , 1952 )
210.230 Records and forms . The Com-
missioner of Mental Health may prescribe
appropriate records to be maintained
covering the operations of the depart-
ment and of the institutions operated by
it, and covering commitment procedures .
Any record forms applicable to commit-
ment procedures shall be furnished to
each court having jurisdiction to commit
mentally
illpersons
,and the records con-
templated by such forms shall thereafter
bemade
bythe committing courts
.

§ 210.240 DEPARTMENT OF MENTAL HEALTH
210.240 Training schools . The Commis-
sioner of Mental Health is authorized to
establish training schools within the de-
partment or within any of the institutions
operated by the department , for the train-
ing of necessary personnel for the insti-
tutions , or may arrange for the training
of employes or prospective employes in
any public or private school or institution
having available facilities for that pur-
pose . Funds of the department may be
used to pay salaries to employes , or to
pay tuition and subsistence for employes
or prospective employes , while receiving
such training . Any employe or prospec-
tive employe who is paid a salary , or for
whom tuition and subsistence are fur-
nished , while receiving such training ,
shall be required to enter into a contract,
prior to receiving such training , that un-
less he continues in the employ of the
department for at least a period equiva-
lent to the training period, immediately
following the completion of such training ,
he will reimburse to the department the
sum paid to or for him by the department
during the period of his training . ( 1952 ,
c. 50 , § 23 ; effective July 1, 1952 )
210.250 Transfer of personnel , proper-
ties , funds , records, facilities and appro-

DEPARTMENT OF MENTAL HEALTH § 210.520
tionalization may be for the entire period
of care and treatment or for any portion
or portions thereof . The factors referred
to in this paragraph shall include the
patient's full record with due regard for
the location of the patient's family , char-
acter of the illness and probable duration
thereof, and such other factors as shall be
considered appropriate .
( c ) No state shall be obliged to receive
any patient pursuant to the provisions of
paragraph ( b ) of this article unless the
sending state has given advance notice of
its intention to send the patient ; furnished
all available medical and other pertinent
records concerning the patient ; given the
qualified medical or other appropriate
clinical authorities of the receiving state
an opportunity to examine the patient if
said authorities so wish ; and unless the
receiving state shall agree to accept the
patient .
(d) In the event that the laws of the re-
ceiving state establish a system of priori-
ties for the admission of patients , an in-
terstate patient under this compact shall
receive the same priority as a local patient
and shall be taken in the same order and
at the same time that he would be taken
if he were a local patient.
( e ) Pursuant to this compact , the de-
termination as to the suitable place of in-
stitutionalization for a patient may be re-
viewed at any time and such further trans-
fer of the patient may be made as seems
likely to be in the best interest of the
patient .
Article IV . ( a ) Whenever , pursuant to
the laws of the state in which a patient is
physically present , it shall be determined
that the patient should receive after -care
or supervision , such care or supervision
may be provided in a receiving state . If
the medical or other appropriate clinical
authorities having responsibility for the
care and treatment of the patient in the
sending state shall have reason to believe
that after - care in another state would be
in the best interest of the patient and
would not jeopardize the public safety ,
they shall request the appropriate authori-
ties in the receiving state to investigate
the desirability of affording the patient
such after -care in said receiving state , and
such investigation shall be made with all
reasonable speed . The request for investi-
gation shall be accompanied by complete
information concerning the patient's in-
tended place of residence and the identity
of the person in whose charge it is pro-
posed to place the patient , the complete
medical history of the patient , and such
other documents as may be pertinent .
(b ) If the medical or other appropriate
clinical authorities having responsibility
for the care and treatment of the patient
in the sending state and the appropriate
authorities in the receiving state find that
the best interest of the patient would be
served thereby , and if the public safety
would not be jeopardized thereby , the
patient may receive after -care or super-
vision in the receiving state .
( c ) In supervising , treating , or caring
for a patient on after- care pursuant to the
terms of this article , a receiving state shall
employ the same standards of visitation ,
examination , care , and treatment that it
employs for similar local patients .
Article V. Whenever a dangerous or po-
tentially dangerous patient escapes from
an institution in any party state , that state
shall promptly notify all appropriate au-
thorities within and without the jurisdic-
tion of the escape in a manner reasonably
calculated to facilitate the speedy appre-
hension of the escapee . Immediately upon
the apprehension and identification of any
such dangerous or potentially dangerous
patient , he shall be detained in the state
where found pending disposition in ac-
cordance with law .
Article VI . The duly accredited officers
of any state party to this compact , upon
the establishment of their authority and
the identity of the patient, shall be per-
mitted to transport any patient being
moved pursuant to this compact through
any and all states party to this compact ,
without interference .
Article VII . ( a) No person shall be
deemed a patient of more than one insti-
tution at any given time . Completion of
transfer of any patient to an institution
in a receiving state shall have the effect
of making the person a patient of the in-
stitution in the receiving state .
( b ) The sending state shall pay all costs
of and incidental to the transportation of
any patient pursuant to this compact , but
any two or more party states may , by

pose , arrange for a different allocation of
costs as among themselves .
( c ) No provision of this compact shall
be construed to alter or affect any internal
relationships , among the departments ,
agencies and officers of and in the govern-
ment of a party state , or between a party
state and its subdivisions, as to the pay
ment of costs , or responsibility therefor .
(d ) Nothing in this compact shall be
construed to prevent any party state or
subdivision thereof from asserting any
right against any person , agency or other

responsible pursuant to any provision of
this compact .

non -party state relating to institutionali-

DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH
211.005 Declaration of legislative policy
concerning public health laws . The Gen-
eral Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky recognizes and hereby declares
that it is an essential function , duty and
responsibility of the government of this
Commonwealth to adequately safeguard the
health of all its citizens , and to establish ,
maintain , implement , promote and conduct
appropriate facilities and services for the
purpose of protecting the public health .
This chapter is enacted with the expressed
legislative intention of effectuating the
aforesaid objectives in the manner provided
herein . ( 1954, с. 157, § 1)

211.040 [2047 ; 2613-2 ; 4618-24 ] State
Board of Health ; members ; qualifications ;
how selected . There shall be a State
Board of Health , consisting of ten mem-
bers , each of whom must be licensed in
this state to practice the profession re-
quired of him by this section as a qualifi-
cation for membership . The board shall
be a continuation of the board appointed
under Chapter 120 of the Acts of 1920 .
Nine of the members shall be appointed by
the Governor , by and with the advice of
the Senate . The Commissioner of Health
shall constitute the tenth member . One
member of the board shall be a registered
pharmacist , and shall be appointed from
a list of three names submitted by the
Kentucky Pharmaceutical Association .
One member shall be a licensed dentist
and shall be appointed from a list of three
names submitted by the Kentucky Dental
Association . One member shall be a li-
censed osteopath and shall be appointed
from a list of three names submitted by
the Kentucky Osteopathic Association .
All other members shall be licensed medi-
cal physicians and shall be appointed from
a list of three names submitted for each
position by the Kentucky State Medical
Association. If the board elects one of its
members as Commissioner of Health , the
Governor shall appoint another member
to complete the full number of the board .
No member of the board , except the Com-
missioner of Health , shall be a stockholder
or member of the faculty or board of
trustees of any medical or dental college.
(1950 , с. 111 ; 1960 , c . 7 ; effective June 16,

211.050 [ 2048 ; 4618-24 ] Terms of board
members ; vacancies . The members of the
State Board of Health shall hold office
for terms of four years and until their
successors are appointed and qualify . In
the appointment of successors and the
filling of vacancies the classification of
membership provided for in KRS 211.040
shall be observed . Any vacancy in the
board occurring before the expiration of
a term shall be filled by the Governor for
the unexpired term subject to confirma-
tion by the Senate , except that the mem-
ber appointed to fill the first vacancy ос-
curring for a term expiring on January 1 ,
1954 and a member appointed to fill a
vacancy occurring for the term expiring
on September 7, 1952 shall be appointed
to terms expiring on December 31 , 1952 .
The terms of all present and future mem-
bers of the board shall expire on Decem-
ber 31 of the fourth year of their respec-
tive terms . ( 1948 , c . 222 , § 3 ; 1950 , с . 203 )
211.060 [ 2047 ; 2050 ] Meetings ; quorum ;
president ; rules ; oaths . The State Board
of Health shall hold meetings at least
semiannually , and more frequently and
at such places and times as the board may
determine . A majority of the members
shall constitute a quorum . The board
shall elect a president from among its
members , and may adopt rules and by-
laws for its government . Any and all
members of the board may administer
oaths in matters pertaining to official
duties of the board. ( 1952 , c . 153 , § 1 ;
effective June 19, 1952)

211.080 [ 2047 ; 2052 ] Powers and duties
of Commissioner of Health . ( 1 ) The Com-
missioner of Health shall be secretary of
the State Board of Health . He shall :
(a) Keep his office at some centrally
located place in the state , designated by
the board .
(b ) Keep a record of the transactions
of the board , and have custody of all
books , papers , documents and property
belonging to the board that may be de-
posited in his office .
( c ) Communicate , so far as practicable ,
with other state boards of health and with
the local boards within this state , and keep
on file all reports received from such
boards and all correspondence of the office
appertaining to the business of the board .
(d) Prepare necessary blank forms of
returns and instructions and forward them
to the local boards of health .
(e) Collect information concerning vital
statistics , knowledge with respect to dis-
eases , and all useful information on the
subject of hygiene , and through an annual
report , and otherwise as the board may di-
rect , disseminate such information among
the people .
( f ) Supply local boards of health, on
demand, with reliable vaccine virus for
the gratuitous vaccination of the poor .
(g) So far as possible , aid in obtaining
contributions to the library of the board .
(h) Perform the duties prescribed by
law or required by the board .
(2) The commissioner may administer
oaths about matters pertaining to his
official duties .

211.140 Declaration of legislative pur-
pose . The purpose of KRS 211.130 to
211.160 is to provide for the acceptance ,
custody and use , by the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , of funds and properties that
may be given, granted , contributed ,
donated , bequeathed , devised and con-
veyed for the hospitalization , training ,
education and care of physically handi-
capped persons , including grants that
may be made for said purposes by the
government of the United States of Amer-
ica . ( 1952 , c . 165, § 2 ; effective June 19 ,
1952)
211.150 Facilities and services for physi-
cally handicapped persons who are edu-
cable . To the extent that funds are made
available , the department may establish ,
provide and maintain facilities and serv-
ices , including hospital -schools and re-
lated medical and nursing care , and voca-
tional , academic and recreational train-
ing, instruction and education , for physi-
cally handicapped persons who are , in the
judgment of the department , found to be
educable after examination and diagnosis
conducted in accordance with procedures
prescribed by the department . ( 1952 , с .
165 , § 3 ; effective June 19 , 1952 )
( 1) The commissioner may , from the
funds available , employ , by contract or
otherwise such medical , clinical , technical
and other personnel that he deems neces-
sary to effectuate the purposes of KRS
211.130 to 211.160 , and fix and pay their
compensation and necessary traveling ex-
penses .
(2) The commissioner may , from the
funds available , procure , by purchase or
lease or otherwise , such property , equip-
ment , services , facilities and supplies that
he deems necessary to effectuate the pur-
poses of KRS 211.130 to 211.160 .
(3) The commissioner is authorized to
accept , on behalf of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , all gifts , donations , contribu-
tions , grants , devises , bequests and con-
veyances of real and personal property
for establishing , providing and maintain-
ing the services and facilities described
in KRS 211.150 , subject only to the con-
dition that same shall be devoted to and
used for said purposes . All funds received
by the commissioner shall be deposited in
the State Treasury and credited to a trust
and agency fund account and expended
only for purposes authorized by KRS
211.130 to 211.160.
(4) The commissioner may make and
issue all necessary rules and regulations
to carry out KRS 211.130 to 211.160 ; pro-
vided , however, that no educable person
shall be eligible for any benefits hereunder
unless he is , and has been continuously
for at least twelve months immediately
preceding the date of his application
therefor , an actual resident of this state .
(5) The commissioner may delegate to
any division of the department , or to any
director thereof , any and all of his author-
ity and duties hereunder .
( 6 ) Upon request of the commissioner ,
approved in writing by the Governor , any
department , agency or commission of the
Commonwealth shall furnish without cost
to the department such services , facilities
and assistance as are available and , in
the judgment of the commissioner and the
Governor, required, to effectuate the pur-
poses of KRS 211.130 to 211.160 or its
administration which is hereby vested in
the department . ( 1952 , c. 165 , § 4 ; effec-
tive June 19 , 1952 )

211.180 Functions of department in the
regulation of certain public health mat-
ters. The department shall enforce the
rules and regulations adopted by the board
for the regulation and control of the mat-
ters set out below and shall formulate , pro-
mote , establish and execute policies , plans
and programs relating to all matters of
public health , including but not limited to
the following matters :
(1) Detection , prevention , and control
of communicable diseases , chronic and de-
generative diseases , dental diseases and
abnormalities , occupational diseases and
health hazards peculiar to industry , home
accidents and health hazards , animal dis-
eases which are transmissible to man , and
such other diseases and health hazards as
may be controlled ;
( 2 ) The control of insects , rodents and
other vectors of disease ; the proper dis-
posal of garbage , waste and other refuse ;
the safe installation and operation of
plumbing and plumbing fixtures ; the safe
disposal of human and other excreta ; the
safe handling of food and food products ;
the safety of cosmetics ; the control of nar-
cotics , barbiturates and other drugs as pro-
vided by law ; the purification of water for
public and semipublic use ; the proper con-
struction and operation of public water
purification plants and distribution sys-
tems , swimming pools and sewage treat-
ment plants , including the issuance of per-
mits to and the supervision of operators
thereof ; the sanitation of schools , indus-
trial establishments and other public and
semipublic buildings ; the sanitation of
state and county fairs and other similar
modification or extension of food - handling
establishment , water purification and dis-
tribution systems, swimming pools , sewage
treatment plants and such other establish-
ments , systems , plants and places as are
designated by the board ; the licensure of
hospitals ; and the control of such other
factors as may be necessary to insure a safe
and sanitary environment ;
( 3) Protection and improvement of the
health of expectant mothers , infants , pre-
school and school age children ;
(4) The practice of midwifery , includ-
ing the issuance of permits to and super-
vision of women who practice midwifery ;
( 5 ) Protection and improvement of the
health of the people through better nutri-
tion. (1954 , c. 157, § 12 ; effective June 17,
1954 )
211.182 Treatment of cancer restricted
to physicians . ( 1 ) For the purposes of
this section , " cancer " means all malignant
neoplasms regardless of the tissue of
origin , including malignant lymphoma and
leukemia .
(2) No person , other than a licensed
medical or osteopathic physician , or den-
tist, shall in any manner hold himself out
to any other person as being able to pre-
scribe treatment for, or cure the disease
of cancer .
( 3 ) No person , other than a licensed
medical or osteopathic physician , or den-
tist , shall in any manner undertake to
treat , or prescribe for the treatment of
the disease of cancer .
(4) No person shall sell or offer to sell ,
or give away or offer to give away , except
upon the prescription of a licensed medi-
cal or osteopathic physician , or dentist ,
any drug , medicine , compound , nostrum
or device which is represented by the
manufacturer or seller thereof to have
curative powers when used in the treat-
ment of the disease of cancer .
(5) Nothing contained herein shall be
construed to impose any limitation upon
the free exercise of religion in this state .
(1960 , c . 47 , § § 1, 2, 3 , 4 , 6 ; effective June
16 , 1960)

DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH ; SCHOLARSHIPS § 211.210
211.184 Enforcement of KRS 211.182
by department and board . ( 1 ) It shall
be the duty of the department to enforce
the provisions of KRS 211.182 , and for
that purpose the investigators , inspectors ,
representatives and agents of the board
and the department shall have the full
power and authority of peace officers in
this state , and shall have the power and
authority to administer oaths , to enter
upon premises at all times for the purpose
of making inspections , to seize evidence ,
to interrogate all persons , and to require
the production of books , papers , docu-
ments or other evidence .
(2) The board may institute , in its own
name , proceedings to enjoin and restrain
violations of KRS 211.182 , regardless of
whether the defendant has been convicted
of violation of the penal provisions
thereof , and shall not be required to pay
any costs or filing fees or furnish any
bond in connection therewith . Violation
of injunctions and restraining orders shall
be punished as a contempt without the
intervention of a jury , and the court's dis-
cretion concerning the degree of punish-
ment shall not be subject to the pro-
visions of KRS 432.260 . ( 1960 , c. 47, § 5 ;
effective June 16, 1960 )
Peace officer , defined , KRS 61.310 , 446.010
211.190 Public health services to be pro-
vided by the department . The department
shall provide public health services includ-
ing the following :
(1 ) Administrative , consultative , tech-
nical , professional and other services
needed to assist local health departments in
the effective maintenance and operation of
their departments ;
(2 ) Administrative , investigative and
clerical services required by the board and
may upon request provide such services to
any other agency of this Commonwealth
authorized to control the practice of any
other healing art ;
(3 ) Administration of grants , gifts or
contributions from the federal government ,
or from other sources , for the purpose of
carrying out the provisions of Public Law
No. 725 ( 79th Congress , 2nd Session , Chap-
ter 958 ) or any other Acts for the same or
similar purposes ;
(4) Central registrations of births , deaths
and other vital records and the furnishing
of copies thereof to the general public in
the manner prescribed by law ;
( 5 ) Statistical services , including the
compilation , analysis and maintenance of
statistics on matters related to public
health, and may provide such services to
organizations and persons interested in
public health ;
( 6 ) Education of the public concerning
all matters relating to health , including
the publication and dissemination of health
information , and the stimulation of citizen
support for the promotion and mainte-
nance of high standards of public health
throughout the Commonwealth ;
( 7 ) Survey and study of the needs of
medical and hospital facilities in the inter-
est of the health of the general public ;
( 8 ) Establishment , maintenance and op-
eration of public health laboratories and
such branches thereof as may be needed ;
( 9 ) Establishment , maintenance and op-
eration of training facilities and schools
for employes of the department and of
local health departments ;
( 10 ) Tabulating , duplicating and such
other ancillary services as are necessary to
the operation of the department , including
the keeping of adequate financial , person-
nel and other records . (1954 , c . 157 , § 13 ;
effective June 17, 1954)
211.200 Assignment of department per-
sonnel to a county in case of public health
emergency or when county lacks adequate
local health department . Whenever , in
the opinion of the commissioner , a public
health emergency exists in any county , or
whenever any county fails to establish ,
maintain and operate a local health depart-
ment therein meeting the standards pre-
scribed by the department , the department
may assign to said county such of its own
personnel as may be designated by the com-
missioner . Such personnel so assigned shall
have the full power and authority of local
health department employes in addition to
their power and authority as representa-
tives of the department . Whenever such
assignment results by reason of the lack of
a local health department or of a local
health department meeting the standards
prescribed by the department , any funds
appropriated or allocated to the local health
department by either the Commonwealth
or the Federal government may be used to
reimburse the department . ( 1954, c. 157 ,
§ 14 ; effective June 17 , 1954)
211.210 Abatement of nuisances . The
department may examine into all nuisances ,
sources of filth and causes of sickness
19

§ 211.220 DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH ; SCHOLARSHIPS
that may, in its opinion be injurious
to the health of the inhabitants in any
county in this state , or in any vessel
within any harbor or port in any county in
this state . Whenever any such nuisance ,
source of filth or cause of sickness is found
to exist on any private property , or in any
vessel within any port or harbor in any
county in this state, or upon any water-
course in this state , the department may
order , in writing , the owner or occupant
thereof , at his own expense, to remove the
same within twenty-four hours , or within
such reasonable time thereafter , as the de-
partment may order . ( 1954 , c . 157, § 15 ;
effective June 17, 1954)
211.220 Powers of department personnel
in the conduct of investigations ; sub-
poenas , process . For the purposes of en-
forcing the public health laws of the Com-
monwealth , investigators , inspectors , offi-
cers , representatives and agents of the de-
partment may enter upon any premises
when necessary for the purpose of making
inspections and investigations , and may
view evidence and interrogate persons , to
the extent required in the performance of
their duties and responsibilities . The board
or the commissioner may issue subpoenas ,
subpoenas duces tecum and all necessary
process in proceedings brought before or
initiated by the department or board , and
such process shall extend to all parts of the
Commonwealth . Service of process may be
made by registered mail or in the manner
prescribed by the Rules of Civil Procedure .
Nothing in this section shall be construed
to authorize the department or board to
regulate the practice of any healing art
where the licensure regulation and control
of same has been conferred by statute upon
some other agency of the state . (1954, с .
157 , § 16 ; effective June 17, 1954)
211.230 Enforcement of obedience to
orders and process ; coercion of witness
to testify . In case of a failure on the part
of any person , firm or corporation to com-
ply with any lawful order of the board , or
with process , or in case of the refusal of
any witness to testify concerning any mat-
ter on which he may be lawfully interro-
gated , the circuit court , or a judge thereof ,
having jurisdiction may , on application of
the board or the commissioner , compel
obedience by proceedings as in contempt
cases . ( 1954 , c . 157 , § 17 ; effective June 17 ,
1954)
211.240 Duties of Attorney General ,
county, city and Commonwealth's attor-
neys . County , city and Commonwealth's
attorneys , and the Attorney General , shall ,
within their respective jurisdictions , pros-
ecute all violations of the penal provisions
of the public health laws of this Common-
wealth . ( 1954 , c . 157, § 18 ; effective June
17, 1954 )

MEDICAL SCHOLARSHIPS
211.290 Rural Kentucky Medical Schol-
arship Fund . The Kentucky State Depart-
ment of Health shall aid , assist and pro-
mote the Rural Kentucky Medical Scholar-
ship Fund with such sums as may , from
time to time , be appropriated by the Gen-
eral Assembly for this purpose . ( 1954 ,
c. 181 , § 1 ; effective June 17 , 1954)
211.300 Declaration of legislative policy
and definition for KRS 211.300 to 211.330 .
(1) In enacting legislation relating to loans
and scholarships for the study of medicine ,
it is the intention of the General Assembly
to alleviate the shortage of physicians en-
gaged in public health work.
(2) As used in KRS 211.300 to 211.330 ,
unless the context requires otherwise ,
" Board " means the State Board of Health .
(1954 , с . 136 , §§ 1 , 2 ; effective June 17 ,
1954)
211.305 Applications for medical educa-
tion loans and scholarships . The board
shall receive and consider all applications
for loans or scholarships made by students
who are bona fide residents of this Com-
monwealth who desire to become physicians
and who are acceptable for enrollment in a
medical school approved by the board . The
board shall make a careful and full in-
vestigation of the ability , character and
qualifications of each applicant to deter-
mine his fitness to become the recipient of
such loan or scholarship . The board may
examine each applicant . It may prescribe
such rules and regulations as it deems
necessary and proper to carry out the pur-
pose and intent of KRS 211.300 to 211.330 .
The board may grant to each applicant
deemed by the board to be qualified to re-
ceive the same , a loan or scholarship as
herein provided for the purpose of acquir-
ing a medical education upon terms and
conditions imposed by the board and pro-
vided for in KRS 211.300 to 211.330 .
(1954 , c . 136, § 3 ; effective June 17, 1954)
211.310 Maximum amount and condi-
tions of loan or scholarship . A loan or
scholarship shall not exceed one thousand
dollars per annum , to defray tuition and
other expenses in a medical college or school
approved by the board . The grant shall be
based upon the condition that on or before
six months from the date the applicant
completes one year of internship he engage
in public health work at some place within
the Commonwealth approved by the board
at the rate of one year for each one thous-
and dollars , or proportional time for lesser
amounts , which shall constitute repayment
of the loan or scholarship . Where the board
is convinced that there is justifiable cause
which the applicant could not have fore-
seen , the board may allow him to repay the
loan or scholarship by paying in cash the
full amount of the loan or scholarship plus
six percent interest . (1954 , c . 136, § 4 ;
effective June 17, 1954)
211.315 Execution of contract for loan
or scholarship . Before being granted a
loan or scholarship each applicant shall
enter into a contract with the board which
shall be deemed a contract with the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , agreeing to the
terms and conditions upon which the loan
or scholarship shall be granted to him .
The contract shall include such terms and
provisions as will carry out the purpose
and intent of KRS 211.300 to 211.330 and
shall be signed by the president of the
board , countersigned by the secretary , and
signed by the applicant . (1954 , c . 136 , § 5 ;
effective June 17, 1954)
211.320 Cancellation of contract ; repay-
ment of loan or scholarship . The board
may cancel any contract between it and
any applicant for a loan or scholarship
upon any cause deemed sufficient by the
board in which event the student shall re-
pay with six percent interest the amount
of the loan or scholarship . The board may
sue in its own name any person who fails
to fulfill his obligations under a contract
for a loan or scholarship . Refusal to com-
ply with a decision of the board relating
to the method of repayment of a loan or
scholarship may constitute grounds for
revocation of license as provided for in
KRS 311.565 . ( 1954 , c . 136 , § 6 ; effective
June 17 , 1954 )
211.325 Contracts with medical schools .
The board may make inquiry of medical
colleges and schools , and make arrange-
ments and enter into contracts with such
21

§ 211.330 DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH ; SCHOLARSHIPS
institutions for the admission of students
granted loans or scholarships by the board .
The board may cancel any contract made
with any college or school . ( 1954 , c. 136,
§ 7 ; effective June 17, 1954)
211.330 Trust or agency fund. Funds
appropriated for the purposes of the medi-
cal scholarship program and all sums re-
ceived in repayment of loans and scholar-
ships shall be placed in the State Treasury
to the credit of a trust or agency fund for
carrying out this program . Loan and
scholarship payments shall be made out of
this fund on a requisition to the Depart-
ment of Finance signed by the secretary of
the board . ( 1954, c. 136, § 8 ; effective June
17 , 1954 )
DENTAL SCHOLARSHIPS
211.405 Purpose of Rural Dental Schol-
arship Fund . In enacting legislation re-
lating to a Rural Kentucky Dental Schol-
arship Fund , the General Assembly has as
its purpose the allevation of the shortage
of dentists in certain rural areas of the
Commonwealth . ( 1958 , c . 35 , § 1 ; effective
June 19 , 1958 )
211.410 Definition for KRS 211.405 to
211.460 . As used in KRS 211.405 to
211.460 , unless the context requires other-
wise , "Board " means the Kentucky State
Board of Dental Examiners . ( 1958 , c . 35 ,
§ 2 ; effective June 19 , 1958 )
211.420 Rural Kentucky Dental Schol-
arship Fund ; granting scholarships . (1 )
There is hereby established within the
Department of Health , a Rural Kentucky
Dental Scholarship Fund .
(2) Commencing July 1, 1958, there
shall be available each fiscal year to ap-
plicants selected by the board with the
approval of the Dental Division of the
State Department of Health , ten scholar-
ships for the study of dentistry leading to
the attainment of the degree of Doctor of
Dental Surgery , or some equivalent de-
gree . If in any years less than ten scholar-
ships are awarded , the number available
but not granted shall be carried over and
added to those available in succeeding
years .
( 3 ) A scholarship shall not exceed one
thousand five hundred dollars per annum .
No recipient of a scholarship shall receive
more than six thousand dollars for his en-
tire course of dental study .
(4) In granting scholarships the board
shall make a careful and full investigation
of the ability , character and qualifications
of each applicant, and may personally ex-
amine each applicant . The board shall ,
whenever possible , grant financial assist-
ance to the applicants with the greatest
financial need , provided such persons are
found to possess such qualities as give rea-
sonable assurance of their successfully
completing the course of study made pos-
sible by the scholarship . (1958 , c . 35 , § 3 ;
effective June 19 , 1958 )
211.430 Eligibility of applicants ; con-
tract to practice . ( 1) To be eligible for a
scholarship made available under KRS
211.405 to 211.460 , an applicant must :
(a) Have been a resident of this Com-
monwealth for not less than five years
immediately preceding the date of appli-
cation ;
(b ) Be acceptable for enrollment in a
dental school accredited by the Council on
Dental Education of the American Dental
Association , and approved by the board ;
and
(c) Furnish satisfactory evidence to the
board that he does not have sufficient fi-
nancial resources to enable him to study
dentistry without assistance .
( 2 ) Before a scholarship is granted , the
applicant shall contract in writing with
the board , that he will, within six months
from the date he completes his term of
study , engage in the practice of dentistry
in a locality or localities within this Com-
monwealth to be designated by the Den-
tal Division of the State Department of
Health , at the rate of one year for each
one thousand five hundred dollars re-
ceived , or proportional time for lesser
amounts . ( 1958 , c . 35 , § 4 ; effective June
19, 1958 )
211.440 Effect of breach of contract. (1)
If the recipient of a scholarship fails , with-
out justifiable cause to practice dentistry
in the locality designated , the entire
amount of scholarship benefits received
under the provisions of KRS 211.405 to
211.460 plus six percent interest thereon ,
shall become due and payable . However ,
where the board determines there is justi-
fiable cause for the failure to practice , it
may relieve the recipient of the obligation
to practice, and shall provide for repay
22

DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH ; SCHOLARSHIPS § 211.540
ment of the amount received plus six per-
cent interest on any terms it deems prop-
er.
(2) Upon recommendation of the board ,
the Attorney General shall institute pro-
ceedings for the purpose of recovering any
amount due the Commonwealth under the
provisions of this section .
(3 ) Failure of a recipient of a scholar-
ship to perform his obligations with re-
spect to his contract to practice in a desig-
nated locality shall constitute grounds for
revocation of his license to practice den-
tistry within the Commonwealth . ( 1958 ,
c . 35 , § 5 ; effective June 19 , 1958 )
211.450 Administrative regulations . The
board may promulgate reasonable rules
and regulations for the purpose of carry-
ing out the provisions of KRS 211.405 to
211.460 . ( 1958 , c . 35 , § 6 ; effective June
19 , 1958 )
211.460 Disposition of funds . Funds ap-
propriated for the purpose of the Rural
Kentucky Dental Scholarship Fund , and
sums received as a result of violations of
service agreements shall be placed in the
State Treasury to the credit of a trust or
agency fund for the purpose of carrying
out the provisions of KRS 211.405 to
211.460 . Provided , however , that any sums
in excess of fifty thousand dollars at the
end of each biennium shall revert to the
General Fund . (1958 , c . 35 , §7 ; effective
June 19 , 1958 )
KENTUCKY COMMISSION ON AGING
211.510 Definition for KRS 211.510 to
211.560 . As used in KRS 211.510 to
211.560 , unless the context otherwise re-
quires , " commission " means the Kentucky
Commission on Aging . ( 1962 , с. 106, Art .
XIII, § 1)
Note : KRS 211.510 takes effect July 1, 1962 .
211.520 Kentucky Commission onAging.
( 1 ) There is hereby created a Kentucky
Commission on Aging , within the Health
and Welfare Agency . The commission
shall consist of sixteen members and
shall include the Commissioner of the
Department of Health , the Commissioner
of the Department of Mental Health , the
Commissioner of the Department of Eco-
nomic Security , the Superintendent of
Public Instruction and twelve members to
be appointed by the Governor , not more
than three of whom may be employes of
the Commonwealth . A chairman of the
commission shall be elected at the first
meeting and annually thereafter .
(2) Of the citizen members first ap-
pointed four shall serve for terms of one
year , four for terms of two years and four
for terms of three years . At the expira-
tion of these terms appointments shall be
for terms of three years . All citizen mem-
bers shall be eligible for reappointment
and shall serve until their successors have
been appointed and qualified . Vacancies
occurring prior to the expiration of a
term shall be filled by the Governor for
the remainder of the unexpired term .
(3) The commission shall meet at least
six times annually on call of the chairman
or on the written request of any eight
members . A majority of the members of
the commission shall constitute a quorum
for the transaction of business . If at any
time the Commissioner of the Department
of Health , the Commissioner of the Do-
partment of Mental Health , the Commis-
sioner of the Department of Economic
Security or the Superintendent of Public
Instruction is unable to attend a commis-
sion meeting , he shall designate another
person who has authority to act in his
behalf at the meeting and who shall be
considered a member of the commission
for the purpose of obtaining a quorum and
transacting business . ( 1962 , c . 106 , Art .
XIII , § 2 )
Note : KRS 211.520 takes effect July 1, 1962 .
Health and Welfare Agency , KRS 12.022
211.530 Staff assistance and administra-
tion . The administrator of the Health
and Welfare Agency shall provide the
commission with staff assistance , and shall
administer the program of the commission .
(1962 , с . 106 , Art . XIII , § 3 )
Note : KRS 211.530 takes effect July 1, 1962 .
211.540 Expenses of citizen members ;
advisory committees . (1) The citizen
members of the commission shall be re-
imbursed for necessary travel and other
expenses incurred in the performance of
their duties .
(2) The commission may create such
advisory committees as it deems necessary
and shall designate a commission member
as chairman of each advisory committee .
(1962 , с . 106 , Art . XIII , § 4)
Note : KRS 211.540 takes effect July 1, 1962 .

§ 211.550 DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH ; SCHOLARSHIPS
211.550 Duties of commission . The duties
of the commission shall be to :
(1) Promote and aid in the establish-
ment of local programs and services for
the aging ;
(2) Conduct programs to educate the
public as to problems of the aging ;
( 3 ) Review existing state programs of
the aging and to make recommendations
to the Governor , to the appropriate de-
partments and agencies of the state , and
to the legislature for improvements in and
additions to such programs ;
(4) Assist and encourage governmental
and private agencies to coordinate their
efforts on behalf of the aging ;
(5) Conduct and encourage other or-
ganizations to conduct studies concerning
the aging ;
(6) Establish , in selected areas and com-
munities of the state , programs of services
for the aging to demonstrate the value
of such programs , and to encourage local
agencies to continue the programs and
to create new services where needed . Em-
phasis shall be given to services designed
to foster continued participation of older
people in family and community life and
to lessen the need for institutional care ;
( 7 ) Cooperate with the federal govern-
ment and with the governments of other
states in programs relating to the aging .
(1962 , с . 106 , Art . XIII , § 5)
211.620 Kentucky Commission on Chil-
dren and Youth . (1) There is hereby
created within the Health and Welfare
Agency , a Kentucky Commission on Chil-
dren and Youth . The commission shall
consist of the commissioners of the De-
partments of Child Welfare , Economic Se-
curity , Health , and Mental Health , the
Superintendent of Public Instruction , the
chief executive officer of the Commission
for Handicapped Children , and eighteen
members to be appointed by the Governor.
A chairman of the commission shall be
elected at the first meeting and annually
thereafter .
(2) Of the eighteen members first ap-
pointed by the Governor , four shall serve
for terms of one year , four for terms of
two years , five for terms of three years ,
and five for terms of four years . At the
expiration of these terms , appointments
shall be for terms of four years . All ap-
pointed members shall be eligible for re-
appointment and shall serve until their
successors have been appointed and quali-
fied . Vacancies occurring prior to the
expiration of a term shall be filled by the
Governor for the remainder of the un-
expired term .
(3) The commission shall meet at least
two times annually on call of the chair-
man or on the written request of any
thirteen members . A majority of the
members of the commission shall con-
stitute a quorum for the transaction of
business . ( 1962 , c. 106, Art . XIV , § 2)
Health and Welfare Agency , KRS 12.022
211.630 Staff assistance ; advisory com-
mittees ; expenses of members . ( 1) The
administrator of the Health and Welfare
Agency shall furnish the commission with
necessary staff assistance , through either
an administrative services unit of the
agency or the facilities of a department
within the agency .
( 2 ) The commission may appoint such
advisory committees as are necessary to
advise it on specific matters relating to
children and youth .
(3 ) Members of the commission shall
serve without compensation but shall be
reimbursed for necessary and actual ex-
penses incurred in attending commission
meetings . ( 1962 , c . 106 , Art . XIV, § 3 )
Note : KRS 211.550 takes effect July 1, 1962 .
211.560 Acceptance and disposition of
gifts and grants . The commission shall
be empowered to accept gifts and grants
at its discretion . Such moneys shall go
into a trust and agency fund to be dis-
bursed by the commission in furtherance
of the purposes of KRS 211.510 to 211.560 .
(1962 , с . 106 , Art . XIII , § 6 )
Note : KRS 211.560 takes effect July 1, 1962 .

211.610 Definition for KRS 211.610 to
211.650 . As used in KRS 211.610 to

quires , " commission ” means the Kentucky
Commission on Children and Youth .
(1962 , с . 106, Art . XIV , § 1)
211.640 Duties of commission . The duties
of the commission shall be to :
24

DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH ; SCHOLARSHIPS § 211.990
(1) Promote and develop effective pro-
grams of education , health , recreation ,
welfare , public safety and correctional
services for children and youth ;
( 2) Conduct continuing programs of
public information to educate the public
as to problems of children and youth ;
( 3 ) Assist and encourage governmental
and private agencies to coordinate their
efforts on behalf of children and youth ;
(4) Cooperate with the Federal Govern-
ment and with the governments of other
states and cities in programs relating to
children and youth ;
(5) Conduct programs of research as to
the needs of children and youth in order
to facilitate more comprehensive and bet-
ter related social planning and action ;
(6) Cooperate with the Department of
Child Welfare , the Department of Eco-
nomic Security , the Department of Health ,
the Department of Mental Health and the
Commission for Handicapped Children in
carrying out the duties prescribed by this
section by making the fullest possible use
of the experience and resources of those
departments ;
(7) Furnish the Governor a biennial re-
port together with recommendations .
(1962 , с . 106, Art . XIV , § 4a )
211.650 Acceptance and disposition of
gifts and grants . The commission shall
be empowered to accept gifts and grants
at its discretion . Such funds shall go into
a trust and agency fund to be disbursed
in furtherance of the purposes of KRS
211.610 to 211.650 . ( 1962 , c. 106 , Art . XIV ,
§ 4b )
Handling private funds and contributions ,
KRS 41.290
PENALTIES
211.990 Penalties . (1) Any owner or
occupant who fails to comply with an order
made under the provisions of KRS 211.210
shall be fined not less than ten dollars nor
more than one hundred dollars , and each
day's continuance of the nuisance , source
of filth or cause of sickness , after the owner
or occupant has been notified to remove it,
shall be a separate offense .
( 2) Except as otherwise provided by
law, anyone who fails to comply with the
provisions of the rules and regulations
adopted pursuant to this chapter or who
fails to comply with an order of the depart-
ment issued pursuant thereto shall be fined
not less than ten dollars nor more than one
hundred doliars . Each day of such vio-
lation or noncompliance shall constitute a
separate offense .
( 3) Any person who violates any pro-
vision of KRS 211.182 shall, upon first
offense , be fined not less than $ 1,000 nor
more than $ 5,000 , or confined in the
county jail for not less than thirty days
nor more than twelve months , or both .
Each subsequent violation of any pro-
vision of KRS 211.182 shall constitute a
felony and be punishable by a fine of not
less than $ 3,000 nor more than $ 10,000 , or
by imprisonment in the penitentiary for
not less than one nor more than three
years , or both . ( 1954 , c . 157 , § 23 ; subsec-
tion ( 3 ) added , 1960 , с . 47, § 7)

GENERAL PROVISIONS
212.010 [ 2054a -21 ] City health boards ;
establishment ; membership . ( 1 ) Except
as provided in subsection ( 2 ) of this sec-
tion , the legislative body of each city of
ten thousand inhabitants or more shall ap-
point six persons who , with the mayor of
the city , shall constitute a board of health
for the city . None of the six appointed
members shall be members of the city leg-
islative body, and at least three of them
must be competent physicians . When the
board is first appointed , two persons shall
be appointed for a term of one year , two
for a term of two years , and two for a
term of three years . Their successors
shall be appointed for terms of three
years . The board of health shall organize
within ten days after its appointment .
(2 ) When a county , except one having
a city of the first or second class , shall by
resolution of its fiscal court or by a vote
of its citizens create , establish and main-
tain a county or district department of
health , such department of health shall
include cities having a population of ten
thousand and over , and no such city may
organize a city board of health , but the
health officer of the county or district de-
partment of health shall serve for the en-
tire county or district .
Note : By virtue of KRS 212.350 to 212.630 ,
enacted in 1942 , this section no longer applies
to cities of the first class .
212.020 [ 2055 ] County health boards ;
appointment ; membership ; compensation .
The State Board of Health shall appoint
three intelligent and discreet licensed and
practicing physicians and one intelligent
and discreet licensed and practicing den-
tist residing in each county who , together
with the county judge and one person
appointed by the fiscal court in each
county, shall constitute a local board of
health for the respective counties in which
they reside . In the event a county does
not have three qualified licensed and
practicing physicians or one qualified
licensed and practicing dentist residing
therein , the State Board of Health may
appoint a resident lay person in lieu
thereof for each such vacancy . The mem-
bers of the local board shall hold office
for a term of two years from the date of
their appointment , and until their suc-
cessors are appointed . The members of
the board shall receive no compensation
for their services . ( 1958 , c . 96 , § 1 ; 1960 ,
c . 6 ; effective June 16, 1960 )
Note : By virtue of KRS 212.350 to 212.630 ,
enacted in 1942 , this section no longer applies
to counties containing a city of the first class .
212.030 [ 2054a -2 ] District health de-
partment ; establishment . A county or dis-
trict health department for the preven-
tion and control of epidemics and com-
municable sickness , as may be determined
by the State Board of Health , may be cre-
ated , established and maintained in and
by counties or districts in the state in the
manner provided in KRS 212.040 to
212.120 . A district for the creation , estab-
lishment and maintenance of a county or
district health department may consist of
one county, or two or more counties con-
tiguous to each other .
Note : By virtue of KRS 212.350 to 212.630 ,
enacted in 1942 , this section no longer applies
to counties containing a city of the first class .
212.040 [ 2054a - 2 ] Establishment for
one county by fiscal court ; appropriation .
The fiscal court of any county may by
resolution at a regular session declare that
the county shall be a district for the crea-
tion , establishment and maintenance of a
county health department . Upon the reso-
lution being passed , the fiscal court shall
at once , out of the funds of the county,
appropriate a sufficient amount for the
creation, establishment and maintenance
of the county health department . If there
are not sufficient funds on hand for such
appropriation at the time the resolution is
adopted , the fiscal court shall , at the next
27

§ 212.050 LOCAL HEALTH UNITS AND OFFICERS
succeeding county levy , make a levy suffi-
cient to produce the necessary amount of
tax for the creation , establishment and
maintenance of the county health depart-
ment. The fiscal court shall annually
thereafter make a levy sufficient to pay
the annual expense of maintenance of the
county health department . After the res-
olution creating the department is en-
tered , any twenty legal voters may , within
thirty days , file with the county judge a
petition signed by them requesting that
the establishment of the county health de-
partment be done by the vote of the people
of the county as provided in KRS 212.080 ,
in which case it shall be so done .
212.050 [ 2054a -3 ; 2054a -4 ] Establish-
ment for several counties by fiscal courts ;
appropriation ; apportionment of expense .
(1) The fiscal courts of two or more con-
tiguous counties may by resolution of each
court , duly passed by a majority vote of
the members present , unite the counties
into a district for the purpose of creating ,
establishing and maintaining a district
health department . The resolution shall
be in full force and effect for two years
awaiting the action of the fiscal court of
one or more contiguous counties to unite
in the formation of a district health de-
partment . Each court , at the time of pass-
ing the resolution , shall provide an ap-
propriation for its proportion of the cost
of the creation , establishment and mainte-
nance of the department , to be paid by the
county . If the funds are not on hand for
that purpose , the fiscal court shall , at its
next regular meeting at which a county
levy is made , levy a tax for that purpose ,
and shall thereafter make an annual levy
sufficient to pay its proportion of the cost
for the succeeding years .
( 2) Where two or more counties unite
to form a district , the cost of creating and
establishing the district department of
health and the cost of the necessary equip-
ment shall be paid by the counties com-
prising the district in proportion to the
taxable property of each county as shown
by their respective county assessments .
The annual expense of maintenance of the
district health department shall be borne
by each county reasonably and equitably
in proportion to the amount of the taxable
property in each county, in amounts de-
termined by the rules and regulations of
the State Board of Health .
212.060 [ 2054a -9 ] When fiscal court
may not establish . When a county by a
vote of its citizens , as provided in
KRS 212.070 to 212.110 , shall vote against
creating , establishing and maintaining a
county or district department of health ,
the department of health may not be cre-
ated , established or maintained by resolu-
tion of its fiscal court until after the ex-
piration of the terms of office of the mem-
bers of the fiscal court who were members
of the court when the election was held .
212.070 [2054a - 5 ] Establishment by
election. If the fiscal court of any county
fails to establish a county or district de-
partment of health , as authorized in
KRS 212.040 and 212.050 , the citizens of
the county, or of two or more contiguous
counties , may have such county or such
contiguous counties established as
county or district department of health in
accordance with the provisions of KRS
212.080 or 212.090 .
a
212.080 [ 2054a - 5 ; 2054a - 6 ] Election in
one county. A number of legal voters
equal to ten percent of the total number
of votes cast at the last general election of
any county may file their petition with
the county judge asking that the proposi-
tion of creating , establishing and main-
taining a county health department be
submitted to the voters of the county at
the next general election held in the
county that does not occur within less
than thirty days after filing the petition .
Each voter signing the petition shall state
his full name and address . At the time of
filing the petition the petitioners shall de-
posit with the county judge a sufficient
sum of money to pay the cost of publica-
tion , as required by this section . Upon
the filing of the petition with the county
judge , he shall enter an order directing
the publication in full of the petition in
the newspaper having the largest circula-
tion in the county at least once a week for
four consecutive weeks next preceding the
general election , and shall further enter
an order directing the clerk of the county
court to have placed upon the ballot at
such election the question , "Are you in
favor of establishing a county department
of health ? " with proper squares in which
the voter may indicate " Yes " or " No. "
If the majority of those voting on the
proposition vote " Yes , " a department of
28

health for the county shall be created , es-
tablished and maintained. The vote shall
be canvassed and returned by the board of
election commissioners for the general
election . If the election is contested , the
members of the fiscal court of the county
shall be made defendants .

212.090 [ 2054a -6 ] Election in several
counties . When the citizens of two or
more contiguous counties desire to have
created , established and maintained a dis-
trict department of health, not less than
twenty qualified legal voters of each
county shall file a petition in the county
of their residence , asking that a district
department of health be created , estab-
lished and maintained , and naming the
several counties to be united in creating ,
establishing and maintaining the district
department of health . The petitions shall
be filed in each county of the proposed
district , and shall be accompanied by the
deposit required by KRS 212.080 , and the
method of proceeding in each shall be the
same as provided in KRS 212.080 , except
that the same proceeding shall be taken
in each county for the general election to
be held at the same time in each county ,
and the question placed upon the ballot
shall read : " Are you in favor of estab-
lishing a district department of health ? "
If the result of the election in any one of
the counties in the proposed district is
against the proposition , the district can-
not be established .

§ 212.130 LOCAL HEALTH UNITS AND OFFICERS
vided, however , that no allotment to any
such county or district health department
shall be less than two thousand five hun-
dred dollars .
( 3 ) In determining the allotments re-
ferred to in subsection (2) of this section ,
the State Board of Health shall endeavor
to provide for a distribution of the funds
in a manner that is reasonably calculated
to equalize , so far as practicable , local
health services to the people of all coun-
ties served by the county and district
health departments . The State Board of
Health may take into consideration varia-
tions existing between counties and dis-
tricts by reasons of difference in popula-
tion , resources , industrialization , tax as-
sessments and tax rates , and other local
factors and conditions ; the legislative in-
tent being hereby declared to be that
counties shall provide, from local sources
of revenue that are available or that may
be made available to them , financial sup-
port of county and district health depart-
ments to the extent of their representative
abilities .
(4) The State Board of Health may , in
its discretion , alter or modify allotments
from time to time and shall cancel any
allotment whenever it finds that there is
no further need or necessity for a partic-
ular county or district health department
for whose benefit the allotment was made ,
or whenever a particular county or dis-
trict health department for whose benefit
an allotment was made is not maintained ,
operated and conducted in accordance
with the standards prescribed by the State
Board of Health . Nothing in this section
shall be construed as requiring the State
Board of Health to allot all funds avail-
able for local health purposes , or as pro-
hibiting the board from alloting such por-
tion thereof, as the board may determine ,
to a reserve account which may be sub-
allotted by the board in such manner that
it considers proper in the event of emerg-
encies , disaster, or unforeseen events ,
without regard to the provisions of sub-
section ( 3 ) of this section .
( 5 ) Notwithstanding the provisions of
KRS 45.230 and any other provisions of
the Kentucky Revised Statutes , any un-
expended or unencumbered balance of any
appropriations made available for allot-
ment and expenditure , as provided above ,
for the first fiscal year of each biennium ,
remaining at the end of such fiscal year ,
shall be carried forward and be available
for expenditure at any time during the
ensuing fiscal year within the biennium
and no portion thereof shall lapse to the
General Fund . ( 1952 , c . 153 , § 3 ; 1958 , с .
126 , § 27 )
212.130 [ 2054a - 10 ] County and district
health departments , how organized . Im-
mediately after receiving notice of the
creation of a county or district depart-
ment of health , the State Board of Health
shall notify the secretary of the county
board or boards of health to call a meet-
ing of the county board or boards of
health for the purpose of organizing the
county or district department of health .
212.140 [ 2054a - 10 ; 2054a -15 ] County
and district health departments , how gov-
erned . ( 1 ) A county department of health
shall be governed by the members of the
county board of health .
(2 ) A district department of health
shall be governed by the county boards of
health of the counties that have created ,
established and maintained the district
department of health . A majority of all
the members of the county boards of
health in the district department of health
shall constitute a quorum .

consolidation of counties , KRS 67.310

LOCAL HEALTH UNITS AND OFFICERS § 212.210
(2) The county board of health of each
county having a county department of
health shall, subject to the approval of the
State Department of Health , appoint a
health officer who shall, subject to merit
system provisions, hold office at the pleas-
ure of both the county board and the State
Department of Health .
(3 ) A health officer may , with the ap-
proval of the State Department of Health
and the local boards of health concerned ,
serve in such capacity for more than one
county.
(4) A health officer may appoint an ad-
ministrative assistant for each county
served by him subject to the approval of
the State Department of Health . An ad-
ministrative assistant shall exercise such
duties as may be delegated to him by the
health officer .
(5) A health officer may employ and
fix the compensation of, by contract or
otherwise , subject to the approval of the
State Department of Health , all medical ,
technical , clerical , professional and other
employes necessary for the maintenance
and operation of the local health depart-
ment in accordance with standards and
merit system provisions prescribed by the
State Department of Health .
(6) In the absence of a local health of-
ficer , the Commissioner of Health or his
duly appointed representative shall serve
as health officer for the county concerned .
(1952 , с . 153 , § 5 ; 1954 , c . 209 , § 1 ; 1958 ,
c. 96 , § 2)
Note : By virtue of KRS 212.350 to 212.630 ,
enacted in 1942 , this section no longer applies
to counties containing a city of the first class .
212.180 [ 2054a - 10 ; 2054a - 15 ; 2054a-21 ;
2055 ] Health officer qualification . Every
health officer provided for by this
chapter shall be a physician duly licensed
by the State Board of Health , and shall
possess such other qualifications that are
prescribed by the State Board of Health .
(1952, с . 153 , § 6 ; subsection ( 2 ) of 1956
edition repealed , 1958 , c . 96 , § 10 )
212.190 [ 2054a -1 ; 2054a -10 ; 2054a - 15 ;
2055 ] Health officers ; compensation . ( 1 )
City health officers shall receive a reason-
able compensation for their services , to be
allowed by the legislative body of the city
and to be paid as other city officers are
paid.
(2 ) The health officer of counties that

212.210 [ 2054a - 14 ; 2057 ] Powers and
duties of all health boards . ( 1 ) The State
Board of Health and the local boards of
health may examine into all nuisances ,
sources of filth and causes of sickness that
may, in their opinion, be injurious to the
health of the inhabitants in any county in
this state , or in any vessel within any har-
bor or port in any county in this state .
Whenever any such nuisance , source of
filth or cause of sickness is found to exist
on any private property , or in any vessel
within any port or harbor in any county in
this state , or upon any watercourse in this
state , the State Board of Health or the
local board of health may order, in writ-
ing , the owner or occupant thereof, at his
own expense , to remove the same within
twenty -four hours , or within such reason-
able time thereafter as the board may
order.
(2) If drinking water used by school
children is found to be dangerous to their
health , the local board of health or State
Board of Health may order that a supply
of pure water be furnished at the expense
of the county or city board of education .
(3) If in the opinion of the local board
of health or State Board of Health a
school building is constructed in violation
of law and is found to be unsanitary or un

sanitary school building to be erected
within a reasonable time by the county or
city board of education in accordance with

2054a- 21 ; 2055 ] Powers and duties of local
health officers . ( 1 ) Every city health offi-
cer shall serve as executive officer and be
an ex- officio member of the city board of
health .
(2) The health officer of a county that
does not have a county department of
health shall enforce the rules and regula-
tions of state and county boards of health .
(3) The health officer of a county that
has formed a county department of health
shall :
( a) Devote his entire time to the duties
of his office , and shall not engage in the
private practice of medicine ;
(b) Be secretary of the county board of
health and keep full minutes of the pro-
ceedings of the county board of health in
a book provided for that purpose ;
(7) Through its health officers , repre-
sentatives and agents , enter upon any of the county health department .
premises when necessary for the purpose
of making inspections and investigations
and view evidence and interrogate persons
to the extent required in the performance
of their duties and responsibilities . The
department or the health officer thereof
may issue subpoenas , subpoena duces
tecum and all necessary process in pro-
ceedings brought before or initiated by
the department or board, and such process
shall extend to all parts of the Common-
wealth . Service of process may be made by
registered mail or in the manner pre-
scribed by the Rules of Civil Procedure ;
(8 ) Provide administrative , investiga-
(c) Be the chief administrative officer
(4) The duties of the health officer of
a district department of health shall be
the same as the duties of a health officer
of a county department of health , except
that his activities, authority and jurisdic-
tion shall extend to all of the counties
whose boards of health govern the district
department of health . He shall divide his
time devoted to the performance of his du-
ties reasonably and equitably between the
counties composing the district over which
he has jurisdiction , in accordance with the
rules and regulations adopted by the gov-
erning body of the district , which rules
and regulations must take into considera-

reported the procedure for filling such
vacancy or vacancies shall be the same as

vided , however , that no member of said
board shall be paid more than two hun-
dred and fifty dollars during any fiscal
year of the board, nor for more than
twenty - five meetings held during any fis-
cal year of said board . ( 1942 , c . 41 , § 4 )
212.410 Organization of board; oath ;
officers ; regulations ; records ; meetings ;
employes ; divisions . The appointive
members of the board , upon appointment
as herein provided , shall qualify by taking
the necessary oath of office before the
judge of the court of such county . Said
board , including the ex - officio members
who shall have the right to vote on mat-
ters acted upon by the board , shall there-
upon proceed to organize and elect from
among its appointed members a chairman
and a vice chairman , who shall serve for
one year and be eligible for reelection . A
majority of the members of the board
shall constitute a quorum for the trans-
action of business . The board for ad-
ministrative purposes is authorized to
create such administrative divisions as it
deems necessary , and may adopt any and
all necessary and appropriate rules and
regulations for the conduct of its business
and for carrying out the provisions of
KRS 212.350 to 212.620 , and shall keep a
record of its resolutions , transactions ,
findings and determinations , which record
shall be a public record . The board shall
hold at least one regular meeting in each
month , and such other meetings upon call
of the chairman of the board as may be
necessary . The board shall appoint a
director of health , with the qualifications
specified and subject to the provisions set
forth in KRS 212.420 , who shall serve as
secretary to and be administrative officer
for the board, and perform such other
duties as the board may direct . The
board is authorized , subject to the pro-
visions of KRS 212.350 to 212.620 , to em-
ploy such agents and employes as it deems
necessary effectively to carry out its
work . The administrative divisions cre-
ated by the board shall be under the di-
rection of the director of health , who for
administrative purposes shall organize
same into such divisions as may be neces-
sary for the proper conduct of the busi-
ness of the board . Said director shall
have the power to designate chiefs of
such divisions who , under his supervision
and control , shall have the direction of
such divisions . (1942 , c . 41 , § 5 )
Administrative organization of cities of first
class , KRS 83.160 to 83.300
212.420 Director of health ; qualifica-
tions , salary , term, removal . The director
of health shall be a physician , qualified as
a public health administrator as provided
by standards set up by the State Board of
Health of Kentucky and duly qualified
and licensed or eligible for license as a
medical practitioner in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky . He shall receive an
annual salary of five thousand dollars ,
37

§ 212.430 LOCAL HEALTH UNITS AND OFFICERS
payable as other salaries are paid, and
shall serve at the pleasure of the board .
If said director of health is removed by
the board he shall be notified thereof in
writing , and before such removal shall be-
come effective said director shall have ten
days within which to make a written re-
quest for a public hearing in regard
thereto . The board shall not be required
to hold a hearing unless so requested by
said director . If no such request is made
said removal shall become effective upon
the expiration of said ten -day period . If
such request is made said public hearing
shall be held at the office of the board
within ten days after such request is re-
ceived by the board, and said director
shall not be removed until after such
hearing has been held, and a decision ren-
dered by the board . The board's decision
shall be final . ( 1942 , c . 41 , § 6)
212.430 Compensation of employes ; civil
service . ( 1 ) The board shall fix the com-
pensation of all agents and employes of
the board, and such compensation shall be
as nearly comparable as practicable with
the compensations paid and received by
corresponding or comparable civil em-
ployes of such city or county .
(2) The agents and employes of the
board shall be employed and governed , as
provided herein , in accordance with the
merit system . For the purpose of gov-
erning the employment , appointment , sus-
pension , lay - off , and dismissal of employes
by the board , and personnel matters re-
lating thereto , any law or laws , or amend-
ments thereof, and any rules and regula-
tions issued pursuant thereto , authorizing ,
creating and governing any city board or
commission empowered to administer and
enforce civil service laws , rules and regu-
lations in and for such city are hereby
made applicable to the personnel and per-
sonnel matters of the board to the extent
of and with respect to corresponding and
comparable offices , positions and places of
employment of and under the board .
Such city board or commission is hereby
authorized and directed to perform , with-
out compensation from the board, all
things necessary to be done to accomplish
the aforesaid purpose , including the crea-
tion and putting into effect of, and main-
taining , a " classified service ," in accord-
ance with which the board will be
governed in the employment of agents
and employes and in the performance of
its duties under this section . The direc-
tor of health of the board shall function
as appointing authority in and with re-
spect to said personnel matters of the
board . Provided , however , regulations of
such city board or commission as may ad-
minister the civil service laws , rules and
regulations in such city , as applied to em-
ployes of the board, shall be not less
stringent than those of the merit system
of the State Board of Health of Kentucky .
(1942 , c . 41 , § 7 )
Civil service in cities of first class , KRS 90.110
to 90.240
212.432 Inclusion of employes in State
Retirement System . Notwithstanding the
provisions of KRS 61.510 to 61.700 ( Chap-
ter 110 , 1956 Acts of the General Assembly
of Kentucky and amendments thereto )
and KRS 78.510 to 78.991 , on July 1, 1962 ,
all regular full -time present and future
public health employes of any joint city-
county health department located in a
county containing a city of the first class
shall be included within the provisions of
the State Retirement System . (1962, c . 177 ,
§ 1)
Note : KRS 212.432 takes effect July 1, 1962 .
212.434 Prior service credit . All regular
full -time public health employes of any
joint city -county health department lo-
cated in a county containing a city of the
first class shall be given credit for any
prior service with such department before
July 1, 1956, but any such regular full-
time public health employe shall be al-
lowed credit for current service for the
period July 1, 1956 to July 1, 1962 ; pro-
vided, such employe pays to the retire-
ment system the contribution he would
have paid for the period from July 1, 1956
to July 1, 1962 , with interest at the rate
determined by the Board of Trustees and
an additional sum equal to fifty percent
of this amount . The payment of the fifty
percent sum shall be considered as accu-mulated contributions of the member .
(1962 , с . 177 , § 2 )
Note : KRS 212.434 takes effect July 1, 1962 .
212.436 Payment of contributions . It
shall be the responsibility of all joint city-
county health departments in counties
containing cities of the first class to pay
38

LOCAL HEALTH UNITS AND OFFICERS § 212.470
the employer's and employe's share into
the State Retirement Fund . ( 1962 , c . 177 ,
§ 3 )
Note : KRS 212.436 takes effect July 1, 1962 .
