212.490 Power to receive gifts and do-
nations ; use ; investment . ( 1 ) The board
may receive and accept specific gifts , do-
nations , contributions or endowments , the
principal or income from which , as the
case may be , shall be used for a specific
purpose other than the ordinary and reg-
ular operating costs and expenses , and
the board is hereby authorized to receive
same and use the principal or income
therefrom , as the case may be , for the
specific purpose for which such gift , de-
vise , bequest or endowment was made .
The amount of monies , funds , or assets so
on hand or held for the use and benefit of
these specific purposes shall not be con-
sidered for the purpose of KRS 212.350 to
212.620 as a balance at the end of the
board's respective fiscal years for the pur-
pose of reducing the amounts hereinabove
provided for operating expenses .
(2) Any donations given for immediate
expenditure may be expended at the dis-
cretion of the board , and such donations
as are given to it for investment may be
invested or reinvested and the income
from such investment or reinvestment ex-
pended in accordance with the terms of
such donations within the purview of
KRS 212.350 to 212.620 .
(3) Such board shall have the power to
mortgage , pledge , sell and convey lands
or goods so donated unless specifically
prohibited by the terms of the donation
and may , in its discretion , expend the pro-
ceeds in carrying out the purposes of KRS
212.350 to 212.620 or may if same are sold ,
invest or reinvest the proceeds in lands or
such securities as are permitted for the
investment of trust funds in KRS 386.020 ,
provided , however , that where the donor
has specified any particular method or
kind of investment the wishes of the
donor shall be complied with if possible .
(1942 , с . 41 , § 12 )
212.500 Selection of custodian for funds
from donations and investments ; bond ;
audits . ( 1 ) The board shall annually se-
lect a strong , safe , and conservative trust
company , doing business in the city in
which said board exists , as custodian of
the funds and securities of the board re-
ceived from donations and investments
for specific purposes , as set out above , and
may authorize said trust company to make
the actual investment and reinvestment
of said funds , but the proposed sale or
purchase of land and securities must first
be submitted to and approved of by the
board. The board shall exact from such
trust company a bond in double the
amount of any money or property re-
ceived under KRS 212.350 to 212.620, that
comes into said trust company's hands ,
and may take the capital stock of the
trust company as security or require an
additional surety bond or other form of
security , in such amount as it shall deem
40

LOCAL HEALTH UNITS AND OFFICERS § 212.540
necessary as security for the funds and
property in the custody of the trust com-
pany .
(2) All expenditures of said funds
shall be made only by order of the board
or by the proper officers designated by
said board .
(3 ) The board shall cause an annual
audit to be made of the receipts , expendi-
tures and investments of said fund by an
accountant or accountants who shall be
approved by the mayor and county judge
of the city and county where such board
is located . (1942 , c. 41 , § 12)
212.510 Use of donations for other pur-
poses . If , at any time in the future , the
causes , prevention and cure of tubercu-
losis or any other disease may be so well
established that research becomes unnec-
essary , or the purposes for which endow-
ments , gifts or donations were made be-
come unnecessary , then the board is
authorized , with the consent of the mayor
of said city and the county judge of said
county , or by an order of a circuit court
having general equity jurisdiction , to de-
vote the funds and income therefrom
which have been donated , set up , or other-
wise received for research work , along
any particular line of disease and cure as
may nearest approach the disease or pur-
pose for which said funds were donated
and received , or with the consent of the
mayor of said city and the county judge
of said county , or upon an order of a cir-
cuit court having general equity jurisdic-
tion , may donate said funds to such re-
search institutions as can best carry out
the wishes of various donors for the gen-
eral purposes for which said endowments ,
gifts or donations were made . ( 1942 , с .
41, § 12)
212.520 Fiscal year ; budget estimates .
The fiscal year of the board shall begin on
July 1 of each year and shall end on June
30 of the following year . It shall be the
duty of the appointive members of the
board , during or before the month of May
of each year, to prepare and certify to the
fiscal court of the county and to the legis-
lative body of the city, for their joint con-
sideration , a preliminary budget showing
the total funds which , in the judgment of
the appointive members of the board , will
be needed for its various departments , to
gether with a statement showing the esti-
mated balance , if any , which will be avail-
able on July 1 for expenditure during the
next fiscal year following the certification
of said statement , and also indicating , as
nearly as may be possible , what additional
funds or assets , other than appropriations ,
will be or become available for expenditure
during said year. The board shall also fur-
nish to said fiscal court and said legisla-
tive body any other information or data
available to it , which said fiscal court or
said legislative body may request . (1942 ,
c . 41, § 13 ; 1954, c. 168 , § 3 ; effective June
17, 1954)
212.530 Transfer of funds of former
health agencies . Immediately following
the qualification of the members and the
organization of the board and the election
of the director of health as herein pro-
vided for , the city , county, and board of
tuberculosis hospital shall each turn over
to the board for its sole use and benefit
under KRS 212.350 to 212.620 all unex-
pended and unencumbered taxes and
monies which have been collected under
any levy , budget or appropriation previ-
ously thereto made for the respective
health departments of the city and the
county and the board of tuberculosis hos-
pital; and thereafter the said city and
county shall likewise pay over to said
board all monies which may be collected
by said city and county on tax levies
made and set up in their respective bud-
gets or appropriations for said city health
department , county health department
and board of tuberculosis hospital prior
to such time as said board is organized
and takes over its duties as in KRS
212.350 to 212.620 provided for . ( 1942 ,
c. 41, § 13)
212.540 Budget . ( 1) When the respec-
tive appropriations have been duly made
by the city and county and an estimate of
the amount of moneys that the board will
receive from appropriations and from any
and all other sources , for a fiscal year has
been made , the board shall prepare and
certify to the fiscal court of the county and
the legislative body of the city for their
approval , a revised financial budget setting
forth the total amounts of funds available
from all sources for expenditures during
the board's fiscal year , and also setting
forth , in as great detail as possible with
respect to each administrative division , the
41

§ 212.550 LOCAL HEALTH UNITS AND OFFICERS
estimated expenditures of the board for the
said fiscal year .
(2) A contingent fund for unantici-
pated expenditures may , with the approval
of the fiscal court of the county and the
legislative body of the city , be established
in order to provide for such contingent and
unanticipated needs as may arise during
the board's said fiscal year and to supple-
ment allotments to divisions which may re-
quire the same . The board , as nearly as
may be practicable , shall make all disburse-
ments and expenditures in each fiscal year
in conformity with the revised budget allot-
ments ; but in the event of an emergency
the board may , with the approval of the
county judge and the mayor of the city,
reduce , withhold or transfer from one divi-
sion to another , funds or assets so allotted .
(1942 , c . 41 , § 14 ; 1954 , c. 168 , § 4 ; effec-
tive June 17, 1954)
212.550 Accounts and financial records ;
audits . The board shall install and main-
tain a modern and efficient system of ac-
counting and keep financial records . The
board , however , may select and use the
finance department of such city to do its
financial accounting and make its dis-
bursements in such manner as may be
agreed upon by and between the board
and the director of finance of said city ,
which work shall be done by said finance
department without compensation from
the board . The Auditor of Public Ac-
counts of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky , the comptroller and inspector of
such city , and the county auditor of such
county, respectively , shall have access to
the books and the records of the board,
and upon the request of the State Board
of Health of Kentucky said Auditor of
Public Accounts , or upon the direction of
the legislative body of such city the said
comptroller and inspector , or upon the
direction of the fiscal court of such county
the said county auditor , shall make an
audit of the board's accounts and report
back thereon . (1942 , c . 41, §15)
212.560 Current borrowing . The board
shall have the power to borrow money on
its own credit in anticipation of the reve-
nue to be derived from appropriations pro-
vided for herein or of funds to be derived
from other sources during the fiscal year
in which the same is borrowed ; and for
such purpose the board may pledge said
appropriations and funds , the collection
and payment of which is anticipated dur-
ing such year. (1942, c. 41, § 16 ; 1954 ,
c. 168 , § 5 ; effective June 17, 1954)

212.600 Board to control health in all
cities in county . All municipalities in any
county of this Commonwealth in which
county there is located a city of the first
class are hereby made subject to the pro-
visions of KRS 212.350 to 212.620 , and it
shall be the duty of the board created
herein to make and enforce all reasonable
regulations controlling or affecting the
health of citizens and residents of said
county , including all municipalities
therein , in conformity with the provisions
of KRS 212.350 to 212.620 and the laws of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , the rules
and regulations of the State Board of
Health of Kentucky , and the ordinances
of said municipalities now or hereafter in
effect and not in conflict with the provi-
sions of KRS 212.350 to 212.620 . Such
regulations shall , as nearly as may be
practicable , be uniform throughout the
county , both within and without the said
municipalities ; provided , however , that
nothing herein contained shall be con-
strued to prevent the board from making
specific health regulations applying only
to such section or sections of said county
as may be deemed to require special treat-
ment . The board shall have power and
authority to examine into all nuisances ,
sources of filth , and causes or probable
causes of sickness , which may in its opin-
ion be injurious to the health of the resi-
dents of such county or of any section or
sections thereof. ( 1942 , с . 41 , § 20)
212.610 Public hearings ; production of
evidence . The board , in such manner as
it shall provide by appropriate rules and
regulations , is authorized to and shall
conduct public hearings , upon notice duly
given , concerning violations , committed
within its jurisdiction , of the provisions
of KRS 212.350 to 212.620 , or the afore-
said health laws, ordinances , or regula-
tions , or of regulations or orders of the
board herein created . The board, for the
purpose aforesaid , is authorized to sub-
poena and examine witnesses , and certify
official acts . Any member of the board is
empowered and authorized to administer
oaths and swear witnesses in any matter
that is before the board . (1942 , c . 41 , § 20 )
212.620 Abatement of nuisances ; en-
forcement of orders . ( 1 ) Whenever any
such aforesaid nuisance , source of filth , or
cause or probable cause of sickness , shall
be found by the board to exist on any
private or public property within such
county, including the municipalities
therein , in violation of said laws or regu-
lations , which said violation injuriously
affects or may affect the health of the
residents of said county or section
thereof , the board shall have the power
and authority to order in writing the
owner or occupant or user thereof , by ap-
propriate action , at the expense of such
owner , occupant or user , to correct and
remove said nuisance , source of filth , or
cause or probable cause of sickness ,
within twenty -four hours or within such
reasonable time as the board may order.
(2 ) In case of a failure on the part of
any person , firm or corporation , or per-
sons , firms or corporations , to comply
with any lawful order of the board, or
with process , or in case of the refusal of
any witness to testify concerning any
matter on which he may be lawfully in-
terrogated , the circuit court or a judge
thereof, having jurisdiction in such
county, may , on application of the board
or of any member thereof , compel obedi-
ence by proceedings for contempt as in
the case of disobedience of the require-
ments of a subpoena issued from such a
court , or a refusal to testify therein .
( 1942 , c. 41, § 20 )

dered repaired , closed or demolished ,

PUBLIC HEALTH TAXING DISTRICTS
212.720 Election for creation of public
health taxing district . Upon request of
any county or city -county board of health
except in counties containing a city of the
first class, the fiscal court of the county
shall adopt a resolution submitting to the
qualified voters of the county , the question
as to whether a public health taxing dis-
trict shall be established for the county .
A certified copy of the order of the fiscal
court shall be filed with the county clerk
at least forty -five days prior to the next
regular election and thereupon the county
clerk shall cause the question to be placed
upon the ballot . The question shall be in
the following form : " Are you in favor of
establishing a public health taxing district
for (insert name of county ) county which
shall have the authority to impose a special
ad valorem tax for the maintenance and
operation of the ( insert name of county or
city-county ) health department , provided
that such special tax shall not exceed ( in-
sert amount ) cents on each one hundred
dollars of the assessed valuation of all
property in the district ? " If a majority of
those voting on the question favor the estab-
lishment of a public health taxing district
for the county it shall be so established
and shall constitute and be a taxing district
within the meaning of Section 157 of the
Constitution of Kentucky . The members
of the county or city -county board of health
shall , by virtue of their office , constitute
and be the governing body of the public
health taxing district and shall perform
the duties attendant thereto in addition to
their duties as members of the county or
city -county board of health . The officers
of the county or city -county board of health
shall be the officers of the public health tax-
ing district . ( 1954 , c. 156, § 1 ; effective
June 17, 1954 )

213.080 [ 2062a-7 ; 2062a - 14 ] Certificate
of death ; who to sign ; what to contain . (1 )
The certificate of death shall be prescribed
and furnished by the State Department of
Health and shall be designed with due con-
sideration for national uniformity . It shall
be written legibly in unfading ink or type-
written . The personal and statistical par-
ticulars shall be authenticated by the sig-
nature of the informant , who may be any
competent person acquainted with the
facts . The statement of facts relating to
the disposition of the body shall be signed
by the undertaker or person acting as
such .
(2) The medical certificate shall be
made and signed by the physician , if any,
last in attendance on the deceased , who
shall specify the time in attendance , the
time he last saw the deceased alive , and
the hour of the day at which death oc-
curred . He shall further state the cause of
death , showing the course of disease or se-
quence of causes resulting in death , giving
the primary cause and any contributory
causes , and the duration of each . Indefi-
nite and unsatisfactory terms , indicating
only symptoms of disease or conditions re-
sulting from disease , will not be sufficient
for issuing a burial or removal permit .
Any certificate not containing the terms
defined by the Division of Vital Statistics
shall be returned to the physician for cor-
rection and definition . Where death may
be the result of either disease or violence ,
the causes shall be carefully defined, and
if the result of violence its nature shall be
stated , and whether it appears to have
been accidental , suicidal or homicidal . In
case of deaths in hospitals , institutions or
away from home , the physician shall fur-
nish the information required in this sec-
tion and shall state where , in his opinion ,
the disease was contracted . (1958 , c. 97,
§ 4 ; effective June 19 , 1958 )
Chiropractor may sign death certificate ,
KRS 312.190
Dentist may sign death certificate , KRS 313.250
213.090 [ 2062a - 8 ] Notice of death with
out physician in attendance ; duties of cor-
oner . ( 1 ) When a death occurs without a
physician in attendance , the undertaker
shall give notice of the death to the regis-
trar , who shall then refer the case to the
local health officer for immediate investi-
gation and certification , prior to issuing
the burial permit . If the circumstances of
the case render it probable that the death
was caused by crime , suicide , or by drown-
ing or other sudden cause , or if death oc-
curred without the attendance of a physi-
cian at any time during the thirty -six
hours immediately prior to death , or if
the body is to be cremated or transported
out of the state for burial , the registrar
shall refer the case to the coroner for his
investigation and certification .
(2 ) Any coroner whose duty it is to
hold an inquest on the body of any de-
ceased person , and to make the certificate
of death required for a burial permit, shall
state in his certificate the nature of the
disease or other cause of death . If the
death was from external causes of vio-
lence , he shall state whether in his opinion
the death was accidental , suicidal or homi-
cidal . He shall in any case furnish the
information required by the Division of
Vital Statistics to properly classify the
death . ( 1960 , c . 51 , § 2 ; effective June 16 ,
1960 )
When coroner to be notified of death , KRS 72.020
213.100 [ 2062a -5 ; 2062a - 9 ] Obtaining
and filing certificate of death . The under-
taker or person acting as undertaker shall
be responsible for obtaining and filing the
certificate of death with the registrar , and
securing a burial or removal permit , prior
to any disposition of the body . He shall
obtain the required personal and statis-
tical particulars from the person best
qualified to supply them , over the signa-
ture and address of his informant . He
shall then present the certificate to the
attending physician , if any , or to the
health officer or coroner , as directed by
the registrar , for the medical certificate
of the cause of death and other particulars
necessary to complete the record, as re-
quired by KRS 213.090. He shall then
state the facts required relative to the date
and place of burial , over his signature and
with his address , and present the com-
pleted certificate to the registrar , within
the time limit , if any, designated by the
local board of health for the issuance of
a burial or removal permit . If there is no
undertaker or person who acts as such , the
head of the family in which the death
occurred shall, within five days of the date
49

§ 213.110 VITAL STATISTICS
of death , notify the local registrar of the
district in which the death occurred of the
fact of the death . The local registrar shall
then procure , promptly , the certificate of
death .
Embalmers and Funeral Directors , Ch . 316
213.110 [ 2062a -5 ; 2062a -9 ; 2062a - 19 ]
Burial , removal and transit permits ; regu-
lations as to . ( 1 ) The body of any person
whose death occurs in this state shall not
be interred , deposited in a vault or tomb ,
cremated or otherwise disposed of , or re-
moved from or into any registration dis-
trict , until a permit for burial , removal or
other disposition has been issued by the
registrar of the registration district in
which the death occurs . If the death oc-
curred from a disease declared by the
State Board of Health to be infectious ,
contagious or communicable and danger-
ous to the public health , no permit for the
removal or other disposition of the body
shall be granted by the registrar except
under conditions prescribed by the state
and local boards of health . Except as
provided in subsections ( 2 ) and ( 3 ) of this
section , no burial or removal permit shall
be issued by any registrar until a complete
and satisfactory certificate and return of
the death has been filed with him . If any
certificate of death is incomplete or un-
satisfactory , the registrar shall call atten-
tion to the defects in the return , and shall
withhold issuing the burial or removal
permit until they are corrected .
(2) A transit permit issued in accord-
ance with the law and health regulations
of the place where the death occurred ,
whether in or outside of this state , may be
accepted by the local registrar of the dis-
trict where the body is to be interred or
otherwise finally disposed of , in lieu of a
certificate of death , but he shall plainly
enter on the face of the copy of the record
made for return to the Division of Vital
Statistics the fact that it was a body
shipped in for interment and the actual
place of death . If the transit permit was
issued outside of this state the local regis-
trar of the district where the body is to
be finally disposed of shall issue a local
burial permit , but if the transit permit
was issued in this state the registrar's
burial or removal permit from the district
where the death occurred may be accepted
as authority for burial at the point of
destination, without the issuance of a local
burial permit .
(3) Nothing in this section shall be con-
strued to delay , beyond a reasonable time ,
the interment or other disposition of a
body , unless the services of the coroner or
the health officer are required or the State
Board of Health deems it necessary for the
protection of the public health . If compli-
ance with this section would result in un-
reasonable delay in the disposition of the
body, the undertaker or person acting as
such shall file with the local registrar or
deputy registrar prior to interment , a pro-
visional certificate of death , which shall
contain the name , date and place of death
of the deceased and an agreement to fur-
nish within five days a complete and satis-
factory certificate of death and the under-
taker or person acting as such shall se-
cure a complete and satisfactory certifi-
cate of death as provided in KRS 213.100
and return it within five days from the
date of burial to the local registrar of the
district in which the death occurred .
(4) The undertaker shall deliver the
burial permit to the sexton or person in
charge of the place of burial , before inter-
ring the body, or, if the body is to be
shipped by a transportation company ,
shall attach the transit permit , containing
the registrar's removal permit, to the box
containing the body . The transit permit
shall accompany the body to its destina-
tion . ( 1958 , c . 97 , § 5 ; effective June 19 ,
1958)
Medical college may procure unclaimed dead
bodies , KRS 311.300 to 311.350
213.120 [ 2062a - 10 ] Form of burial per-
mit . The form of the burial permit shall be
prescribed and furnished by the State De-
partment of Health . ( 1958 , c . 97 , § 6 ; ef-
fective June 19 , 1958)
213.122 Use of unclaimed dead bodies
for educational or scientific purposes ;
transportation ; records . (1) Notwith-
standing the provisions of KRS 311.300
to 311.350 , any hospital or institution may
make application to any medical school
incorporated within this state for the
loan of unclaimed whole dead bodies or
parts thereof for educational or scientific
purposes , provided the approval of the
State Board of Health has first been ob-
tained. Approval shall be granted or
denied by the State Board of Health on
the basis of proposed use , need , qualifi
50

VITAL STATISTICS § 213.150
cation of personnel and adequacy of equip- board of health . He shall also keep a
ment and facilities .
( 2 ) A special transit permit shall be
obtained for the transportation of such
dead bodies or parts thereof from the
Division of Vital Statistics of the State
Department of Health . Transportation
from the medical school and back to the
medical school and the ultimate burial of
all such bodies or parts thereof shall be
in accordance with the provisions of this
chapter and KRS 311.340 .
(3 ) All approved recipients shall keep
a record of all bodies received by them
and comply with all other regulations of
the State Board of Health . ( 1962 , c . 158 )
213.125 Cremation ; coroner's certifi-
cate ; permit . No person shall cremate or
cause to be cremated , or transport or cause
to be transported for the purpose of cre-
mation , the body of any person whose
death occurs in this state , without first
obtaining from the coroner of the county
in which the death occurred , a death cer-
tificate stating the cause of death and
authorizing the cremation , or transporta-
tion for cremation , of the body . Such
certificate shall be filed immediately with
the local registrar of vital statistics . No
burial transit permit for the cremation or
transportation for the purpose of crema-
tion of the body of any such person shall
be issued pursuant to KRS 213.110 by any
registrar of vital statistics , except upon
such authorization by the coroner as pro-
vided herein . ( 1960 , c . 51 , § 1 ; effective
June 16 , 1960 )
213.130 [2062a - 11 ] Duties of persons in
charge of burial grounds . No sexton or
person having charge of or being the
owner of any cemetery , crematorium or
other premises in which dead bodies are
interred , cremated , or otherwise disposed
of , shall inter , cremate , or permit the in-
terment , cremation , or other disposition
of the body of any deceased person on
such premises unless it is accompanied by
a burial , removal , or transit permit as
required by KRS 213.110 . Fach such per-
son shall indorse upon the permit , over his
signature , the date of interment , crema-
tion or other disposition of the body, and
return all permits so indorsed to the local
registrar within ten days from the date
of interment , cremation or other disposi-
tion , or within the time fixed by the local
record of all interments , cremation , or
other dispositions of bodies made on the
premises under his ownership or charge ,
stating the name of the deceased , his place
of birth , the date of burial , cremation or
other disposition , and the name and ad-
dress of the undertaker . This record shall
at all times be open to public inspection .
(1960, c . 51 , § 3 ; effective June 16 , 1960 )
213.140 [ 2062a -16 ] Physicians , mid-
wives and undertakers to register . Every
physician , midwife and undertaker shall
register his name , address and occupation
with the local registrar of the district in
which he resides , and shall thereupon be
supplied by the local registrar with a copy
of this chapter , together with the rules
and regulations prepared by the Division
of Vital Statistics relative to its enforce-
ment . Within thirty days after the close
of each calendar year, each local registrar
shall make a return to the Division of
Vital Statistics of all physicians and mid-
wives who have been registered in his
district during the whole or any part of
the preceding calendar year. No fee or
other compensation shall be charged by
local registrars to physicians , midwives or
undertakers for registering their names
under this section , or making returns
thereof to the Division of Vital Statistics .
213.150 [ 2062a -4 ; 2062a -20 ] Fees of
local registrar , physician , midwife , sexton
and undertaker . ( 1) Each physician or
midwife shall be entitled to receive the
sum of twenty - five cents for each certifi-
cate of birth or death properly and cor-
rectly filled out, signed and reported .
Each local registrar in a county having
no county health department shall be en-
titled to receive the sum of fifty cents for
each such certificate registered with him
and correctly copied and duly returned
to the Division of Vital Statistics . In case
no births or deaths are registered during
any month , the local registrar in a county
having no health department shall be en-
titled to receive the sum of fifty cents for
each report to that effect promptly made
in accordance with this chapter .
(2) All amounts payable to local reg-
istrars , physicians and midwives under
this section , shall be paid by the treasurer
of the county in which the registration
district is located upon certification by
the Division of Vital Statistics .
51

§ 213.160 VITAL STATISTICS
(3 ) The Division of Vital Statistics
shall certify , at least annually to the
treasurers of the several counties , the
number of births and deaths registered
together with the names of the local reg-
istrars , physicians and midwives entitled
to fee prescribed by this section , and the
amount due each at the rate fixed herein .
(4) No local registrar , physician , mid-
wife , sexton or undertaker shall charge
or collect from any member of a family in
which a birth or death occurs , any fee for
complying with the provisions of this
chapter . ( 1952 , с . 126 ; 1954, с . 135 , § 4)
213.160 [ 2062a - 17 ] Institutions to keep
records of births , deaths and sickness . ( 1 )
The superintendent , manager or other per-
son in charge of any institution , public or
private , to which persons resort for treat-
ment of diseases or confinement , or are
committed by process of law , shall, at the
time of admission of any person to the in-
stitution , make a record of all the personal
and statistical particulars relative to that
person that are required in the form of
the certificate provided for by this chap-
ter , as directed by the Division of Vital
Statistics . In case of persons admitted or
committed for medical treatment of dis-
ease , the physician in charge shall specify ,
for entry in the record, the name of the
disease and where , in his opinion, it was
contracted . The personal particulars from
the individual himself, if practicable , and
if not they shall be secured in as complete
a manner as possible from relatives , at-
tending physicians and midwives .
( 2 ) Institutions specified in subsection
(1) shall prepare and forward a report on
the first day of each week to the county
health department , listing all births and
deaths occurring during the preceding
week . The birth list shall specify the date
of birth , the name of the father and the
maiden name of the mother . The death list
shall specify the name of the deceased ,
the date of death , and the name of the fu-
neral director removing the body for bur-
ial. The State Department of Health shall
furnish each such institution with neces-
sary forms for making such reports. ( 1958 ,
c . 97, § 7 ; effective June 19 , 1958 )
213.170 [ 2062a - 19 ] Local registrars to
distribute forms ; records and reports . The
local registrar shall supply blank forms of
certificates to persons who require them .
He shall carefully examine each certifi-
cate of birth , stillbirth or death when
presented for record, to see that it has
been properly made out . If the certifi-
cates are properly made out he shall sign
his name as registrar in attest of the date
of filing in his office . He shall also make
a complete and accurate copy of each
certificate , to be filed and permanently
preserved in his office as the local record ,
in the manner directed by the Division of
Vital Statistics . He shall on the first and
fifteenth of each month transmit to the
Division of Vital Statistics all original
certificates registered by him. When a
birth , stillbirth or death certificate filed
with a local registrar shows the residence
of the mother or the deceased to be in
another county , the registrar shall mail
a copy of the certificate to the local reg-
istrar of the county of residence . If no
births , stillbirths or deaths occur in any
month , he shall , on the first and fifteenth
of each month , report that fact to the
Division of Vital Statistics . (1954, c. 135 ,
§ 5 ; effective July 1, 1954)
213.180 [ 2062a - 2 ; 2062a - 18 ] Forms ; in-
structions ; reports ; records ; duties and
powers of State Board of Health as to .
(1) The State Board of Health shall pre-
pare , print and supply to all registrars
suitable blanks and forms used in register-
ing , recording and preserving the returns
or in otherwise carrying out the provisions
of this chapter , and shall prepare and is-
sue such detailed instructions as may be
required to secure the uniform observance
of its provisions and the maintenance of a
perfect system of registration . No other
blanks shall be used than those supplied
by the State Board of Health . The Divi-
sion of Vital Statistics shall carefully ex-
amine the certificates received monthly
from the local registrars , and if any are in-
complete or unsatisfactory shall require
that further information , necessary to
make the certificate complete and satisfac-
tory , be furnished . All physicians , mid-
wives and undertakers connected with
the case shall furnish any information
they possess regarding any birth or death ,
upon demand of the Division of Vital
Statistics in person , by mail , or through
the local registrar .
(2) The Division of Vital Statistics
shall arrange , bind and permanently pre-
serve the certificates in a systematic man
52

VITAL STATISTICS § 213.210
ner , and shall prepare and maintain a
comprehensive and continuous card index
of all births , sickness and deaths regis-
tered . The cards shall show the name of
the child , inmate or deceased , the place
and date of birth , sickness or death , the
number of the certificate , and the volume
in which it is contained . The division
shall inform all registrars what diseases
are to be considered as infectious , con-
tagious or communicable and dangerous
to the public health as decided by the
State Board of Health , in order that , when
sickness and deaths occur from such dis-
eases , proper precautions may be taken
to prevent the spreading of dangerous
diseases .
(3) The State Board of Health shall ,
every five years , have printed and bound
in suitable volumes , not to exceed in num-
ber one hundred and fifty sets , an alpha-
betical index list of all births and deaths
reported and registered , setting out the
full name appearing on the certificate , fol-
lowed by the name of the county, the com-
plete date , the volume and certificate num-
bers and in cases of births the name of the
mother , and the Director of the Division
of Vital Statistics shall certify to the cor-
rectness of the index list . The index list
shall be prima facie evidence of the in-
formation therein contained in all courts
of the state .
(4) A copy of the volume or volumes
containing the index list of births and
deaths shall be furnished and delivered
to each county health department and
shall become a permanent record of the
county . Any such volumes now in pos-
session of the county court clerk may be
transferred to the county health depart-
ment with the approval of the county
judge .
(5) The cost of preparing , printing and
delivering the index list shall be paid from
appropriations made to the Department
of Health . (1954, c . 135 , § 6 )
Change of records in case of adoption ,
KRS 199.570
213.190 [ 2062a - 21 ] Certified copies of
records ; fees . (1) The Division of Vital
Statistics shall, upon request , furnish any
applicant a certified copy of the record of
any birth , death , or other record regis-
tered with such division as provided by
law when the division is satisfied that the
applicant has a direct , tangible and legiti-
mate interest in the information or record
requested . The applicant shall pay a fee
of one dollar for a copy of a birth , death ,
or other certificate or search of the files if
no copy is found . Any such certified copy
shall be prima facie evidence of the facts
therein stated .
(2) No fee or compensation shall be al-
lowed or paid for furnishing certificates
of birth or death required in support of
any claim against the government for
compensation , insurance , back pay or oth-
er allowances of any person who has at
any time served as a member of the Army,
Navy , Marine Corps or Air Force of the
United States . (1954 , c. 135 , § 7 ; 1958 , с .
97 , § 8 ; effective June 19 , 1958 )
Fees to be paid into state treasury , KRS 41.070
213.195 Delayed birth certificates for
persons born prior to 1911. The Division
of Vital Statistics may register delayed
birth certificates for persons born in the
Commonwealth prior to 1911. The facts
stated in the certificate shall be supported
by such evidence as is required by the
regulations of the State Board of Health .
Certificates shall be prepared on special
forms supplied by the division . Certifi-
cates thus recorded shall be preserved and
treated as all other certificates . ( 1954 ,
c . 135 , § 8 ; effective July 1 , 1954)
213.200 [2062a-24 ] Privileged communi-
cations . For the purpose of this chapter ,
the confidential relations and communica-
tions between physician and patient are
placed upon the same basis as those pro-
vided by law between attorney and client ,
and nothing in this chapter shall be con-
strued to require any such privileged com-
munication to be disclosed .
213.210 [ 2062a -23 ] Enforcement . Local
registrars are charged with the strict and
thorough enforcement in their districts of
the provision of this chapter , under the
supervision and direction of the Division
of Vital Statistics . They shall make an
immediate report to the Division of Vital
Statistics of any violations of this chapter
coming to their attention . The Division
of Vital Statistics is charged with the
thorough and efficient execution of the
provisions of this chapter in every part of
the state , and with supervisory power
over local registrars , to the end that all
of the requirements shall be uniformly
complied with . It may investigate cases
of irregularity or violation of law , and
53

§ 213.320 VITAL STATISTICS
all registrars shall aid it , upon request , in
such investigations. When the division
deems it necessary , it shall report cases of
violation of any of the provisions of this
chapter , with a statement of the facts and
circumstances , to the prosecuting attorney
of the proper county or municipality ,
who shall forthwith initiate and promptly
follow up the necessary court proceedings
to prosecute the violation .
213.320 Domestic relations statistics
agency ; regulations . There is hereby
created within the Division of Vital Sta-
tistics of the State Department of Health
a section for the collection , indexing , tabu-
lations, and registration of data relating
to marriages , divorces , annulments and
adoptions . The State Board of Health
shall adopt such rules and regulations as
may be necessary to carry out the pro-
visions of KRS 213.320 to 213.340 . ( 1958 ,
c . 34, § 1 ; effective June 19 , 1958 )
213.330 Report of marriage statistics ;
clerk's fee . Each county court clerk shall ,
on or before the tenth day of each month ,
furnish to the Division of Vital Statistics
from the marriage licenses issued and the
marriage certificates returned to the clerk
during the previous month such informa-
tion as may be required by the State De-
partment of Health upon forms prescribed
and furnished by the department . The
county court clerk shall collect from the
applicants for a marriage license at the
time the license is issued fifty cents which
shall constitute his fee for forwarding the
required information to the Division of
Vital Statistics of the State Department
of Health . ( 1958 , c . 34, § 2 ; 1962 , c . 209 ,
§ 1)
213.340 Report of information and final
judgments in divorce and annulment
cases ; fee . ( 1 ) In all actions for divorce ,
absolute or limited or annulment of mar-
riage, the plaintiff or his attorney or
legal representative shall file concurrently
with the complaint, such information as
is requested on forms prescribed and fur-
nished by the Division of Vital Statistics .
(2) Each circuit court clerk shall, within
forty -five days after entry of a final
judgment of divorce , absolute or limited
or annulment of marriage, complete the
form prescribed and furnished by the
Division of Vital Statistics and forward
it to the Division of Vital Statistics .
(3) The circuit court clerk shall be paid
a fee of fifty cents for preparing and
forwarding the required information to
the Division of Vital Statistics of the
State Department of Health . The fee for
each such report shall be added to the
court costs allowed in such cases. ( 1958 ,
c . 34 , § 3 ; 1960 , с. 236 ; 1962 , c . 209 , § 2 )
213.990 [ 2055a ; 2062a -4 ; 2062a -22 ]
Penalties . ( 1 ) Any physician in medical
attendance upon any deceased person at
the time of death who neglects or refuses ,
on request , to make out and deliver to the
undertaker , sexton or other person in
charge of the disposition of the body themedical certificate of cause of death shall
be fined not less than five dollars nor more
than fifty dollars .
(2) Repeated failure to report births
and deaths shall be sufficient cause for the
revocation of a physician's certificate to
practice medicine in this state .
(3 ) Any physician who knowingly
makes a false certification of the cause of
death in any case shall be fined not less
than fifty dollars nor more than two hun-
dred dollars .
(4) Any person who fails to comply
with the provisions of subsection ( 1 ) or
(2 ) of KRS 213.050 shall be fined not less
than five dollars nor more than fifty dol-
lars .
(5) Any undertaker , sexton or other
person acting as undertaker who inters ,
removes , or otherwise disposes of the body
of any deceased person without the re-
quired burial or removal permit shall be
fined not less than twenty dollars nor
more than one hundred dollars .
( 6) Any registrar or deputy registrar
who neglects or fails to enforce the pro-
visions of this chapter in his district , or
neglects or refuses to perform any of the
duties imposed upon him by this chapter
or by the instructions and directions of
the Division of Vital Statistics , shall be
fined not less than ten dollars nor more
than one hundred dollars . In addition ,
any local registrar who fails to perform
efficiently the duties of his office shall
forthwith be removed from office by the
county health officer .
(7) Any person who willfully alters any
certificate of birth or death , or the copy
of any certificate of birth or death , on file
54

VITAL STATISTICS § 213.990
in the office of the local registrar , shall be
fined not less than ten dollars nor more
than one hundred dollars , or be impris-
oned in the county jail not more than
sixty days , or both .
(8) Any person who violates any of the
provisions of this chapter or who willfully
neglects or refuses to perform any duties
imposed upon him by the provisions of
this chapter for which no specific penalty
is provided , or who furnishes false infor-
mation to a physician , undertaker , mid-
wife or informant for the purpose of mak-
ing an incorrect certification of birth or
death , shall be fined not less than five dol-
lars nor more than one hundred dollars .
or(9 ) Any transportation company
common carrier that transports or carries ,
or accepts for transportation or carriage ,
the body of any deceased person without
an accompanying removal or transit per-
mit , shall be fined not less than fifty dol-
lars nor more than two hundred dollars .
In case the death occurred outside the
state , and the body is accompanied by a
certificate of death , or a burial , removal
or transit permit issued in accordance with
the law or health regulations in force
where the death occurred , such death cer-
tificate , or burial , removal or transit per-
mit will authorize the transportation or
carriage of the body into or through this
state .
(10 ) Any person who violates KRS
213.125 shall be fined not less than one
thousand dollars nor more than five
thousand dollars or imprisoned in the
county jail for not less than thirty days
nor more than one year, or both . ( Sub-
section ( 10 ) added , 1960, c . 51 , § 4 ; effec-
tive June 16 , 1960 )

214.020 [ 2049 ; 2056 ] Quarantine . ( 1 )
When the State Board of Health believes
that there is a probability that any in-
fectious or contagious disease will invade
this state , it shall take such action and
adopt and enforce such rules and regula-
tions as it deems efficient in preventing
the introduction or spread of such in-
fectious or contagious disease or diseases
within this state , and to accomplish these
objects shall establish and strictly main
56

COMMUNICABLE DISEASES § 214.034
tain quarantine at such places as it deems railway or road for a time sufficient to dis-
proper .
(2 ) The State Board of Health may
establish quarantine ground in some suita-
ble place and establish the quarantine to
be observed in the locality ; cause to be
erected temporary buildings or hospitals
necessary for the medical treatment of any
person who may be kept in quarantine and
affected with contagious or infectious dis-
ease , and cause the inspection or disinfec-
tion of travelers ' baggage , merchandise ,
and articles in transit through such quar-
antine grounds or stations ; enforce in-
spection of persons and articles at such
stations or grounds, as well as the purifica-
tion of persons , baggage and articles, and
require the transportation of passengers
from such quarantine station ; and assign
the charge and control of each quarantine
station to a competent physician and his
necessary assistants or employes , who shall
receive such compensation as the board
may fix as the value of their services .
(3) In the counties bordering on the
state boundary lines , the local boards of
health may declare and maintain quar-
antine in the county, or in any particular
place therein , against the introduction of
any contagious or infectious disease pre-
vailing in any other state or county.
When such quarantine is established , the
local board declaring the quarantine shall ,
through its presiding or chief officer , give
the State Board of Health written notice
of the quarantine and of its extent . The
State Board of Health shall , as early as
practicable , by its sanitary or executive
committee , ascertain the necessity for the
quarantine , and shall either approve and
enforce the quarantine or declare it raised .
(4) When the State Board of Health
and its agents and employes , or the local
boards of health acting under the direc-
tion and regulations of the state board ,
have reasonable ground to believe that any
watercraft navigating the Mississippi or
Ohio Rivers or any of their tributaries is
infected with any epidemic or infectious
disease , they may prevent the landing of
the craft at any point or place on the Ken-
tucky shore . When they have reasonable
grounds to believe any railway train , coach
or other vehicle contains persons or arti-
cles infected with epidemic or infectious
diseases , they may detain the train , coach
or vehicle at any station or point on such
infect or purify it .
( 5 ) When quarantine has been estab-
lished pursuant to subsection ( 4) of this
section no railway conductor , driver or
person in charge of any coach or vehicle
shall willfully avoid or prevent the in-
spection or purification of the coaches
or vehicles under his charge or control .

214.030 [ 2061a-1 ] Prevention of tuber-
culosis ; powers of State Board of Health .
The powers and duties of the State Board
of Health in the study and prevention of
tuberculosis are coextensive with the state ,
and the objects of the Division of Tuber-
culosis are :
( a) To study tuberculosis in all its
forms and relations , to secure and dis-
seminate information with reference to
tuberculosis , to promote and carry on a
campaign of education with reference
thereto , and in general to pursue any other
activities to inform the public as to the
nature of tuberculosis , its dangers and the
means whereby its spread may be pre-
vented .
( b ) To secure proper legislation for
the relief and prevention of tuberculosis .
( c ) To cooperate with the public au-
thorities , state and local boards of health ,
the National Association for the Study
and Prevention of Tuberculosis , medical so-
cieties and other organizations in ap-
proved measures adapted for the preven-
tion of tuberculosis .
(d) To encourage the establishment
throughout the state of local associations
for the purpose of undertaking in their
particular localities the work carried on
by the division .
( e ) To encourage adequate provision
for consumptives by the establishment of
sanatoria , hospitals and dispensaries .

KRS 211.100

214.130 [ 2062b - 1 ; 2062b -4 ] Annual
schools to be held for prevention of tra-
choma , ophthalmia and other eye diseases .
( 1 ) Each county board of health shall , in
cooperation with the county medical so-
ciety and the State Board of Health , ar-
range for an annual school for the phy-
sicians , midwives and nurses of the county
to teach the importance of , the latest and
best methods for the early recognition and
treatment of , the dangers from , and the
precautions to be used against , infection
and contagion to all who come in contact
with cases of trachoma , ophthalmia or any
other disease of the eyes of the newborn ,
or with anything used by or for them ,
and the importance and imperative duty of
at once reporting all cases of such diseases
to the local health authorities , and of
keeping a true record of all such cases .
(2) As used in this section and in KRS
214.150 , “ ophthalmia in the newborn "
means any inflammation , swelling and red-
ness of the eye , or eyes , of a baby , either
apart from or together with any unnatural
discharge from the eye .
214.140 [ 2062b -2 ] Duties of state and
county boards of health as to eye diseases .
The State Board of Health shall secure
the cooperation and assistance of the na-
tional health authorities in dealing with
the diseases mentioned in KRS 214.130 ,
and shall prepare and issue bulletins and
other literature containing information as
to the prevalence and infectious character
of such diseases , and the precautions to be
used against infection , and shall furnish
formulae and other information for the
use of physicians and midwives in the
management and treatment of these dis-
eases . The county boards of health shall
furnish physicians and midwives with the
simple drugs to be used for the indigent
in preventing and treating these diseases .
Division for Prevention of Trachoma and Blind-
ness , purposes of , KRS 211.100
214.150 [ 2062b -3 ] Duties of physicians ,
nurses , heads of institutions and heads of
families as to eye diseases . ( 1 ) Every
physician , every midwife and every trained
nurse who , while in attendance upon a
baby , less than thirty days old or upon its
mother , has observed ophthalmia in the
baby , shall report the case of ophthalmia
to the local board of health . If the case
occurs within the jurisdiction of a city
board of health it shall be reported to the
city board within six hours after observa-
tion , and if the case occurs within the
jurisdiction of a county board of health it
shall be reported to the county board
within twenty-four hours after observa-
tion . Where there is no physician , mid-
wife or trained nurse in attendance upon
the baby or its mother , the case shall be
reported , in the same manner and within
the same time , by the head of the family
and the head of the institution in which
the baby is .
(2 ) Every physician shall report each
case of trachoma diagnosed by him as at-
tending or examining physician within
five days after such diagnosis .
(3 ) Every physician , midwife and nurse
shall take the necessary precautions to
prevent the spread of ophthalmia and tra-
choma
214.160 [2062b - 5 ] Blood specimen of
pregnant women to be taken ; laboratory
test . Every physician and every other
person legally permitted to engage in at-
tendance upon a pregnant woman in this
state shall take or cause to be taken from
the woman a specimen of blood , for sero-
logical test for syphilis , as soon as he is
engaged to attend the woman and has
reasonable grounds for suspecting that
pregnancy exists . If the woman is in
labor at the time the diagnosis of preg-
nancy is made , which may make it inad-
visable to obtain a blood specimen then ,

the specimen shall be obtained within ten
days after delivery . The specimen of
blood shall be submitted to the laboratory
of the Department of Health or a labora-
tory approved by the department , for the
purpose of having made a serological test
for syphilis . The test shall be of a type
approved by the Department of Health .
If the laboratory test is made by the lab-
oratory of the Department of Health , it
shall be made without charge .

214.170 [ 2062b - 6 ] Person taking to
identify blood specimen ; reports of tests .
Every physician or other person who takes
or causes to be taken from a woman in
pregnancy , or suspected pregnancy ,
blood specimen for serological test for
syphilis , shall identify the specimen as be-
ing from a pregnant woman when sub-
mitting it for test . The laboratory shall
report the result of the test in triplicate
on forms prepared and furnished by the
Department of Health . The original re-
port shall be sent the person submitting
the specimen , the duplicate shall be for-
warded to the Department of Health not
later than Saturday of the week in which
the examination is made , and the triplicate
shall be retained by the laboratory for its
files .
214.180 [ 2062b-7 ] Birth certificate ;
statement as to blood test . Every physi-
cian or other person required to report
births and stillbirths shall state on each
birth certificate or stillbirth certificate
whether a blood test for syphilis was made
during pregnancy upon a specimen of
blood taken from the woman who bore the
child for which the certificate is filed . If
such a test was made , the person shall
state the name of the test used , the date
made and the name of the laboratory mak-
ing the test . If no test was made , the per-
son shall state the reason why the test
was not made . In no event shall the re-
sult of the test be stated on the birth or
stillbirth certificate .

214.230 [2635c - 1 ; 2635c -8 ] Advertise-
ment of appliances forbidden . No person
shall display or expose for sale any of the
articles described in KRS 214.190 , or any

vertising them . It shall be unlawful to

Advertisements relating to sexual diseases pro-

214.240 [ 2635c -5 ; 2635c - 6 ] Restrictions
as to methods of sale . No article of the
class specified in KRS 214.190 shall be sold ,
offered for sale or given away through
the medium of any vending machine , or
by any house to house or street solicita-
tion. No such article shall be sold at
wholesale or at retail unless the manufac-
turer is identified by name and address
on the appliance and on the container in
which the appliance is sold or is intended
to be sold .
214.250 [ 2635c - 6 ; 2635c -7 ] Appliances
to meet certain requirements . No article
of the class specified in KRS 214.190 shall
be sold in this state unless it complies with
the standards as to grade and quality pre-
scribed by the State Board of Pharmacy .
No rubber appliance shall be sold or other-
wise disposed of , or become the subject of
a license , unless it is capable of enduring
inflation with one cubic foot of air and is
free from holes , imperfect rings or blisters .
No animal membrane appliance shall be
sold or otherwise disposed of, or become
the subject of a license , unless it is un-
patched, free from holes , free from any
foreign substance that may be used as a
filler for concealing imperfections or dis-
colorations and is at least nine inches in
length .
214.260 [ 2635c - 7 ; 2635c - 13 ] Rules for
enforcement of appliance law . The State
Board of Pharmacy shall prepare , print
and distribute rules and regulations , not
inconsistent with law , for the conduct of
proceedings for the issuance , enforcement
and revocation of licenses , and may
adopt rules and regulations to enforce the
provisions of KRS 214.190 to 214.270 .
Administrative regulations not effective until
thirty days after filing with Legislative Re-
search Commission , KRS 13.085
214.270 [ 2635c - 10 ] Confiscation of ap-
pliances . Any person authorized to en-
force the terms of KRS 214.190 to 214.260
shall confiscate all merchandise in the
hands of a licensee or violator at the time
of the arrest, and the merchandise shall
be impounded until the outcome of the
trial of the licensee or violator . If the
licensee or violator is found guilty , the
merchandise shall be destroyed by the
State Board of Pharmacy . If he is found
not guilty , it shall be returned to him .
214.280 [2062d-1 ] Regulation of mat-
tresses ; " mattress " defined . As used in
KRS 214.290 to 214.310 , “ mattress ” means
any mattress , mattress pad or cushion ,
stuffed or filled with cotton , wool , hair ,
upholstered spring , or other soft material ,
to be used on a couch or other bed for
sleeping or reclining purposes .
214.290 [2062d-2 ] Materials used in
mattresses ; regulation of . No person shall
employ or use , in the making , remaking or
renovating of any mattress , any material
that has been used in or has formed a part
of any mattress used in or about any pub-
lic or private hospital , or any institution
for the treatment of persons suffering
from disease , or for or about any person
having any infectious or contagious dis-
ease , or any material of which prior use
has been made , unless all of said material
has been thoroughly sterilized and disin-
fected by a reasonable process approved
by the board of health of the city where
the mattress is made , remade or renovated .
The use of shoddy made from second-
hand materials , jute , old comforts , pads
or mattresses in the manufacture of new
mattresses to be offered for sale as new
mattresses is prohibited .
214.300 [ 2062d-3 ; 2062d -4 ; 2062d -5 ;
2062d - 6 ] Labels on mattresses ; regulation
of . ( 1 ) There shall be securely sewed
upon the outside of each mattress manu-
factured , or offered for sale , a muslin ,
paper or linen label or tag , upon which
shall be legibly written or printed a de

scription of the material used as the filling city or within a distance of one mile of its
of the mattress .
(2 ) If all the material used in the manu-
facture of the mattress has not been pre-
viously used , the words " manufactured
of new material " shall appear upon the
label or tag , together with the name and

( 3) Any mattress made from any ma-
terial previously used shall have stamped

(4) If labeled felt or felted cotton , it

has all been carded in layers or sheets by

(5 ) In the description upon the label or
tag of the material used as the filling of
any mattress , no term or designation likely
to mislead shall be used .
(6) No person except a purchaser at
retail shall remove , deface or alter , or
attempt or cause the removal , defacement
or alteration of any mark or statement
placed upon any mattress under the pro-
visions of this section .
214.310 [ 2062d-7 ] Enforcement of mat-
tress law . ( 1 ) Any police officer or mem-
ber of any municipal board of health , or
other city official, who has reason to be-
lieve that the provisions of KRS 214.280
to 214.300 have been or are being violated,
shall give notice to the State Department
of Health .
(2) Any individual who has reason to
believe that the provisions of KRS 214.280
to 214.300 have been or are being violated
may present the relevant facts to the
board of health or any of its deputies . It
shall then be the duty of the board of
health to make an investigation of the
facts , and if the board is of the opinion
that there is or has been a violation it shall
prosecute the person guilty thereof.
(3) Any individual may institute pro-
ceedings to enforce KRS 214.280 to 214.300
boundary .
CONTROL OF COMMUNICABLE
TUBERCULOSIS
214.330 Declaration of legislative intent
relating to control of communicable
tuberculosis . In enacting KRS 214.330 to
214.370 and subsections ( 13 ) and (14 ) of
KRS 214.990 , it is the intention of the Gen-
eral Assembly to provide for the effective
control of the spread of communicable
tuberculosis . The provisions of KRS 214.330
to 214.370 and subsections ( 13) and (14 ) of
KRS 214.990 are to be interpreted as recog-
nizing that communicable tuberculosis is a
disease dangerous to public health . (1954 ,
c . 223 , § 1 ; effective June 17, 1954)
214.340 Definitions for KRS 214.330 to
214.370 and subsections (13) and ( 14) of
KRS 214.990 . As used in KRS 214.330 to
214.370 and subsections (13) and ( 14 ) of
KRS 214.990 :
(1) " Health Officer " means the health
officer or health administrator of any local
health department ;
(2 ) " Communicable tuberculosis ” means
tuberculosis which is capable of being
transmitted from one human being to
another . ( 1954 , c . 223 , § 2 ; effective June
17 , 1954)
214.350 Duties of persons having com-
municable tuberculosis . Any person who
has communicable tuberculosis shall :
(1) Avoid close contact with babies and
small children ;
(2 ) Cover his nose and mouth with dis-
posable tissue paper while coughing or
sneezing and destroy the tissue paper by
burning it or by causing it to be burned ;
(3) Collect all sputum in a sputum con-
tainer or in some other manner approved
by the State Board of Health and burn

214.990 [ 2049 ; 2055a ; 2056 ; 2062b -3 ;
2062b - 8 ; 2062d- 9 ; 2635c - 12 ; 3909 ; 4615 ;
G.S. , Ch . 102 , Art . II, § 8 ] Penalties . ( 1 )
Every head of a family who willfully fails
or refuses and every physician who fails
or refuses to comply with KRS 214.010
shall be fined not less than ten dollars nor
more than one hundred dollars for each
day he neglects or refuses to report . Re-
peated failure to report is sufficient cause
for the revocation of a physician's certifi-
cate to practice medicine in this state .
(2) Any owner or person having
charge of any public or private convey-
ance , including watercraft , who refuses to
obey the rules and regulations made by
the State Board of Health under subsec-
tions ( 1) and (2 ) of KRS 214.020 shall ,
for each offense , be fined not less than
fifty dollars nor more than five hundred
dollars , or imprisoned in the county jail
for not less than ten nor more than sixty
days , or both .
(3 ) Any person who violates any of
the provisions of subsection ( 5 ) of KRS
214.020 shall be fined not less than fifty
dollars nor more than five hundred dol-
lars , or imprisoned not less than ten nor
more than sixty days , or both .
(4) Any person who fails or refuses to
comply with any of the provisions of
KRS 214.040 to 214.060 shall be fined not
less than five dollars nor more than twenty
dollars for each failure or refusal .
(5) Any justice of the peace , member
of a city legislative body , physician , or
superintendent of a charitable or correc-
tional institution who fails or refuses to
perform the duties required of him by
KRS 214.070 to 214.090 shall be fined not
less than twenty dollars nor more than
fifty dollars for each failure or refusal .
(6 ) Any person who violates any of
the provisions of KRS 214.110 shall be
fined ten dollars .
(7) Any person who fails to comply
with any of the provisions of subsections
(1) and ( 2 ) of KRS 214.150 shall be fined
not more than one hundred dollars .
(8 ) Persistent failure or refusal of any
physician , midwife or nurse to comply
with any of the provisions of KRS 214.150
constitutes proper grounds for the revoca-
tion of the right of such person to practice
his profession .
( 9 ) Any physician or other person
legally permitted to engage in attendance
upon a pregnant woman during pregnancy
or at delivery who fails to exercise due
diligence in complying with KRS 214.160
to 214.180 shall be fined not less than five
dollars nor more than ten dollars .
( 10) Any person who violates any of
the provisions of KRS 214.190 to 214.270
shall be fined not less than one hundred
dollars or imprisoned in the county jail
for not more than ninety days , or both .
(11) Any person who violates any of
the provisions of KRS 214.280 to 214.310
shall be fined not less than twenty dollars
nor more than one hundred dollars for
each offense , or imprisoned in the county
jail for not less than three months nor
more than six months , or both .
( 12) Any officer of a city or other per-
son who violates any of the provisions of
KRS 214.320 shall be fined not less than
63

§ 214.990 COMMUNICABLE DISEASES
five hundred dollars nor more than one
thousand dollars , and be liable in damages
to any person injured thereby ; if will
fully done , the injured person or his heirs
or representatives may recover punitive
damages .
( 13 ) Any person who violates the pro-
visions of KRS 214.350 shall be guilty of
a misdemeanor and shall be punished by
a fine not exceeding five hundred dollars
or by imprisonment not exceeding six
months , or both ; provided , however , that
if, in the opinion of the health officer suit-
able local facilities are available for the
confinement of persons having communi-
cable tuberculosis , such person so con-
victed shall be committed to said local
facilities for the service of any imprison-
ment adjudged by the court , unless sooner
released by the health officer , when , in
his opinion , such person may be dis-
charged without danger to the health of
other persons .
(14) Whenever , in the opinion of the
health officer , suitable local facilities are
not available for the confinement of per-
sons having communicable tuberculosis ,
such person shall be committed to such
facilities as may be established or con-
tracted for by the State Department of
Health for this purpose .
( 15 ) Any person who violates any pro-
vision of KRS 214.034 shall be fined not
less than ten dollars nor more than one
hundred dollars , or imprisoned for not
less than ten days nor more than sixty
days , or both . (Subsections (13) and ( 14 )
added , 1954, c. 223 , § 5 ; subsection ( 15 )
added by 1962 , с . 95 , § 5)

215.006 State Tuberculosis Hospital
Commission ; appointment ; terms ; chair-
man ; meetings ; per diem and expenses .
(1 ) The commission shall consist of five
members . The Commissioner of Health
shall be ex officio a member and the other
four members shall be appointed by the
Governor . The appointed members shall
be appointed for terms of four years . Any
vacancy shall be filled by appointment by
the Governor for the unexpired term .
(2 ) The commission shall annually elect
a chairman from its membership , for a
term of one year . The commission shall
meet regularly and at the call of the
chairman , or at the call of the Governor,
or upon written request of two or more
commission members , at such time and
place as is designated in the call .
(3) The members of the commission ,
other than the Commissioner of Health ,
shall receive a per diem of ten dollars for
each meeting attended and

215.014 Treatment of pulmonary fungus
diseases in state hospitals . The commis-
sion is authorized to accept and treat pa-
tients having histoplasmosis or other pul-
monary fungus diseases in the state tuber-
culosis hospitals insofar as permitted by
available facilities not fully utilized in
the treatment of tuberculosis patients .

tuberculosis sanatorium in Jefferson
County (Hazelwood) . Repealed , 1948 , с .

215.061 Division of state into tuber-
culosis hospital districts . The commission
shall divide the state into tuberculosis
hospital districts . The commission shall
have authority , with the approval of the
Governor , to transfer counties from one
district to another when necessary in
order to utilize the hospital facilities effi-
ciently . (1954, c . 151, § 6 ; 1960 , c . 22 ;
effective June 16, 1960)
215.062 Executive director and other
personnel . ( 1 ) The commission shall em-
ploy and fix compensation of an execu-
tive director for the hospitals . The execu-
tive director shall make his headquarters
in Frankfort and shall be authorized to
employ sufficient staff to permit sound ad-
ministration of the hospitals . He shall
be responsible to the commission for the
total administration of the hospitals, with-
in policies established by the commission ,
and for other such duties as may be pre-
scribed by the commission . The executive
director shall have such qualifications as
may be prescribed by the commission and
the Division of Personnel Efficiency of the
Department of Finance .
(2) The commission shall employ and
fix the compensation of such qualified pro-
fessional , technical and administrative
personnel as it deems necessary for the
operation of the hospitals and may regu-
late the terms of service and types of
duties .
( 3 ) Physicians serving in executive ca-
pacities shall be graduates from accredited
schools of medicine ; have full licenses to
practice medicine in the state of Ken-
tucky , have had at least five years of clin-
ical experience in the field of tuberculosis
and be members , in good standing , of the
Kentucky State Medical Association .
(4) Physicians not serving in executive
capacities shall have licenses to practice
medicine in Kentucky .
(5 ) Other professional, technical and
administrative personnel serving in execu-
tive capacities shall have such profes-
sional qualifications as may be prescribed
by the commission and the Division of
Personnel Efficiency of the Department
of Finance . ( 1954 , с . 151 , §§ 7 , 8 ; effec-
tive June 17 , 1954)
215.063 Allocation of beds ; rates for
hospitalization . The commission shall pre-
scribe the method of allocation of beds
and in so doing shall make available for
medically indigent patients at least one-
half of the accommodations . Subject to
the approval of the Governor, the com-
mission shall fix rates for hospitalization .
(1954, с . 151, § 9 ; effective June 17 , 1954)
215.064 Admission of patients for treat-
ment . ( 1 ) Subject to the provisions of
KRS 215.063 and 215.066 , subsection ( 2)
of this section , and to such rules and reg-
ulations as the commission may prescribe ,
any medically indigent patient or paying
patient who is a resident of the hospital

with the certification as to economic con-

(2 ) Medically indigent patients and

provisions of KRS 215.063 , the commis-
sion shall establish waiting lists for each
hospital whenever the number of appli-

215.067 Transfer of patients . The com-
mission shall have authority to transfer
patients , whether medically indigent pa-
tients or paying patients from one hos
pital to another when such transfer better
serves the needs of the patients . ( 1954,
c . 151, § 12 ; effective June 17, 1954)
215.068 Cost of transporting patients .
The cost of transporting paying patients
from the place of their residence to any
of the hospitals shall be paid by themselves
or by their friends or relatives . The cost
of transporting medically indigent pa-
tients from the place of their residence to
the hospital for hospitalization , for out-
patient care or from one hospital to an-
other shall be paid by the fiscal court of
the county of which they are resident.
( 1954 , c. 151, § 13 ; effective June 17, 1954)
215.069 Continued drug therapy for
discharged indigent patients . ( 1 ) Every
citizen and resident of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky who has been discharged
from a tuberculosis hospital within the
Commonwealth of Kentucky who is rec-
ommended for continued drug therapy by
the hospital director , and who , upon a
showing to the commissioner of the State
Board of Health that he or she cannot pay
for such drug therapy, shall be granted
a certificate by the State Board of Health
which will entitle said person to secure
all drugs needed for the treatment of
tuberculosis without charge to such per-
son .
(2) The cost of such drugs shall be
paid by the State Board of Health upon
presentment of the verified bill by the
person dispensing said drugs . ( 1958 , с .
114, § 1 ; effective June 19, 1958 )
