215.080 [ 2061a - 3 ] Tuberculosis sanato-
rium districts authorized. Sanatoria for
the treatment of tuberculosis may be
68

TUBERCULOSIS HOSPITALS § 215.120
erected and maintained in and by districts
in this state in the manner prescribed in
KRS 215.090 to 215.350 . A district for
the erection and maintenance of a tuber-
culosis sanatorium may consist of one
county or more than one county .
215.090 [ 2061a -3 ] Establishment of san-
atorium district for one county by fiscal
court ; appropriation . The fiscal court of
any county may by resolution declare that
the county shall be a district for the erec-
tion and maintenance of a sanatorium for
the treatment of tuberculosis . Upon the
resolution being passed , the fiscal court
shall appropriate out of the funds of the
county a sufficient amount for the erection
of the sanatorium . If there are not suffi-
cient funds on hand for such appropria-
tion at the time the resolution is adopted ,
the fiscal court may , at the next succeed-
ing county levy , make such levy as will be
sufficient to produce the necessary amount ,
and shall further make a levy for the
maintenance of the sanatorium . The fis-
cal court shall annually thereafter make
a levy of sufficient amount of tax to pay
the annual expenses of maintenance of the
sanatorium .
Mandatory levy of county tax for maintenance
of tubercular institution, KRS 68.090
215.100 [ 2061a - 3 ] Establishment for
several counties by fiscal courts ; appro-
priation ; apportionment of expense . ( 1 )
The fiscal courts of two or more counties
may by resolution duly passed by each
court unite the counties into a district for
the purpose of establishing therein a sana-
torium for the treatment of tuberculosis .
At the time of passing the resolution , each
court shall provide an appropriation for
the proportion of the cost of erection of
the sanatorium to be paid by its county .
If the funds are not on hand for that
purpose , the fiscal court shall , at its next
regular meeting at which a county levy is
made , levy a tax for that purpose , and
also levy a tax for the payment of the
proportionate part of the maintenance of
the sanatorium to be paid by its county .
Each fiscal court shall thereafter make an
annual levy sufficient to pay the propor-
tion of the cost of maintenance of the
sanatorium to be paid by its county .
(2 ) Where two or more counties unite
to form a district , the cost of erecting the
sanatorium and the cost of all betterments
and additions thereto shall be paid by the
counties comprising the district in pro-
portion to the taxable property of each
county as shown by their respective
county assessments . The annual expense
of maintaining the sanatorium and the
care and treatment of the patients therein
shall be borne by each county reasonably
and equitably in amounts ascertained un-
der rules and regulations of the State
Board of Health .
Mandatory levy of county tax for maintenance
of tubercular institution , KRS 68.090
215.110 [2061a-4 ] Establishment by
election . If the fiscal court of any county
fails to establish a tuberculosis sanato-
rium district as authorized in KRS 215.090
or 215.100, the citizens of the county or of
two or more counties may have such
county or counties established as a tuber-
culosis sanatorium district in accordance
with the provisions of KRS 215.120 or
215.130 .
215.120 [2061a -4 ) Election in one
county . A number of citizens equivalent
to ten percent of the votes cast at the last
general election in any county the fiscal
court of which has failed to establish a
sanatorium district may file their petition
with the county judge asking that the
proposition of establishing the county as
a district for the erection of a sanatorium
for the treatment of tuberculosis be sub-
mitted to the voters of the county at the
next general election held in the county
that does not occur within less than thirty
days after the filing of the petition . Each
voter signing the petition shall state his
full name and address . At the time of
filing the petition the petitioners shall
deposit with the county judge a sufficient
sum of money to pay the cost of publica-
tion , as required by this section . Upon
the filing of the petition with the county
judge he shall enter an order directing
that the petition be published in full in
the newspaper having the largest circula-
tion in the county at least once a week for
four consecutive weeks next preceding the
general election , and shall further enter
an order directing the clerk of the county
court to have placed upon the ballot at
such election the question , " Are you in
favor of establishing a tuberculosis sana-
torium district ? " , with proper squares in
which the voter may indicate " Yes " or
" No. " If the majority of those voting on
the proposition vote " Yes , " a tuberculo
69

§ 215.130 TUBERCULOSIS HOSPITALS
sis sanatorium district shall be estab-
lished . The vote shall be canvassed and
returned by the board of election com.
missioners for the general election . If the
election is contested the members of the
fiscal court of the county shall be made
defendants .
Contest of election on public questions , KRS
122.140
215.130 [ 2061a -4 ] Election in several
counties . When the citizens of two or
more counties desire to have such counties
established as a district for the erection of
a sanatorium for the treatment of tuber-
culosis , and the fiscal courts have failed to
establish the district , a number of citizens
equivalent to ten percent of the votes cast
at the last general election of each county
shall file a petition in the county of their
residence asking that the district be estab-
lished and naming the several counties to
be united in the district . The petition
shall be filed in each county of the pro-
posed district , accompanied by the deposit
required by KRS 215.120 and the method
of proceeding shall be the same as pro-
vided in KRS 215.120 , except that the
same proceeding shall be taken in each
county for the general election to be held
at the same time in each county. If the
result of the election in any one of the
counties in the proposed district is against
the proposition , the district cannot be es-
tablished .
215.140 [2061a -5 ] County may join es-
tablished district . (1) A county may join
an already established tuberculosis sana-
torium district if the consent of the dis-
trict board of trustees of the already
established district is first secured .
(2) If the fiscal court of the county de-
siring to join has declared by resolution
that the county shall be a tuberculosis
sanatorium district or a part of a district ,
the application for consent shall be made
by the fiscal court .
(3 ) If the county desiring to join has
become a tuberculosis sanatorium district
or a part of a district by action of the
voters , the application for consent shall
be made by the board of trustees of that
district .
(4) If the county desiring to join has
not become a district or a part of a dis-
trict , the application for consent shall be
made by a petition signed by not less than
twenty qualified voters of the county.
When it is necessary to take a vote to de-
clare any county a tuberculosis sanato-
rium district , to enable it to join an al-
ready established sanatorium district , the
ballot shall read as follows : " Are you in
favor of declaring this county a tubercu-
losis sanatorium district , for the purpose
of joining the already established tuber-
culosis sanatorium district of
County ( or Counties ) ? " The result of
the election shall be certified to the fiscal
court of the county in which the election
was held , and if the result is favorable to
the proposition submitted the fiscal court
shall immediately declare the county a
portion of the already established tuber-
culosis district and certify its action to
the State Board of Health , whereupon that
board shall determine the number of trus-
tees to compose the board of the district,
and the representation to be accorded
each county on the district board , accord-
ing to the provisions of KRS 215.160 .
215.150 [ 2061a - 6 ] Establishment and
maintenance of district upon certification
of favorable election . The result of any
county or district election with respect to
establishing a sanatorium district shall be
certified to the fiscal court of the county
or counties composing the district to be
established , and the fiscal court or courts
shall, if the result of the election is certi-
fied as in favor of the establishment of the
district , forthwith proceed to declare the
county or counties a district for the es-
tablishment of a sanatorium for the treat-
ment of tuberculosis , and shall proceed to
put the district into effect in the same
manner as they are authorized to do upon
their own initiative . The cost and ex-
penses of erection and maintenance shall
follow the provisions of KRS 215.090 or
215.100 , as the case may be .
Mandatory levy of county tax for maintenance
of tubercular institution , KRS 68.090
215.160 [2061a-7 ] District board of
trustees ; how appointed ; membership ;
qualifications . (1) Upon the creation of a
tuberculosis district , the fiscal court of
each county in the district shall at once
notify the State Board of Health of the
establishment of the district . The State
Board of Health shall then recommend to
the county judge of each county in the
district the appointment of suitable per-
sons to the district board of trustees .
Where the district consists of one county
70

TUBERCULOSIS HOSPITALS § 215.170
the county judge shall appoint seven per-
sons as members of the district board of
trustees . Where the district consists of
several counties the district board of trus-
tees shall consist of not less than two nor
more than four persons from each county,
the total membership not to consist of less
than seven persons . Where a county in a
district composed of more than one county
has a population in excess of twenty
thousand , the county shall be allowed a
trustee for each ten thousand of popula-
tion in excess of twenty thousand popula-
tion, but not to exceed four trustees in all .
The membership of each board shall in-
clude both men and women , and at least
one member shall be a registered physi-
cian . The State Board of Health in rec-
ommending persons to the county judge
for appointment to the district board of
trustees shall recommend twice as many
persons for each county as the county is
entitled to have trustees appointed , and
the county judge shall immediately make
his selection from those recommended .
Where a county has joined an already es-
tablished district the State Board of
Health shall recommend to the county
judge of each county included in the new
district twice as many persons for ap-
pointment to the district board of trustees
as the county is entitled to have ap-
pointed , and the county judge shall select
the trustees for his county from this list .
The terms of the trustees of the counties
composing the previously existing district
shall expire immediately upon the organi-
zation of the new board .
(2) In any tuberculosis district contain-
ing a city of the second class two persons
shall be appointed trustees on the district
board by the mayor of that city , and in a
tuberculosis district containing more than
one city of the second class one person
shall be appointed trustee on the district
board by the mayor of each city . The
mayor shall make appointments from a
list submitted by the State Board of
Health containing the names of twice as
many persons as the mayor is entitled to
appoint . The number of trustees ap-
pointed by the mayor shall be in addition
to the number allotted for appointment
by the county judge .
(3 ) When any tuberculosis sanatorium
district has received or may hereafter re-
ceive any gift, grant or devise of money
or property of the value of $ 100,000 or
more on the condition, agreement or un-
derstanding that the donor or any person
designated by him shall have the right to
designate not exceeding two additional
members of the board of trustees of the
district , the district board may , by resolu-
tion , increase the number of trustees com-
posing the board by one or two members ,
as the case may be , over the number pro-
vided for by subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) of
this section . The additional trustees shall
be appointed by the county judge , or if
the district is composed of more than one
county , by the county judge of the county
in which the sanatorium is located or
proposed to be located . The county judge
shall appoint the person or persons desig-
nated by the donor or by the person
designated by him .
215.170 [ 2061a - 7 ; 2061a - 8 ] Term of of-
fice of board members ; vacancies . ( 1 )
One -third of the persons first appointed to
the district board of trustees by the
county judge on recommendation of the
State Board of Health shall serve for a
term of two years , one -third for a term
of three years , and one -third for a term
of four years . Where the board consists
of a number that cannot be divided by
three , the number in excess of one -third
of the next even number into which the
board can be divided shall be appointed
for a term of four years . Thereafter , as
their terms expire, their successors shall
be recommended and appointed in the
same manner , but for a term of four years
each . The trustees shall hold office until
their respective successors are appointed
and qualified.
( 2 ) Trustees appointed by a mayor un-
der subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 215.160 shall
hold office for a term of four years and
until their successors are appointed and
qualified .
(3) If one additional trustee is ap-
pointed under subsection ( 3 ) of KRS
215.160, he shall hold office until the next
expiration of office occurs in the board .
If two additional trustees are appointed
under that subsection , one shall be ap-
pointed to hold office until the next ex-
piration of office occurs in the board , and
the other until the second expiration of
office occurs in the board . Thereafter
their successors shall be appointed in the
same manner , but for a term of four years
71

§ 215.180 TUBERCULOSIS HOSPITALS
and until their successors are appointed shall have general control of the property
and qualified .
(4) Any vacancy occurring in the terms
of office of trustees appointed by a county
judge or a mayor on the recommendation
of the State Board of Health shall be filled
for the unexpired term by the county
judge or mayor , as the case may be , by
appointment on recommendation of the
State Board of Health of two names from
the county in which the vacancy occurred .
Any vacancy occurring in the office of a
trustee appointed under subsection ( 3 ) of
KRS 215.160 shall be filled for the unex-
pired term by the county judge by ap-
pointment on the designation of the donor
or the person designated by him .
215.180 [ 2061a -9 ] Organization of
board ; officers ; quorum . Each district
board of trustees shall be a body corpo-
rate under the name and style of " District
Board of Tuberculosis Sanatorium Trus-
tees for County ( or Coun-
ties) ." The trustees shall , as soon as
......
possible after their appointment and qual-
ification , adopt a seal and organize by
electing a president , a secretary and a
treasurer , each to serve for two years and
until his successor is elected and qualified .
The same person may be elected to serve
both as secretary and treasurer and need
not be a member of the board of trustees .
The treasurer shall give bond to the Com-
monwealth for the faithful performance
of his duties , in such sum and form and
with such sureties as the district board of
trustees shall approve . The treasurer may
be removed at any time , in the discretion
of the district board of trustees , and a
successor appointed . A majority of the
board of trustees shall constitute a quo-
rum .
215.190 [2061a-13 ] Trustees not com-
pensated ; expenses paid . The members of
the board of trustees shall not receive
compensation for their services , but shall
be reimbursed for their actual expenses
necessarily incurred in the performance
of their duties , upon vouchers duly ap-
proved by the board of trustees , signed by
the secretary and countersigned by the
president .
215.200 [ 2061a - 8 ; 2061a - 11 ; 2061a - 17 ;
2061a-20 ] Powers of board . ( 1 ) The gov-
ernment of the district sanatorium shall
be vested in the board of trustees , which
and affairs of the sanatorium and shall
take such action as is necessary to carry
out the purposes of KRS 215.080 to
215.350 .
(2) The district board of trustees may :
( a) Borrow money on the credit of the
board in anticipation of the revenue to be
derived from county and city taxes levied
for the tuberculosis sanatorium district
for the fiscal half -year in which the money
is borrowed , and to pledge the taxes levied
for the tuberculosis sanatorium district
for the payment of the principal and in-
terest of the loan. The interest paid shall
not exceed six percent per annum and the
principal shall not exceed fifty percent of
the anticipated revenue for the fiscal half-
year in which the money is borrowed .
(b ) Establish , operate and maintain , in
connection with the sanatorium or as a
part thereof , clinics , dispensaries , day
camps and summer camps ; provide for
visiting nurses ; promote and carry on
campaigns of education ; and use other
suitable and adequate means and methods
necessary and proper for the treatment ,
relief and prevention of tuberculosis . The
board may use the property , equipment
and supplies of the sanatorium for these
purposes , or it may cooperate with the
anti -tuberculosis leagues , medical socie-
ties and organizations that are engaged
in carrying on such work .
( c) Establish bylaws it deems neces-
sary and expedient to define the duties of
officers , assistants or employes , to fix the
conditions of admission to the sanatorium
and the support and discharge of patients ,
and to conduct in a proper manner the
professional and business affairs of the
sanatorium .
(d) Establish and enforce a suitable
system of rules and regulations for the in-
ternal government , discipline and man-
agement of the sanatorium .
(e) Provide by rule the character of ex-
amination to which any applicant for ad-
mission to the sanatorium shall submit for
the purpose of determining whether or not
the applicant is afflicted with tuberculosis .
215.210 [ 2061a - 10 ] Selection of site ; ap-
proval of site and plans by State Board
of Health . When a tuberculosis district
has been established and an appropriation
made or a tax levied for the construction
72

TUBERCULOSIS HOSPITALS § 215.270
of a sanatorium , the board of trustees
shall select a site for the sanatorium , but
before any site can be finally selected and
adopted it shall be approved by the State
Board of Health . The site selected by the
district board of trustees shall be in the
part of the district that in the judgment
of the board is best adapted to the wants
of the institution and most economical to
the district , regard being had to water
supply , drainage , facility of access , a
quality of soil suitable for farming pur-
poses , and the price asked for the land .
All plans and specifications for the erec-
tion of the sanatorium shall be submitted
to the State Board of Health for approval .
If approved , the district board of trustees
shall be authorized to proceed at once with
the erection. If not approved , the State
Board of Health shall make such recom-
mendations as to it seem best . If the rec-
ommendations are accepted by the board
of trustees , it shall proceed with the erec-
tion of the sanatorium in accordance with
the recommendations .
215.220 [ 2061a - 16 ] Visitation by trus-
tees ; reports by superintendent and offi-
cers ; annual meeting of board. ( 1 ) There
shall be a thorough visitation of the sana-
torium by two members of the board of
trustees monthly, and by the whole board
annually . On each of these occasions a
written report of the condition of the san-
atorium shall be submitted by the super-
intendent .
(2) On a day to be fixed by the bylaws ,
there shall be held each year the regular
annual meeting of the board , at which the
superintendent of the sanatorium and the
secretary and treasurer of the board shall
each submit a report of the affairs of the
sanatorium in the form prescribed by the
State Board of Health , and the secretary
and treasurer shall also submit a state-
ment of his accounts . The reports of the
superintendent , secretary and treasurer ,
and the latter's statement of accounts ,
shall be transmitted in duplicate by the
board , with their annual report , to the
State Board of Health .
215.230 [ 2061a - 12 ] Payment of taxes to
board of trustees . The fiscal court of each
county composing the district shall from
time to time , as taxes levied for the pur-
poses of the sanatorium are collected , ap-
propriate them to the use of the sana-
torium , and shall direct the county treas-
urer to pay the amount of the appropria-
tion to the secretary of the sanatorium ,
and to take the receipt of the secretary ,
countersigned by the president of the san-
atorium , as his voucher therefor .
Mandatory levy of county tax for maintenance
of tubercular institution , KRS 68.090
215.240 [2061a - 18 ] Trustees and em-
ployes not to be interested in sanatorium's
contracts . No member of the board of
trustees of the sanatorium , and no em-
ploye thereof , shall be interested in or
receive any benefit from any contract
made with the sanatorium .
215.250 [ 2061a -19 ] Duties of treasurer .
The treasurer of the board of trustees
shall :
(1) Have the custody of all money , se-
curities and obligations belonging to the
sanatorium , and shall disburse money
only for the uses and purposes of the sana-
torium and in the manner prescribed by
the bylaws on itemized vouchers allowed
by the board of trustees , signed by the sec-
retary and countersigned by the president .
(2 ) Keep a full and accurate account
of all receipts and payments in the man-
ner directed in the bylaws , and such other
accounts as the board of trustees pre-
scribe .
(3) Render statements of accounts of
the several books , funds and property in
his custody whenever required by the
board of trustees .
(4) Have all accounts and records fully
made up to the last day preceding the an-
nual meeting , and present the same to the
board of trustees at its annual meeting .
215.260 [2061a - 24 ] Records open to
Auditor. Every sanatorium district estab-
lished under KRS 215.080 to 215.350 shall
at all reasonable times keep open for the
inspection of the Auditor of Public Ac-
counts all of its records and books of ac-
counts .
215.270 [ 2061a-14 ] Medical superin-
tendent ; appointment ; qualifications ; re-
moval ; temporary superintendent . The
district board of trustees shall appoint a
medical superintendent of the sanatorium
who shall not be a member of the board.
The superintendent must be a legally
qualified physician who is a graduate in
medicine and surgery from a medical col-
lege approved by the State Board of
Health , of good standing and acknowl
73

§ 215.280 TUBERCULOSIS HOSPITALS
edged skill in his profession , and must
have had special training and experience
in a hospital or sanatorium for tuberculo-
sis . The superintendent shall be under
the general supervision of the board of
trustees , and may be removed by the board
at any time for cause upon written
charges preferred and after an opportu-
nity to appear and make defense . The
board may appoint a successor to the
superintendent , and may for good cause
employ some one to act temporarily as
superintendent who is not possessed of all
the above -mentioned qualifications , but
such temporary employment shall be for a
term not exceeding twelve months , and
such employe shall at no time be a member
of the board .
215.280 [ 2061a -15 ] Powers and duties
of medical superintendent ; assistants and
employes ; fees . ( 1 ) The medical super-
intendent shall :
( a) Be the chief executive officer of the
sanatorium .
(b) Have general superintendence of
the buildings , grounds , furniture , fixtures
and stock and the direction and control of
all persons therein , subject to the bylaws
and regulations prescribed by the district
board of trustees .
( c) Ascertain daily , in person or by rep-
resentative , the condition of each patient
and prescribe or direct his treatment .
(d) Cause full and fair records of all
his official acts and the entire business and
operation of the sanatorium to be kept
regularly from day to day, in the manner
and to the extent prescribed by the by-
laws , see that all the accounts and records
are fully made up , and present the same
to the board of trustees at its annual meet-
ing .
(e) Admit any member of the board of
trustees or any member or officer of the
State Board of Health at any time into
every part of the sanatorium , and exhibit
to him , on demand , all books , papers , ac-
counts and writings belonging to the san-
atorium or pertaining to its business man-
agement , discipline or government .
(f) Make at the time of reception of
patients a record of the date of admission ,
age , residence , occupation and such other
statistics in regard to every patient ad-
mitted to the sanatorium as the bylaws re-
quire .
(2) The medical superintendent may :
(a) Appoint , with the advice and con-
sent of the board of trustees , an assistant
physician or physicians , each of whom
shall be a legally qualified physician , a
graduate in medicine and surgery from
some medical college recognized as in good
standing by the State Board of Health
and of acknowledged skill in the medical
profession .
(b) Remove any assistant physician
with the consent of the board of trustees .
( c) Employ , supervise and remove at
will all employes at the sanatorium .
(3) All money collected by the medical
superintendent shall be immediately paid
over by him to the treasurer of the sana-
torium , and his receipt taken therefor . No
personal fees , charges or pecuniary com-
pensation of any kind shall be collected
by the medical superintendent or any em-
ploye of the sanatorium for services ren-
dered to a patient in the sanatorium.
215.285 Commission may elect coverage
under Workmen's Compensation law . The
State Tuberculosis Sanatoria Commission
may elect coverage under the Workmen's
Compensation laws for all employes under
its control and supervision . (1952 , c. 128 ,
§ 1 ; effective June 19 , 1952 )
215.290 [ 2061a - 20 ] Conditions of ad-
mission as free patient; number of pa-
tients . No person shall be received into a
district sanatorium as a free patient unless
the person has been a resident of this state
and of the district for at least twelve
months next preceding his application for
admission to the sanatorium , and unless
he files with his application for admission
a certificate of the county judge of the
county in which he is a resident stating
that from evidence submitted to the
county judge , the judge is of the opinion
that the applicant is unable to pay for
maintenance in the sanatorium .
greater number of persons shall be ad-
mitted to the sanatorium than can be prop-
erly taken care of and treated . Each
county of a district consisting of more
than one county shall have the right to
have admitted its proper and proportion-
ate number of free patients who are un-
able to pay their maintenance in the sana-
torium .
No

Note : The 1962 amendment to KRS 215.360
takes effect March 19 , 1962 .
215.370 [ 2061b - 2 ] Division of proceeds
of sale in a joint district . If any district
is composed of more than one county , the
net proceeds of sale under KRS 215.360 ,
after paying all court costs , debts and
claims , shall be divided between the
counties in the same proportion in which
they contributed towards the purchase of
the property and the construction of the
sanatorium .
215.380 [2061b-3 ] Dissolution of district
on sale of property . In case a sale of all
its property is decreed and made as pro-
vided in KRS 215.360 , the sanatorium dis-
trict in which the property is located shall
be thereby discontinued .
215.390 [ 2061a -4a ; 2061a - 23 ] County
may contract for care of its patients in
sanatorium of another district ; diversion
of funds collected for sanatorium . ( 1)
The fiscal court of any county, in lieu of
providing for the erection of a district
sanatorium for tuberculosis , may contract
with the district board of trustees of any
other district where a sanatorium has been
constructed , for the care and treatment
of the residents of its county who are suf-
fering from tuberculosis . The fiscal court
of the county in which the patients reside
shall pay to the sanatorium of the district
receiving the patients the actual cost in-
curred in their care , treatment and other
necessaries , the cost of their transporta-
tion , and such further sum as the board
of trustees may under proper rules and
regulations provide .
(2) When any county that has levied a
tax and collected funds from the levy for
the purpose of studying , treating and pre-
venting tuberculosis through the establish-
ment of a county sanatorium or otherwise
is not inclined or financially able to erect
the sanatorium , the fiscal court shall di-
vert any and all funds collected under the
provisions of the Acts of 1908 and 1918
for purposes described in this subsection
to the purchase of a county farm or poor
farm , and for the erection of proper and
necessary buildings thereon , and for the
support and maintenance of the county
farm or poor farm , wherein the unfortu-
nates of the county are kept and main-
tained on account of their inability to sup-
port themselves either from being afflicted
with tuberculosis or otherwise .

HOSPITALS , CLINICS AND PERSONAL CARE HOMES § 216.080
216.020 [ 938e- 2 ] Election on bond issue
for hospital . In order to provide money
for the construction and furnishing of the
county hospital , the fiscal court may enter
an order submitting to the voters , at the
regular November election , the question
of whether bonds of the county shall be
issued for this purpose . The order shall
provide the date and maturity of the
bonds , the rate of interest they shall bear ,
and the total amount to be issued , and
shall also contain the necessary details in
reference to the execution and delivery of
the bonds , their denominations , coupons
to be annexed , and a sinking fund to re-
tire the bonds at maturity .
Issuance of Bonds and Control of Funds , Ch. 66
216.030 [ 938e - 3 ] Fiscal court to sell
bonds and expend proceeds . If the voters
of the county determine that bonds shall
be issued , the bonds shall , when issued ,
be placed under the control of the fiscal
court of the county , which shall determine
when , at what price and how they shall
be sold, but the bonds shall not be sold
for less than par . When the bonds are
sold , their proceeds shall be placed to the
credit of the county in the county deposi-
tories , and the expenditure of the money
in the construction and equipment of the
hospital shall be under the supervision and
control of the fiscal court .
216.040 [ 938e - 5 ] Control of hospital ;
fees ; nursing instruction . The county
hospital shall be under the control of the
proper county authorities . The fees to be
charged patients for attention given and
such other fees or charges as may be de-
manded shall be fixed , regulated and con-
trolled by the governing authority of the
hospital , and shall be applied to the main-
tenance fund of the hospital . The hos-
pital may provide for instruction and
training of nurses , and issue diplomas to
pupils who have completed courses pre-
scribed by rules and regulations adopted
in connection therewith .
Registered Nurses ; Practical Nurses , Ch. 314
216.050 [ 938e - 6 ] County shall levy tax
for maintenance of hospital . The fiscal
court shall levy a tax sufficient to main-
tain the hospital after its erection and
equipment and to pay the current ex-
penses and necessary repairs and addi-
tions thereto , and to pay the interest on
the bonded indebtedness and establish a
sinking fund for retirement of the bonds
at maturity . The sum derived from the
tax shall be applied to the purposes pro-
vided in this section .
216.060 [ 1840e - 1 ] Clinic may be main-
tained by county containing city of sec-
ond class . The fiscal court of a county
containing a city of the second class may
establish and maintain a clinic for the use
and benefit of the indigent school children
of the county and may levy and collect a
tax of not more than two cents on the
one hundred dollars for this purpose . The
clinic shall be managed and conducted by
a board of trustees consisting of ten mem-
bers appointed by the county judge . The
trustees shall serve for a term of four
years and without compensation .
216.070 [ 913-1 ] County may contract
with city , county, city -county board of
health or private hospital for hospitaliza-
tion of indigent residents ; reimbursement
for emergency care . ( 1) Any county of
this state may , for the hospitalization of
its indigent residents , at the expense of
the county, enter into a contract with any
other city , county, or city -county board of
health in this state that owns and operates
its own hospital , or may enter into such
contract with any privately - owned hos-
pital in any county of this state .
(2 ) Whether or not any county has en-
tered into a contract provided for in sub-
section ( 1) of this section , any county of
this state whose indigent residents have
been furnished medicine , medical care , or
hospitalization on an emergency basis by
any city - owned hospital or hospital estab-
lished and operated under the provisions
of KRS 212.350 to 212.630 may reimburse
such hospital for said services . ( 1956 , с .
119 ; effective May 18, 1956 )
216.080 [ 3235i - 1 ] Cities of second to
fifth classes may establish hospital . Any
city of the second , third , fourth or fifth
class may , under the provisions of KRS
216.240 , purchase , establish , erect , acquire ,
maintain and operate a municipal hospital
and necessary appurtenances thereto
within or without the corporate limits of
the city , for the purpose of supplying the
city and its inhabitants with modern and
sanitary hospital facilities . ( 1946 , c . 169 ,
§ 17 )
Note : The title to 1946 , c. 169 , which amended
KRS 216.080 to 216.100 , and 216.240 , purported
to cover both municipal hospitals and county
buildings , which may raise a question as to
whether the title is in violation of Const . 51 .

§ 216.090 HOSPITALS , CLINICS AND PERSONAL CARE HOMES
City may issue revenue bonds for any public
project , KRS 58.010 to 58.120
Hospitals operated by governmental units exempt
from payment of fees and licensure but re-
quired to comply with provisions of KRS
216.400 to 216.510 , KRS 216.490
216.090 [ 3235i- 2 ] Ordinance for estab-
lishment of hospital ; election on . ( 1) Be-
fore any city of the second , third , fourth
or fifth class may purchase , establish ,
erect , acquire , maintain or operate a
municipal hospital , the legislative body of
the city shall pass an ordinance declaring
it desirable that this be done , and shall
prepare an estimate of the probable cost
of the hospital . If revenue bonds are to
be issued to pay the cost the ordinance
shall so provide . An election shall be had
on the adoption of the ordinance if a
number of legal voters of the city equal
to twenty -five percent of the total num-
ber of votes cast in the city at the last reg-
ular election file a petition within ten days
after the ordinance is passed asking that
the question of approval of the ordinance
be submitted to a vote of the people . If
the petition is filed the election shall be
held at the next regular election held in
the city . ( Subsection ( 1 ) amended , 1946 ,
c. 169 , § 18 )
(2) If an election is petitioned for , the
mayor shall certify the ordinance to the
county court clerk , who shall cause to be
printed on the ballots for use in the city
the question : “ Are you in favor of pur-
chasing , establishing , erecting , acquiring ,
maintaining and operating a municipal
hospital in accordance with the estimate
of cost adopted by the City of
(here insert name of city ), and the issu-
ance of revenue bonds in the amount of
$ ........ (here insert total face amount
of bonds estimated by the legislative body
of the city to be necessary to pay the cost
of the hospital , based upon the estimate of
cost ) ? " Opposite the question shall be
printed " Yes ” and “ No , ” with the proper
squares for stamping . The mayor shall
advertise the election and its object for at
least seven days before the date of the
election in the newspaper having the
largest circulation in the city , or by hand-
bills posted at not less than four con-
spicuous places in each voting precinct in
the city and at the door of the city hall .
All legal voters of the city may vote at the
election .
(3) If an election is held, the city shall
not purchase , establish , erect , acquire ,
maintain or operate a municipal hospital
or issue revenue bonds unless a majority
of all the qualified voters voting on the
question vote in favor thereof .
(4) Before revenue bonds are issued the
legislative body of the city shall select the
location of the municipal hospital , prepare
the necessary plans and specifications , and
take all steps necessary in its judgment
for the acquisition of the land , right of
ways , constructions , franchises and ease-
ments necessary for the construction of
the hospital .
216.100 [ 3235i- 3 ] Ordinance for bond
issue . Except where an election has been
held under KRS 216.090 and the proposi-
tion has been defeated , any city of the
second , third , fourth or fifth class may ,
by ordinance , borrow money and issue
negotiable bonds for the purpose of de-
fraying the cost of purchasing , establish-
ing , erecting and acquiring a municipal
hospital and necessary appurtenances
thereto . The ordinance shall specify the
proposed undertaking , the amount of
bonds to be issued , and the maximum rate
of interest the bonds are to bear . The
ordinance shall further provide that the
proposed hospital , with necessary ap-
purtenances thereto , is to be purchased ,
established , erected or acquired pursuant
to the provisions of KRS 216.080 to
216.240. ( 1946 , с . 169 , § 19 )
216.110 [ 3235i- 4 ] Interest rate and term
of bonds . All bonds issued under the
provisions of KRS 216.080 to 216.240 may
bear interest at a rate not exceeding six
percent per annum , payable semiannually ,
and shall be executed in such manner and
be payable at such times , not exceeding
thirty years from the date thereof , and at
such place as the legislative body shall
determine .
216.120 [ 3235i- 5 ] Negotiability of
bonds ; tax exemption ; signatures ; how
sold ; maximum yield ; not a city debt .
All bonds issued under KRS 216.080 to
216.240 shall be negotiable and shall not
be subject to taxation . If any officer
whose signature appears on the bonds or
coupons ceases to be such officer before
delivery of the bonds , the signature shall
nevertheless be valid and sufficient for all
purposes the same as if he had remained
in office until delivery . The bonds shall
80

HOSPITALS , CLINICS AND PERSONAL CARE HOMES § 216.180
�
be sold in such manner and upon such
terms as the legislative body of the city
deems for the best interest of the city , or
any contract for the purchase or acquisi-
tion of any municipal hospital may pro-
vide that payment thereof shall be made in
such bonds . In no event shall any bonds
be negotiated on a basis to yield more than
six percent . The bonds shall be payable
solely from the revenue derived from the
operation of the hospital as provided in
KRS 216.160 , and shall not constitute an
indebtedness of the city within the mean-
ing of the Constitution . It shall be plainly
stated on the face of each bond that it was
issued under the provisions of KRS 216.080
to 216.240 and does not constitute an in-
debtedness of the city within the meaning
of the Constitution .
216.130 [ 3235i - 6 ] Use of proceeds of
bonds . All money received from any
bonds issued under KRS 216.080 to 216.240
shall be applied solely to the purchase ,
establishment , erection or acquisition of
the municipal hospital and necessary ap-
purtenances thereto , except that the
money may be used also to advance the
payment of interest on the bonds during
the first three years following the date of
the bonds , and to advance the expense of
operation and maintenance for one month
after the opening of the hospital .
216.140 [ 32351-6 ; 3235i −7 ] Bondholders '
lien ; enforcement . There shall be a statu-
tory mortgage lien upon the municipal
hospital and appurtenances in favor of
the holders of the bonds and coupons . The
hospital and appurtenances shall remain
subject to the lien until payment in full
of the principal and interest of the bonds .
Any holder of the bonds or coupons may ,
by action either at law or in equity , pro-
tect and enforce the lien , and may by
proper proceedings enforce and compel
performance of all duties required by KRS
216.080 to 216.240 , including the making
and collecting of sufficient rates , the seg-
regation of the income and revenue , and
the application thereof .
216.150 [ 3235i- 8 ] Receiver in case of
default . If there is a default in the pay-
ment of the principal or interest of any
of the bonds issued under KRS 216.080 to
216.240 , any court having jurisdiction of
the action may appoint a receiver to ad-
minister the municipal hospital on behalf
of the city, with power to charge and col-
lect rates for the services the hospital
renders sufficient to provide for the pay-
ment of any bonds or obligations outstand-
ing against the hospital and for payment
of the operating expenses , and to apply the
income and revenue in conformity with
KRS 216.080 to 216.240 and the ordinance
referred to in KRS 216.160 .
216.160 [ 3235i-9 ] Sinking fund , opera-
tion and depreciation accounts to be pro-
vided . On or before the issuance of the
bonds the city legislative body shall , by
ordinance , set aside and pledge the income
of the municipal hospital into a separate
and special fund to be used and applied
in payment of the cost , maintenance , op-
eration and depreciation of the hospital .
The ordinance shall fix the amount of
revenue necessary to be set aside and ap-
plied to the payment of the principal and
interest of the bonds , and the proportion
of the balance of the income to be set
aside as a proper and adequate deprecia-
tion account . The remaining portion of
the balance shall be set aside for the rea-
sonable and proper operation and main-
tenance of the hospital . The rates to be
charged for the use of the hospital and
the services it renders shall be fixed and
revised from time to time so as to be suffi-
cient to provide for the payment of in-
terest upon all bonds and to create a sink-
ing fund to pay the principal thereof when
it becomes due , to provide for the opera-
tion and maintenance of the hospital , and
to provide an adequate depreciation ac-
count .
216.170 [ 3235i- 10 ] Use of surplus in op-
erating fund . If a surplus is accumulated
in the operating and maintenance fund
equal to the cost of maintaining and op-
erating the municipal hospital during the
remainder of the calendar, operating or
fiscal year, as may be provided by the or-
dinance required by KRS 216.160 , the city
legislative body may at any time transfer
the excess to the depreciation account to
be used for any improvements or addi-
tions to the hospital .
216.180 [ 3235i - 11 ] Use of depreciation
account . The funds accumulating to the
depreciation account shall be expended
in balancing depreciation on the munici-
pal hospital , or in making new construc-
tions or additions thereto . Any such ac
81

§ 216.190 HOSPITALS , CLINICS AND PERSONAL CARE HOMES
cumulations may be invested as the legis.
lative body may designate . The income
from such investments shall be carried
into the depreciation account .
216.190 [ 3235i-12 ] Refunding bonds .
The city may issue refunding bonds for
the purpose of providing funds for the
payment of any outstanding bonds , in ac-
cordance with the procedure prescribed
for the issuance of the original bonds . The
refunding bonds shall be secured to the
same extent and shall have the same
source of payment as the bonds that are
refunded .
216.200 [ 3235i- 13 ] Additional bonds for
establishment of hospital . If the city leg-
islative body finds that the bonds first au-
thorized will be insufficient to accomplish
the purpose desired , additional bonds may
be authorized and issued subject to the
same procedure .
216.210 [ 3235i- 14 ] Original issue may
include bonds for additions and improve-
ments . Any city acquiring a municipal
hospital pursuant to the provisions of KRS
216.000 to 216.240 may , at the time of issu-
ing bonds for such acquisition , provide
for additional bonds for additions and
permanent improvements to be placed in
escrow and to be negotiated from time to
time as proceeds for that purpose are nec-
Such bonds when so negotiated
shall have equal standing with the bonds
of the same issue .
essary .
216.220 [ 3235i-15 ] Subsequent issue of
bonds for additions and improvements .
Any city acquiring a municipal hospital
under the provisions of KRS 216.080 to
216.240 may provide for additions to and
improvements of the hospital by an addi-
tional issue of bonds in the same manner
as the original bonds .
216.230 [ 3235i- 16 ] City may provide for
administration of income . Any city ac-
quiring a municipal hospital under the
provisions of KRS 216.080 to 216.240 may
provide by ordinance such provisions and
stipulations for the administration of the
income and revenues and for the security
of the bondholders as the legislative body
of the city deems necessary .
216.240 [ 3235i- 17 ] Hospital commission
to operate hospital . ( 1 ) Any city of the
second , third , fourth or fifth class pur-
chasing , establishing , erecting or acquir-
ing a municipal hospital under the pro-
visions of KRS 216.080 to 216.230 shall by
ordinance provide for the appointment
of a commission of seven members to oper-
ate, manage and control the municipal
hospital , which commission shall be known
as the " Municipal Hospital Commission . ”
The commission shall provide rules , regu-
lations and by-laws for the management
of the municipal hospital and shall , out of
the revenue of the hospital , pay necessary
operating expenses , repairs and additions
thereto , and provide a sufficient reserve
fund to insure that the hospital is kept in
repair and in safe and sanitary condition
and to provide against any emergency
that may arise . The commission shall
from time to time pay to the city the sur-
plus revenue derived from the operation
of the hospital as provided in KRS
216.160 . ( Subsection ( 1 ) amended , 1946 ,
c . 169 , § 20 )
(2 ) In order that the commission may
be nonpartisan and nonpolitical , no per-
son shall be appointed a member thereof
who has , within the last two years before
his appointment , held any city , county ,
state or Federal office , or who is related
within the third degree to the mayor or
any member of the legislative body of the
city . The commission shall not appoint ,
to any subordinate office created by it .
any person who is related to any member
of the legislative body or to the mayor or
chief executive of the city , or to any mem-
ber of the commission . No officer or em-
ploye of the city , whether holding a paid
or unpaid office , shall be eligible to be a
member of the commission . The members
of the commission shall be citizens , tax-
payers and legal voters of the city and
shall not at the time of their appointment
be indebted to the city or be surety on
the official bond of any officer of the city .
No member of the commission shall be
interested in any contract for the furnish-
ing of supplies or service of any kind to
the hospital or to the city . If at any time
during his term of office any member of
the commission becomes a candidate for
or is elected or appointed to any public
office , he shall automatically vacate his
office on the commission and another per-
son shall be appointed .
(3 ) Each commissioner shall execute
bond with a surety company in the penal
sum of one thousand dollars , conditioned
82

HOSPITALS , CLINICS AND PERSONAL CARE HOMES § 216.430
upon the faithful performance of his offi-
cial duties . The bonds shall be approved
by the legislative body of the city and the
premiums shall be paid by the city . An
action may be maintained upon any com-
missioner's bond by any person injured by
a violation of the covenants therein con-
tained .
(4) The members of the commission
shall serve without compensation .
(5) The first municipal hospital com-
mission appointed in each city under this
section shall be appointed for terms as
follows : Two members for a term of one
year , two for a term of two years , and
three for a term of three years . Upon
the expiration of the first terms , successors
shall be appointed for a term of three
years .
LYING - IN HOSPITALS
216.250 [ 2090 ; 2091] Private lying-inhospitals ; how established . ( 1 ) Any per-
son who desires to establish or to continue
to keep open a private lying -in hospital
for women shall , before opening or con-
tinuing the hospital for the reception of
patients , produce before the mayor of the
city , if the hospital is to be established
within an incorporated city , or before the
county judge , if the hospital is to be es-
tablished outside of a city , a statement
signed by two regular physicians , known
to the mayor or judge , that the person de-
siring to open or maintain the hospital is
of good character and reputation , that
the premises proposed to be used for the
hospital are suitable and suitably fur-
nished for the proper care and nursing of
women during childbirth and confinement ,
and describing the same , and that the
establishment or continuance of the hos-
pital will be for the public convenience .
(2) The mayor or county judge , upon
receiving such statement , shall immedi-
ately inspect the premises and make in-
quiry as to the facts set forth in the state-
ment . If he is satisfied that the statement
is true he shall issue a permit to the per-
son to open or continue the private lying -in
hospital .
216.260 [ 2093 ] Lying-in hospitals sub-
ject to inspection . Private lying -in hos-
pitals are subject to such medical inspec-
tion as the city council , in a city , or the
county court , if not in a city, may direct ,
and to the inspection of the grand jury .
LICENSING OF HOSPITALS
216.400 Definitions for KRS 216.400 to
216.500 and subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 216.990 .
As used in KRS 216.400 to 216.500 and
subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 216.990 :
( 1 ) " Hospital ' means a place devoted
primarily to the maintenance and opera-
tion of facilities for the diagnosis , treat-
ment , or care , for more than twenty -four
hours , of two or more non -related in-
dividuals suffering from illness , disease ,
injury, or deformity , or a place including
nursing and convalescent homes and all
institutions for the care of the sick , de-
voted primarily to providing , for more
than twenty -four hours , obstetrical or
other medical or nursing care for two or
more non -related individuals ;
( 2 ) " Person " means any individual ,
firm , partnership , corporation, company ,
association , or joint stock association and
the legal successor thereof ;
( 3 ) " Governmental unit " means the
state , or any county or municipality , or
a department , division , board or agency
of any of the foregoing ;
(4) “ Licensing agency " means the
State Board of Health . ( 1952 , c . 16 , § 1 ;
effective July 1, 1952 )
216.410 Declaration of legislative pur-
pose. The purpose of KRS 216.400 to
216.500 and subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 216.990
is to provide for the development , estab-
lishment and enforcement of minimum
standards for the construction, mainte-
nance and operation of hospitals as de-
fined herein . ( 1952, c . 16 , § 2 ; effective
July 1, 1952)
216.420 Application for license . An ap-
plication for a license to establish , con-
duct , manage or operate a hospital shall
be made to the licensing agency upon
forms provided by it and shall contain
such information as the licensing agency
reasonably requires , which may include
affirmative evidence of ability to comply
with such reasonable standards , rules and
regulations as may be lawfully prescribed
hereunder . Each application shall be ac-
companied by a license fee of ten dollars .
(1952 , с . 16 , § 3 ; effective July 1, 1952)
216.430 Issuance and renewal of li-
censes ; annual reports ; hospitals oper-
ated by governmental units ; posting of
licenses . Upon receipt of an application
83

§ 216.440 HOSPITALS , CLINICS AND PERSONAL CARE HOMES
for license accompanied by the license fee ,
the licensing agency shall issue a license ,
if the applicant and the hospital facil-
ities meet the requirements established
under KRS 216.400 to 216.500. Any hos-
pital which is in operation at the time
KRS 216.400 to 216.500 becomes effective
shall be licensed upon application even
though it does not comply with the stand-
ards at that time . However , failure to
comply with the required standards with
in two years thereafter shall result in
the institution of an action for revocation
or suspension of the license . Each license ,
unless sooner suspended or revoked , shall
expire on June 30 following its issuance ,
and be renewable annually for a fee of
five dollars upon filing by the licensee and
approval of the licensing agency , of an
annual report upon such uniform dates
and containing such information in such
form as the licensing agency may reason-
ably require . All fees collected shall be
deposited in the State Treasury for the
use of the licensing agency in carrying
out the purposes of KRS 216.400 to
216.500 . Hospitals operated by govern-
mental units shall be exempt from the
payment of fees and licensure but shall
comply with all other requirements of
KRS 216.400 to 216.500 . Each license
shall be issued only for the premises and
persons or governmental units named in
the application and shall not be trans-
ferrable or assignable without the written
approval of the licensing agency . Licenses
shall be posted in a conspicuous place on
the licensed premises . ( 1952, c. 16, § 4 ;
effective July 1 , 1952 )

216.440 Denial , suspension or revoca-
tion of license ; hearings and review . The
licensing agency , after notice to the appli-
cant or licensee and after a hearing as
herein provided , is authorized to deny ,
suspend or revoke a license in any case
in which it finds that there has been a sub-
stantial failure to comply with the re-
quirements established under KRS 416.400
to 416.500 . Notices of hearing shall be
issued in the name of the licensing agency
by the secretary thereof and shall state
the time and place of the hearing , which
shall not be less than thirty days from
service of the notice, and shall also con-
tain a concise statement of the reason for
the hearing and sufficient information
to reasonably apprise the applicant or
licensee of the substance of the facts
claimed to constitute a violation of KRS
416.400 to 416.500 . Service of such notices
may be effected in the manner prescribed
by law for the service of civil process , or
may be effected by any member or em-
ploye of the licensing agency personally
delivering same to the applicant or licen-
see , or by the licensing agency forwarding
same to the applicant or licensee , by United
States registered mail postage prepaid
at the address of the applicant or licensee
as reflected by the records of the licensing
agency . The hearing may be conducted
by the licensing agency in person or by a
hearing officer or a hearing committee
appointed by the licensing agency ; all
members of the licensing agency , and all
persons appointed by the licensing agency
as hearing officer or as members of a hear-
ing committee are authorized to admin-
ister oaths , issue subpoenas for the attend-
ance of witnesses and for the production
of books , papers and documents and to
cite for contempt , before the circuit or
quarterly court in the county in which
the hearing is held, disobedience of its
process or contumacious or disrespectful
conduct. The applicant or licensee pro-
ceeded against shall be known as “ re-
spondent " and shall be entitled to be
represented at the hearing in person or by
counsel , or both , and shall be entitled to
introduce testimony by witnesses or , if
the licensing agency so permits, by depo-
sitions . All decisions revoking , suspend-
ing or denying licenses or applications
shall be made by the licensing agency
and shall be recorded in the minutes of
the licensing agency , which may review
any and all orders and rulings made by
a hearing officer or hearing committee ,
and which shall consider the entire tran-
script of the record of hearing that shall
be prepared and furnished to the licens-
ing agency in the event the hearing is
conducted by a hearing officer or hearing
committee . The secretary of the licensing
agency shall notify , by registered United
States mail postage prepaid , the appli-
cant or licensee of the decision of the
licensing agency , which decision shall be-
come final and conclusive thirty days
after notice thereof is given as above pro-
vided , unless the applicant or licensee ,

HOSPITALS , CLINICS AND PERSONAL CARE HOMES § 216.450
within said thirty-day period, appeals to
the court therefrom pursuant to KRS
216.480 . The procedure governing hear-
ings authorized by this section shall be
in accordance with rules promulgated by
the licensing agency with the advice and
approval of the hospital council . (1952 ,
c . 16, § 5 ; effective July 1, 1952 )
Contempt , limit of penalty for, KRS 432.260
216.450 Minimum standards for con-
struction , alterations and operation of
hospitals . ( 1 ) Before construction is be-
gun involving an expenditure of more
than fifteen thousand dollars for the con-
struction of new buildings or additions or
alterations to existing buildings or any
change in facilities , for a hospital , the
licensee shall submit plans to the licens-
ing agency for approval . No system of
water supply , plumbing , sewage , garbage
or refuse disposal for a hospital shall be
installed nor any such existing system be
materially altered or extended until com-
plete plans and specifications for the in-
stallation , alteration or extension , to-
gether with such information as may be
required , have been submitted and ap-
proved by the Department of Health in
accordance with the sanitary codes in ex-
isting Kentucky statutes . Hospitals shall
comply with all local zoning regulations .
They shall be so located as to be reason-
ably free from noises , smoke , foul odors
or other disturbing or injurious nuisances .
(2) Hospitals shall be served by a good
road which is kept passable at all times
of the year . There must be one or more
telephones or other communication sys-
tems as may be needed in order to sum-
mon help promptly in case of fire or other
emergencies .
(3 ) The construction of the building
shall be such as to provide for the safety
and protection of the patients and person-
nel . The building shall be kept in good
repair as related to the comfort and safety
of its occupants . The building shall com-
ply with the rules , regulations , and stand-
ards of safety in accordance with existing
Kentucky statutes governing fire and
safety precautions . The building shall be
so constructed and screened as to prevent
the entrance of rats , flies , mosquitoes and
other rodents and insects capable of trans-
mitting disease . The walls , floors , and
ceilings shall be of such a character to
permit frequent washing, cleaning or
painting and the maintenance of sanitary
conditions . The building shall comply
with all existing local buildings codes .
(4) The hospital shall be provided with
a heating system , capable of maintaining
a minimum temperature of 68 ° Fahrenheit
at all times in the patient areas .
(5) Each patient's room shall have suf-
ficent lighting for reading and all other
uses as needed . All entrances , hallways ,
stairways , inclines, ramps , cellars, attics ,
storerooms , kitchens , laundries , and ser-
vice units must have sufficient lighting to
prevent accidents and to promote effici-
ency of service . Emergency lighting
facilities must be provided and distri-
buted so as to be readily available to per-
sonnel on duty . Flashlights or battery
operated lamps shall be in readiness at
all times for use in the delivery and op-
erating rooms . At no time may open
flame type of lights be used in these
rooms .
( 6) The building shall be adequately
ventilated at all times . Special attention
shall be given to the ventilation of
patients ' quarters so as to supply fresh
air and to prevent accumulation of objec-
tionable odors . Toilets without outside
window ventilation may not be used un-
less there is an adequate system of forced
ventilation .
(7) If elevators are provided they shall
be maintained , checked and kept in good
condition at all times . Stairways shall be
provided with such handrails as may be
desirable . Stairways should be of
width and design which will easily accom-
modate removal of a patient by stretcher
and should be enclosed with a vertical
cut - off at each floor level .
a
( 8 ) There shall be adequate hand -wash-
ing facilities throughout the institution .
There shall be an adequate number of
utility rooms for the cleaning of bed
pans and general sanitation purposes .
The institutions shall be maintained in a
Ac-clean , orderly and sanitary manner .
cumulated waste material must be re-
moved daily . There must be frequent
cleaning of floors , walls , woodwork and
windows . The premises must be kept free
from rodent and insect infestation . Bath
and toilet facilities must be maintained in
a clean and sanitary condition at all times .
( 9 ) The water supply must be approved
by the Department of Health . There
85

§ 216.450 HOSPITALS , CLINICS AND PERSONAL CARE HOMES
shall be an ample supply of hot water
available at all times for general use .
(10 ) Sewers shall be discharged into
a municipal sewerage system where such
a system is available ; otherwise , the
sewage shall be collected , treated and dis-
posed of in an independent sewerage sys-
tem which complies with the standards
approved by the sanitary codes of the
Department of Health and city regula-
tions .
( 11 ) All plumbing , plumbing fixtures ,
sterilizers and other similar equipment
shall be so constructed , installed and
maintained as to prevent cross connections
or other sanitary hazards , as required by
the standards approved by the sanitary
codes of the Department of Health and
applicable city regulations . A sanitary
bath , toilet and lavatory must be provided
on each floor where patients are cared
for .
( 12 ) The garbage disposal system must
be approved by the Department of Health .
Adequate provision shall be made to pre-
vent obvious odors , the spread of disease
and insanitary conditions resulting from
the improper handling of garbage and
other refuse .
(13 ) The hospital shall make provision
for the proper cleaning of linen and other
washable goods with special provision for
handling contaminated linen . Hospitals
maintaining and operating laundries shall
provide ventilation for the elimination of
steam and odors and proper insulation to
prevent the transmission of noises to
patient areas .
( 14 ) The hospital shall comply with the
existing laws , rules and regulations gov-
erning the handling of food and food
products in the State of Kentucky . The
facilities shall be adequate to serve as
many patients as the hospital accom-
modates .
(15 ) The hospital shall provide suitable
accommodations for all its patients . There
shall be adequate floor space , furnishings ,
bed linens , and such other utensils , equip-
ment and supplies as is reasonably re-
quired for the proper care of the patients
accommodated . Proper precaution shall
be taken to prevent the spread of disease .
There shall be provision for the proper
sterilization of all supplies , utensils and
equipment and for storing them in a clean ,
convenient and orderly manner . An ade-
quate system for bed patients to use in
calling nurses and attendants shall be
maintained .
( 16 ) If there are operating and deliv-
ery rooms , they shall be provided with
adequate equipment to meet the require-
ments of the hospital both in quality and
in quantity .
(17 ) If drugs are used , an adequate
supply and other medicinal agents shall
be available at all times to meet the re-
quirements of the hospital . They shall
be stored in a safe manner and kept prop-
erly labeled and accessible . Narcotics
and other dangerous or poisonous drugs
shall be handled in a safe manner to pro-
tect against their unauthorized use . There
shall be adequate refrigeration for bio-
logicals and drugs which require refrig-
eration . The existing laws , rules and
regulations governing drugs and poisons
shall be complied with .
( 18 ) If X -ray facilities are provided in
any hosiptal , there shall be ample space
for the X -ray equipment , film storage ,
view boxes and dark room equipment.
Proper precautions to protect patients
and hospital personnel against undue
radiation shall be taken .
( 19 ) If a laboratory is provided , there
shall be ample space , equipment and sup-
plies to meet the requirements of the hos-
pital .
(20 ) If unembalmed bodies are held
for more than twenty -four hours , a proper
well ventilated morgue with refrigerated
compartments shall be provided .
(21) Nothing in KRS 216.400 to
216.500 and subsection ( 2 ) of KRS
216.990, or in the rules and regulations
adopted pursuant thereto , shall be con-
strued as applying to the practice of :
(a) Medicine ,
(b ) Osteopathy ,
(c) Chiropractic ,
(d) Healing by prayer in accordance
with the creed or tenets of any well rec-
ognized church or religious denomina-
tion ,
( e) Any other practice or system of
the healing arts which is recognized or
permitted by the laws of the State of
Kentucky .
( 22 ) Nothing in KRS 216.400 to 216.500
and subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 216.990 , or in
the rules and regulations adopted pursu-
ant thereto , shall be construed in any way
to have the effect of denying a license to
any hospital by reason of the type or
86

system of practice of any of the healing
arts , which are so recognized or per-
mitted .
(23 ) Adequate and complete records
shall be written for all patients admitted
to the hospital . These records shall be
filed in an orderly and accessible manner
in the hospital . All orders for patients
shall be written in ink or indelible pencil .
Such orders shall be preserved on the
patient's chart or in the established rec-
ord of such orders .
( 24 ) Every reasonable effort shall be
made to provide the best possible comfort
and safety for the patients , and no person
shall be denied emergency care solely be-
cause of race , creed or color . Accepted
principles of hospital operation shall be
followed in the public interest. The re-
porting of all births , deaths , reportable
diseases and other vital statistics required
under existing laws , rules and regulations
shall be complied with .
(25 ) All of the existing laws , rules and
regulations of the Department of Health
shall be complied with as related to the
control of the spread of communicable
diseases . If patients are accepted with
communicable diseases , suitable isolation
and quarantine facilities shall be pro-
vided . Every effort shall be made to pre-
vent the spread of disease in the hospital
and to prevent the spread of disease from
patients in the hospital to persons outside
the hospital . Every reasonable safeguard
shall be provided for the maintaining and
protection of the health of the personnel
of the hospital .
(26 ) All hospital employes shall be re-
quired to have such examinations and
tests as may be necessary to determine
their freedom from communicable dis-
eases which might endanger the health of
the patients or other employes . The ex-
aminations shall be repeated at such
in-
tervals

ofthe Hospital Council

of this section shall not apply

216.470 Powers of licensing agency .
Subject to approval by the Hospital Coun-
cil, the licensing agency is empowered to
establish standards and requirements not
in conflict with the provisions of KRS
216.400 to 216.500 and 216.990 ( 2 ) which
it deems necessary to properly protect the
public , and to promulgate rules and regu-
lations for the administration and en-
forcement of KRS 216.400 to 216.500 and
216.990 ( 2 ) and the standards and require-
ments established as aforesaid . The licens-
ing agency is empowered to employ such
clerks , inspectors and other personnel as
it considers necessary to properly admin-
ister and enforce KRS 216.400 to 216.500
and 216.990 ( 2 ) . The licensing agency
shall, through its authorized agent or
agents , have full and free access to the re-
quired records , grounds , and buildings of
every institution licensed or who has ap-
plied for license under KRS 216.400 to
216.500. (1952 , c . 16 , § 8 ; effective July
1, 1952 )

216.610 Definitions for KRS 216.610 to

§ 216.680 HOSPITALS , CLINICS AND PERSONAL CARE HOMES

to 216.700 . Provisional licenses shall re-

216.680 Grounds for denial , suspension
or revocation of license . The commissioner
may deny , suspend or revoke a license on
any of the following grounds :
(1) Failure to meet or violation of any
of the provisions of KRS 216.610 to
216.700 or the rules and regulations law-
fully promulgated pursuant thereto ;
(2 ) Permitting , aiding or abetting the
commission of any illegal or immoral act
in the home ;
(3) Conduct or practice found by the
commissioner to be detrimental to the wel-
fare of the residents of the home ;
(4) Failure to file reports or pay con-
tributions as required under KRS Chapter
341. ( 1960 , с . 87 , § 8 ; 1962 , с . 112 , § 7)
216.690 Hearing on denial, suspension
or revocation of license ; appeal . (1) In
any case where the department refuses to
issue , or suspends or revokes a license ,
written notice shall be given to the appli-
cant or licensee by registered mail, stating
the proposed action and grounds therefor ,
and notifying the applicant or licensee
that such license will be refused or re-
voked unless the applicant or licensee
makes application for a hearing before
the commissioner within ten days of such
notice . If application for such hearing is
made , the commissioner shall set a time
for the hearing , of which ten days ' notice
shall be given to the applicant or licensee
and at the hearing the applicant or li-
censee shall be entitled to be heard in
person or by counsel , and to present wit-
nesses . Following the hearing, the com-
missioner shall render a decision in writ-
ing setting forth his findings of fact and
conclusions of law , and affirming , modi-
fying or setting aside the findings of the
department , and shall send a copy thereof
to the applicant or licensee by registered
mail .
( 2 ) Within twenty days after such de-
cision , any party aggrieved thereby may
secure judicial review thereof by filing a
petition for review against the commis-
sioner in the circuit court of Franklin
County . The petition shall state fully the
grounds upon which review is sought,
assign all errors relied on , and shall be
verified by the petitioner .
( 3 ) With his answer , the commissioner
shall certify and file with the court all
documents and papers , and a transcript
of all testimony taken in the matter , and
orders made therein , together with the
findings of fact and decision therein . If
consented to by the petitioner , an abstract
of any portion of the record may be certi-
fied by the commissioner in lieu of certify-
ing such portions of the record in full .
(4) The court in its review of such
action shall hear the record as certified
by the commissioner and no other evidence
shall be received . The court shall dispose
of the cause in a summary manner , limit-
ing its decision to a determination of
whether or not :
(a) There was sufficient probative evi-
dence to support the commissioner's
order ;
(b ) The regulations upon which the
order was based are reasonable ;
(c) The commissioner acted arbitrarily ,
unlawfully , or in such a manner as to con-
stitute an abuse of discretion .
(5) The court shall enter judgment af-
firming , modifying or setting aside the
order , or decision , or in its discretion re-
manding the cause to the commissioner
for further proceedings in conformity
with the direction of the court. (1960 , с .
87 , § 9 )
216.700 Remedy by injunction . Not-
withstanding the existence or pursuit of
any other remedy , the department may in
the manner provided by law , maintain an
action in the name of the State of Ken-
tucky for injunction or other process
against any person or agency to restrain
or prevent the establishment , conduct ,
management or operation of a home for
aged or infirm without a license under
KRS 216.610 to 216.700 . In any such court
proceedings , the department may apply
for and on due showing be entitled to have
issued the court's subpoena requiring
forthwith the appearance of any defend

216.750 Definitions for KRS 216.750 to
216.780 . As used in KRS 216.750 to
216.780 :
( 1 ) "Nursing home ” and “ personal care

216.760 Functions of department . The
Department of Economic Security shall be
responsible for promotion of interest in
the development of additional facilities for
the housing and care of the elderly , and
for providing consultative and technical
assistance to public and private groups
engaged in the development of such fa-
cilities . The department's functions shall
include but not be limited to :
( 1 ) Promotion of local and community
interest in the problem of housing and
care for the elderly .
( 2) Assisting local housing commissions
in the development of low rent housing
projects for the elderly .
( 3 ) Provision of information as to need
for facilities in particular areas or loca-
tions .
(4) Provision of advice and assistance
in the planning of facilities as to area to
be served , size , type, staffing , operation
and maintenance .
(5 ) Provision of information as to the
availability of federal financial assistance
and the procedures which should be fol-
lowed in applying for such assistance .
( 6 ) Provision of information as to the
availability of state financial assistance
and the procedures which should be fol-
lowed in applying for such assistance .
(7) Provision of information as to the
availability of private financial assistance .
(8 ) Provision of information as to
li-
censing requirements
ofthe state or its
political subdivisions
.

food deemed adulterated . (Repealed , 1960 ,

217.025 When food deemed adulterated .
A food shall be deemed to be adulterated :
(1) (a) If it bears or contains any
poisonous or deleterious substance which
may render it injurious to health ; but in
case the substance is not an added sub-
stance such food shall not be considered
adulterated under this subsection if the
quantity of such substance in such food
does not ordinarily render it injurious to
health ; or
some , or injurious to health ; or
(e) If it is the product of a diseased
animal or an animal which has died other-
wise than by slaughter, or that has been
fed upon the uncooked offal from a
slaughterhouse ; or
(f) If its container is composed , in
whole or in part , of any poisonous or
deleterious substance which may render
the contents injurious to health ;
(2) ( a) If any valuable constituent has
been in whole or in part omitted or
abstracted therefrom ; or
(b) If any substance has been substi-
tuted wholly or in part therefor ; or
( c ) If damage or inferiority has been
concealed in any manner ; or
(d) If any substance has been added
thereto or mixed or packed therewith so
as to increase its bulk or weight , or reduce
its quality or strength or make it appear
better or of greater value than it is ;
(3) If it is confectionery and its bears
or contains any non -nutritive article or
substance except harmless coloring , harm-
less flavoring , harmless resinous glaze
not in excess of four -tenths of one per-
centum , harmless natural wax not in ex-
cess of four -tenths of one percentum ,
harmless natural gum , and pectin ; pro-
vided that this subsection shall not apply
to any chewing gum by reason of its con-
taining harmless non -nutritive mastica-
tory substances ;
(4) If it bears or contains a coal - tar
color other than one from a batch which
has been certified under authority of
the federal act ;
( 5 ) If it is filled milk . ( 1960 , с . 247, §
3 ; effective June 16 , 1960)
