218.192 Injunctive remedies . Notwith-
standing the existence or pursuit of any
other remedy , civil or criminal , the State
Board of Health or the State Department
of Health may maintain , in its own name ,
an action to restrain or enjoin any viola-
tion of this chapter . (1958 , c . 99 , § 5 ; ef-
fective June 19 , 1958 )
218.194 Contraband nature of vehicles
used in drug traffic . ( 1 ) The following
property found and seized within the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky is hereby declared
to be contraband : Any vehicle , water-
craft , or aircraft in which any person is
illegally possessing or transporting nar-
cotic drugs . "Illegally possessing " means
and includes the holding of any narcotic
drugs unless lawfully acquired and in-
tended for lawful uses .
(2) Any officer , agent , inspector , or
representative of the State Department of
Health and any peace officer within this
state may , upon probable cause , without
warrant seize contraband as herein de-
fined in subsection ( 1 ) , and hold it sub-
ject to the order of the court before the
owner or one in possession of the contra-
band has been charged with violation of
this chapter . Upon conviction of the de-
fendant , the court shall enter an order
vesting title to all the contraband prop-
erty in the State Department of Health .
The State Department of Health may re-
tain and use such property in the enforce-
ment of this chapter or dispose of such
property as hereinafter provided .
( 3 ) ( a ) Contraband property included
in subsection ( 1 ) shall be subject to the
right of any owner or lienor whose lien is
valid and of record , to intervene and es-
tablish his rights in the property by prov-
ing that the property was being used in
connection with traffic in narcotic drugs
without the knowledge , consent or ap-
proval of the owner or lienor . If the
owner of the property does so prove , the
court shall order the property restored to
him. If the lienor so proves , the court
shall order the sale of the property at
public auction, unless an agreement is
made between the lienor and the State De-
partment of Health which shall not be-
come final until it has been approved by
the court . The State Department of Health
may deliver any property found to be con-
traband to a lienor whose claim has been
established by order of a court of compe-
tent jurisdiction , upon payment to the
State Department of Health of the differ-
ence of the fair market value of the prop-
erty so seized and the claim of the lienor .
(b) Where an agreement has been made
between the lienor and the State Depart-
ment of Health and approved by the court ,
a public auction shall not be required . If
an agreement is not entered into between
the State Department of Health and the
lienor or approved by the court , and a
public auction is required to be held, the
public auction shall be conducted by the
sheriff of the county in which the prop-
erty is seized . The sheriff shall receive
and be allowed the same fees as allowed
for sales under execution .
(c) The expenses of keeping and sell-
ing such property and the amount of all
valid recorded liens that are established
by intervention as being bona fide , shall
be paid out of the proceeds of the sales
whether they are private or public . The
balance shall be deposited in the State
Treasury in a revolving fund for the use
of the State Department of Health in
carrying out the provisions of this chapter .
(d) If the defendant is acquitted , no
property seized as contraband in connec-
tion with the arrest of the defendant shall
be ordered returned or restored unless the
person from whose possession the property
was taken proves that he was in lawful
possession of the property , and if no other
person appears and proves that he owns
129

§ 218.200 UNIFORM NARCOTIC DRUG ACT
the property or has a valid recorded lien
on the property and that the property was
being used without his knowledge and con-
sent , title shall vest in the State Depart-
ment of Health at the end of ninety days .
(e) If the owners or lienholders of any
contraband seized by any officer , agent ,
or representative of the State Department
of Health , or any peace officer of this
state , cannot be located within ninety
days , and during that time fail to appear
and claim the contraband , or if the owner
or lienholder appears and agrees , title to
the contraband shall immediately vest in
the State Department of Health , in which
event it may sell the contraband at a pri-
vate sale or retain and use the vehicle for
the enforcement of this chapter . ( 1958 , с .
99 , § 6 ; effective June 19 , 1958 )
218.200 [ 3716-20 ] Board of health to
prescribe regulations . The State Board
of Health shall make all needful rules and
regulations for carrying the provisions of
this chapter into effect .
Administrative regulations not effective until
thirty days after filing with Legislative Re-
search Commission , KRS 13.085
218.210 [ 3716-21 ] Penalties . ( 1 ) Any
person who sells , supplies , prescribes , ad-
ministers , dispenses or furnishes , or causes
to be sold , supplied , prescribed , adminis-
tered , dispensed or furnished , any nar-
cotic drug in violation of this chapter to a
person under twenty - one years of age ,
shall be guilty of a felony and shall be
fined not more than twenty thousand dol-
lars and confined in the penitentiary for
not less than twenty years , and may be so
confined for a longer period or for life .
(2 ) ( a ) Any person in possession of
any narcotic drug in violation of any pro-
vision of this chapter shall be guilty of a
felony and shall, for the first offense be
punished by confinement in the peniten-
tiary for not less than two years nor more
than ten years and by a fine of not more
than twenty thousand dollars , and for
each subsequent offense be punished by
confinement in the penitentiary for not
less than five years nor more than twenty
years and by a fine of not more than
twenty thousand dollars .
(b ) Any person who violates any pro-
vision of this chapter , for which a specific
penalty is not otherwise provided , shall
be guilty of a felony and shall, for the
first offense , be punished by confine-
ment in the penitentiary for not less than
five years nor more than twenty years
and by a fine of not more than twenty
thousand dollars , and , for each subsequent
offense be punished by confinement in
the penitentiary for not less than ten
years nor more than forty years , and by
a fine of not more than twenty thousand
dollars .
(3) No court or judge thereof shall
postpone or suspend judgment or sentence
or probate a defendant when the defend-
ant has pleaded guilty to or been con-
victed of selling , supplying , prescribing ,
administering , dispensing or furnishing , or
causing to be sold , supplied , prescribed ,
administered , dispensed or furnished , any
narcotic drug in violation of this chapter
to a person under twenty- one years of age ,
or when the defendant has previously
pleaded guilty to or been convicted of a
violation of any of the other provisions of
this chapter , except KRS 218.250 , or has
previously pleaded guilty to or been con-
victed of a violation of any of the nar-
cotic laws of the United States of America
or any state or territory thereof .
(4) Whenever a person charged with
a violation of subsection ( 1 ) of KRS
218.250 waives a jury , the court or judge
thereof may , upon the defendant plead-
ing or being found guilty , sentence the
defendant to imprisonment for twelve
months without the intervention of a jury .
Notwithstanding the provisions of subsec-
tion ( 2 ) of KRS 218.250 , no court or judge
thereof shall postpone or suspend judg-
ment or sentence or probate a defendant
when the defendant has pleaded guilty to
or been convicted of a violation of KRS
218.250 , unless the defendant consents to
enter , and forthwith enters an institution
approved by the court for the treatment
of narcotic addicts and remains therein
until discharged by said institution ; fail-
ure of the defendant to do so shall auto-
matically revoke his probation and require
the prompt entry of judgment and service
of sentence in the county jail .
( 5) Any person who violates subsection
( 2 ) of KRS 218.160 shall be punished by
a fine of not less than one hundred dollars
nor more than five hundred dollars , and
for each subsequent offense shall be pun-
ished by imprisonment in the county jail
for ninety days and by a fine of not less
than five hundred dollars . ( 1952 , c . 120 ;
1958 , c . 99 , § 8 ; 1962 , с . 6 , § 3 )

218.220 [ 3716-22 ] Effect of acquittal or
conviction under Federal Narcotic Laws .
No person shall be prosecuted for a viola-
tion of any provision of this chapter if
such person has been acquitted or con-
victed under the Federal Narcotic Laws
of the same act or omission which , it is
alleged , constitutes a violation of this
chapter .
218.230 [ 3716-23 ] Interpretation . This
chapter shall be so interpreted and con-
strued as to effectuate
itsgeneral purpose
,
to make uniform the laws of those states
which enact
it.

HOTELS AND RESTAURANTS
219.010 [ 2059 ] Definitions for KRS
219.010 to 219.110 . ( 1 ) Every building or
structure kept , used , maintained , adver-
tised or held out to the public as a place
where sleeping accommodations are fur-
nished to the public , whether with or
without meals , shall , for the purpose of
this chapter , be deemed a hotel .
( 2 ) Every building or structure kept ,
used , maintained , advertised or held out
to the public as a place where meals and
lunches are served without sleeping ac-
commodations shall , for the purpose of
this chapter , be deemed a restaurant , and
the person in charge thereof , whether as
owner , lessee , manager or agent , shall be
deemed proprietor of the restaurant .
219.020 [ 2059a - 1 ] Division of Hotel In-
spection to enforce chapter ; records . The
State Board of Health , through its Divi-
sion of Hotel Inspection , shall enforce the
provisions of this chapter . The division
shall keep such records as are necessary
for public use and inspection , showing the
condition of all hotels and restaurants ,
their sanitary condition , the names of the
proprietors thereof , and any other infor-
mation that may be for the betterment of
the public health . The division shall en-
force any orders , requests , rules or regu-
lations promulgated by the State Board of
Health .
Administrative regulations not effective until
thirty days after filing with Legislative Re-
search Commission , KRS 13.085
219.030 [ 2059a -2 ] Hotel and restaurant
certificates ; application; fee . ( 1 ) Every
person engaged in the business of con-
ducting a hotel or restaurant shall , each
year , procure an inspector's certificate
for each hotel or restaurant conducted by
him , except that one certificate shall be

sufficient for each combined hotel and
restaurant where each is conducted in the
same building and under the same man-
agement . Each certificate shall expire on
December 31 next following its issuance .
(2) The State Board of Health shall
furnish to any person desiring to conduct
a hotel or restaurant an application blank
for a certificate . The application shall
state the full name and address of the
owner of the building and of the lessee
and manager of the hotel or restaurant ,
together with a full description of the
building and property to be used for the
business , its location, the name under
which the business is to be conducted , and
such other information as may be required
by the State Board of Health . The appli-
cation shall be accompanied by the inspec-
tion fee , which for hotels shall be three
dollars , plus twenty -five cents for each
bedroom in excess of ten , and for restau-
rants shall be three dollars , plus twenty-
five cents for each five chairs , stools or
spaces where persons are fed in excess of
ten , but no fee shall exceed ten dollars .
(3 ) If the application is approved by
the State Board of Health , a certificate to
conduct the business shall be issued . The
certificate shall not be transferable . No
hotel or restaurant shall be maintained
or conducted without such certificate , ex-
cept that after making application for a
certificate and pending the issuance of the
certificate the hotel or restaurant shall be
permitted to operate as such until the
final refusal of the application by the
State Board of Health . No hotel or res-
taurant shall be denied relief in the courts
in an action instituted by a hotel or res-
taurant by reason of the fact that a cer-
tificate has not been issued to the hotel or
restaurant .
219.040 [ 2059a - 13 ] Certificates to be
displayed . Every hotel or restaurant se-
curing a certificate under the provisions
of KRS 219.030 shall keep it posted in a
conspicuous place in the office of the hotel
or restaurant .
219.050 [ 2059a -12 ] Cancellation of cer-
tificate . If any owner, manager or person
in charge of any hotel or restaurant has
been convicted as provided in subsection
( 2 ) of KRS 219.060, and fails for a period
of sixty days after such conviction to
comply with any provision of this chapter ,
his certificate to conduct business may be
cancelled by the State Board of Health .

,

, and shall

inevery department with

§ 219.080 HOTEL , RESTAURANT AND TRAILER PARK REGULATIONS
(2) No room shall be used for a sleep-
ing room unless it has at least one window
that opens upon and provides easy access
to the outside of the building or to a light
well , air shaft or court , and unless it has
one door opening on a hallway .
(3) In all cities where a system of wa-
terworks and sewerage is maintained for
public use , every hotel and rooming house
shall be equipped with suitable water
closets for accommodation of its guests .
The closets shall be connected by proper
plumbing with the sewerage system , and
the means of flushing such water closets
with the water of the system shall be in
such manner as provided in the regula-
tions of the State Board of Health . All
lavatories , bathtubs , sinks, drains , closets
and urinals must be connected and
equipped in a similar manner . In all
cities , not having a system of waterworks ,
every hotel shall have properly con-
structed sanitary privies as provided in
the rules and regulations of the State
Board of Health .
(4) Each hotel shall have a main pub-
lic washroom , convenient and of easy ac-
cess to guests .
219.080 [ 2059a - 8 ] Individual towels ;
beds and bedding ; disinfection of rooms .
( 1 ) All hotels shall provide each bedroom
with at least two clean towels daily for
each guest . They shall also provide the
main public washroom with clean indi-
vidual towels , maintaining them in view
and reach , for the use of guests during
the regular meal hour , and where no regu-
lar meal hours are maintained , then be-
tween the hours of 6:30 a.m. and 9:00
a.m. , 11:30 a.m. and 2:00 p.m ., and 6:00
p.m. and 8:00 p.m ., so that no two or more
guests will be required to use the same
towel unless it has first been washed .
Such individual towels shall not be less
than ten inches wide and fifteen inches
long after being washed . This provision
shall not prohibit the use of individual
paper towels in such washrooms .
(2 ) All hotels shall provide each bed or
sleeping place for the use of guests with
pillow slips and under and top sheets .
Each sheet , shall be made ninety -nine
inches long , and of sufficient width to
completely cover the mattress and springs
and shall not be used if it measures less
than ninety inches after being laundered.
The sheets and pillow slips shall be made
of white cotton or linen , and all such
sheets and pillow slips , after being used
by one guest , must be washed and ironed
before they are used by another guest , a
clean set being furnished each succeeding
guest . All bedding , including mattresses ,
quilts , blankets , pillows , sheets and com-
forts , used in any hotel must be thor-
oughly aired, disinfected and kept clean .
No bedding shall be used that is worn
out or unfit for use .
( 3 ) Any room in any hotel or restau-
rant infected with vermin or bedbugs
shall be fumigated, disinfected and reno-
vated until the vermin or bedbugs are
exterminated , at the expense of the pro-
prietor of the hotel . When any com-
municable disease occurs in any room in
any hotel or rooming house , the room shall
not be occupied until it has been cleaned
and disinfected under the supervision of
the health officer in accordance with the
rules and regulations of the state and
local boards of health .
219.090 [ 2059a -5 ] Fire extinguishing
equipment in small hotels . All hotels and
restaurants two stories high with ten or
more sleeping rooms shall have , for each
twenty -five hundred feet of area or frac-
tional part thereof , an efficient chemical
fire extinguisher conveniently located in
a public hallway outside of the sleeping
rooms , and always in condition for use , or
a one and one -fourth inch inside stand-
pipe with hose connection and a hose of
sufficient length always attached in such
hallway , which standpipe shall be sup-
plied by a sufficient pressure of water .
219.100 [ 2059a - 6 ] Fire extinguishing
equipment and precautions in large hotels .
All hotels and restaurants more than two
stories with ten or more sleeping rooms
shall have , each six thousand feet of area
or fractional part thereof , a one and three-
fourths inch standpipe , and a sufficient
one and one -fourth inch hose connected
therewith on each floor, constantly fur-
nished with sufficient water pressure from
waterworks or pumps which can be put
into instant action , or a two and one -half
inch metallic standpipe with metallic lad-
der attached above the first story , located
upon the outside of the wall extending
above the roof , and so situated as to be
accessible from the roof and each story
above the first , with valves and male hose
134

HOTEL , RESTAURANT AND TRAILER PARK REGULATIONS § 219.120
connections at every story and on the roof ,
and a female hose connection at the base
of the pipe of such size and pattern as to
allow connection with the equipment of
the local fire department . There shall also
be provided , for each eighty -five hundred
feet of area or fractional part thereof , at
least one efficient chemical fire extin-
guisher on each floor containing sleeping
apartments . If, for lack of waterworks or
steam to operate pumps , the inside stand-
pipe is impracticable , then in addition to
the fire extinguisher there shall be placed
in the hallway of each floor containing
sleeping apartments one barrel of water
and two pails labeled " For Fire Purposes
Only ." For each twenty -five hundred
feet of area or fraction thereof a red
light shall be kept burning all night at the
head of each stairway above the first floor ,
and near each approach to a stationary
fire escape in each sleeping room above
the first floor there shall be posted the
following printed notice : " Exit in Case
of Fire . Upon leaving this room , turn to
the (here insert right or left ) and by pass-
ing (here insert distance in feet ) you will
reach a red light , which indicates (here
insert fire escapes or stairways ) . "
219.110 [ 2059a -7 ] Fire escapes ; fire-
proof stairways . ( 1 ) Every hotel and
restaurant that is more than two stories
high shall be equipped with an iron stair-
way on the outside of the building extend-
ing from the cornice of the building to
within twelve feet of the ground , and con-
necting on each floor above the ground
with an opening from that floor . The
stairway shall have platform landings at
each floor not less than six feet in length
and three feet in width , and the stairway
and landings shall be guarded by an iron
railing not less than thirty inches in height
and shall be safely fastened and secured .
The stairway shall be not less than two
feet wide , with steps of not less than six-
inch tread , and shall be placed at an angle
of not more than forty -five degrees . The
way of egress to the fire escapes shall at
all times be kept free and clear of obstruc-
tions . Fire escapes shall be placed where
the hotel inspector directs . There shall be
provided one such fire escape for each
fifteen sleeping rooms on each floor above
the third floor . Every hotel less than four
stories high shall have hallways placarded
to indicate all stairways and exits , and
shall keep a five - eighths inch manila rope
of sufficient length to reach the ground ,
having knots at least every fifteen inches
apart, in each bedroom , such rope to be
fastened six feet above the floor near the
window , in a substantial manner and cap-
able of sustaining at least five hundred
pounds in weight , except that recognized
automatic fire escapes may be used in lieu
of knotted rope .
( 2 ) Whenever it is proposed to erect a
building three stories or more in height
as a hotel, the owner , contractor or builder
of the hotel shall construct it so that one
main hall on each floor above the ground
floor runs through to the outside wall of
the building . Every building converted
into a hotel must comply with the pro-
visions of this section .
(3 ) The provisions of this section relat-
ing to outside fire escapes , ropes and auto-
matic appliances shall not apply to hotels
having or making provision for interior
fireproof stairways approved as such by
the hotel inspector .
TRAILER PARKS
219.120 Definitions for KRS 219.120 to
219.250 . As used in KRS 219.120 to
219.250 , unless the context requires other-
wise :
(1) " Trailer coach ” or “ Mobile home "
means any vehicle or similar portable
structure used , or so constructed as to
permit its being used , as a conveyance
upon the public streets or highways , and
designed to permit the occupancy thereof
as a dwelling place for one or more per-
sons ;
( 2 ) " Dependent trailer coach " or " De-
pendent mobile house " means a trailer
coach which does not have toilet and bath
or shower facilities ;
(3) " Independent trailer coach " or
" Independent mobile home " means a
trailer coach with self-contained toilet
and bath or shower facilities ;
(4) " Trailer coach park " or " Park ”
means an area of land upon which two
or more occupied trailer coaches are har-
bored , either free of charge or for revenue
purposes , and shall include any building ,
structure , tent , vehicle , or enclosure used
or intended for use as a part of the equip-
ment of such trailer coach park ;
( 5 ) “ Trailer coach space " or " Trailer
coach site " means a plot of ground within
135

a trailer coach park designated for the ac-
commodation of one trailer coach ;
(6) " Department " means the State De-
partment of Health ;
(7 ) " Board " means the State Board of
Health . ( 1954 , c. 207 , § 1 ; effective July
1, 1954)

application shall be accompanied by a
permit fee of twenty -five dollars . (1954,
c . 207 , § 2 ; effective July 1, 1954)

219.170 Procedure for suspension and
revocation of operating permits . ( 1) The
department , after notice to the applicant
or holder of a permit to operate and after
a hearing as herein provided , is author-
ized to deny , suspend or revoke a permit
to operate in any case where it finds that
there has been a failure to comply with
the requirements established under KRS
219.120 to 219.250 .
( 2 ) Notice of hearing shall be issued in
the name of the department . It shall
state the time and place of the hearing
which shall not be less than thirty days
from service of the notice , and shall con-
tain a concise statement of the reason for
the hearing and sufficient information to
reasonably apprise the applicant or
holder of the permit of the substance of
the facts claimed to constitute a violation
of KRS 219.120 to 219.250 .
( 3 ) Service of notices may be made in
the manner prescribed by law for the
service of civil process , or may be made
by any employe of the department per-
sonally delivering same to the applicant
or holder of the permit , or by the depart-
ment forwarding same to the applicant
or holder of the permit by United States
registered mail postage prepaid at the
address of the applicant or holder as
reflected by the records of the department .
(4) The hearing may be conducted by
a hearing officer or a hearing committee
appointed by the department . All per-
sons appointed by the department as hear-
ing officer or as members of a hearing
committee are authorized to administer
oaths , issue subpoenas for attendance of
witnesses and for the production of books ,
papers and documents , and to cite for con-
tempt , before the circuit or quarterly
court in the county in which the hearing
is held, disobedience of its process or in-
subordinate or disrespectful conduct .
(5 ) The applicant or holder of the per-
mit proceeded against shall be known as
respondent " and shall be entitled to be
represented at the hearing in person or by
counsel , or both , and shall be entitled to
introduce testimony by witnesses or , if
the department so permits , by deposition .
( 6 ) All decisions revoking , suspending
or denying permits or applications shall
be made by the department , which may
review any and all orders and rulings
made by a hearing officer or hearing com-
mittee . and which shall consider the entire
transcript of the record of hearing that
shall be prepared and furnished to the
department in the event the hearing is
conducted by a hearing officer or hearing
committee .
(7) The department shall notify by
registered United States mail postage pre-
paid the applicant or holder of permit of
the decision of the department which de-
cision shall become final and conclusive
thirty days after notice thereof is given
as above provided , unless the applicant
or holder of the permit within said thirty
day period , appeals to the board there-
from pursuant to KRS 219.240 .
(8) The procedure governing hearings
authorized by this section shall be in ac-
cordance with the rules promulgated by
the board . ( 1954, c. 207 , § 6 )
219.180 Duties of permit holders . Each
trailer coach park issued a permit to
operate or to be constructed under the
provisions of KRS 219.120 to 219.250
shall :
(1) Comply with all local zoning regu-
lations ;
( 2 ) Be in charge of a responsible at-
tendant or caretaker at all times ;
(3 ) Not allow a nuisance to be created
or allow a nuisance to continue to exist
in the trailer coach park ;
(4) Be so located that the drainage of
the park area will not endanger any water
supply ;
(5) Be well drained ;
(6) Furnish an adequate supply of
water of safe , sanitary quality , approved
137

§ 219.190 HOTEL , RESTAURANT AND TRAILER PARK REGULATIONS
by the department , as well as an adequate to the applicant or his agent ; and one
supply of hot water ;
(7) Provide adequate toilet , lavatory
and bathing facilities for occupants of de-
pendent trailer coaches ;
( 8 ) Maintain buildings at a tempera-
ture of at least sixty - eight degrees Fahren-
heit during the period from October 1 to
May 1 ;
( 9 ) Provide a laundry room or build-
ing containing laundry trays to accomo-
date the patrons of the trailer coach park ;
however no laundry trays shall be located
in toilets or bathrooms ;
(10 ) Dispose of all sewage and other
water - carried wastes into a municipal
sewage system whenever available ; how-
ever where connections are not available,
disposal shall be into a private system
which includes a sanitary means of dis-
posal, the operation of which creates
neither a nuisance nor a menace to health ,
and is approved by the department ;
(11) Permit no water or waste to fall
on the ground from a trailer coach ;
(12 ) Supply a sufficient number of ade-
quate flyproof and watertight containers
for the storage of garbage and rubbish ;
( 13 ) Dispose of garbage and rubbish in
a manner which creates neither a nuisance
nor a menace to health and which is ap-
proved by the department ;
(14) Employ adequate insect and rod-
ent control measures ;
( 15) Make all buildings fly and rodent
proof and eliminate rodent harborages ;
(16) When community kitchens and
dining rooms are provided , maintain such
facilities and equipment as are supplied,
in a sanitary condition and good repair ;
(17 ) Be subject to the rules and regu-
lations of the State Fire Marshal ;
(18) Notify immediately the local health
department of any suspected communica-
ble disease within the trailer coach park .

is empowered to establish standards and
requirements for trailer coach parks with-
in the provisions of KRS 219.120 to
219.250 , and to promulgate rules and regu-
lations for the administration and enforce-
ment of KRS 219.120 to 219.250 and the
standards and requirements established .
(1954 , с . 207 , § 9 ; effective July 1, 1954)
219.210 Exemption of quarters for farm
labor . Nothing in KRS 219.120 to 219.250
should be construed to include buildings ,
tents , or other structures maintained by
any individual or company on their own
premises and used exclusively to house
their own farm labor , or the area or
premises on any farm upon which are
harbored trailer coaches occupied by per-
sons employed upon the farm for not
more than ninety days in any calendar
year in the production , harvesting or pro-
cessing of agricultural products produced .
(1954 , с . 207 , § 10 ; effective July 1, 1954)
219.220 Enforcement powers of depart-
ment ; inspections ; right of entry to prem-
ises . The department shall enforce the
provisions of KRS 219.120 to 219.250 and
the rules and regulations adopted pursu-
ant thereto affecting health , sanitation ,
water supply, sewage , garbage and waste
disposal , and the department shall cause
to be inspected at least once each year
each trailer coach park and all accomoda-
tions and facilities connected therewith .
The department may carry out the provi-
sions of KRS 219.120 to 219.250 through
the local health departments . Such offi-
cials or employes of the department and
the local health departments are hereby
granted the power and authority to enter
upon the premises of each trailer coach
park at any time for the purposes set
forth in this section . ( 1954 , c . 207 , § 11 ;
effective July 1 , 1954 )
219.230 Regulations applicable to parks
operated only during one season . Trailer
coach parks operating only between May
1 to October 1, shall be governed by sep-
arate rules and regulations of the board
if in the opinion of the board such rules

219.240 Appeals from decisions of de-
partment . Any applicant or holder of a
permit aggrieved by a decision of the de-
partment made in accordance with KRS
219.170 may , within thirty days after the
mailing of notice of the decision as pro-
vided in KRS 219.170 , prosecute an appeal

219.250 Disposition of permit fees . All

for the use of the department in carrying

PENALTIES
219.990 [ 2059a - 10 ] Penalties . ( 1 ) Any
person who operates a hotel or restaurant ,
or who lets a building used for such busi-
ness , without complying with the provi-
sions of KRS 219.010 to 219.110 , or after
the cancellation or revocation of the cer-
tificate provided for in KRS 219.010 to
219.110 , shall be fined not less than ten
dollars nor more than one hundred dol-
lars , or imprisoned in the county jail for
not more than ninety days , and each day's
operation of the hotel or restaurant shall
constitute a separate offense .
( 2) Any person who violates any pro-
visions of KRS 219.120 to 219.250 shall be
fined not less than ten dollars nor more
than one hundred dollars . Each day of
violation shall constitute a separate of-
fense . (Subsection ( 2 ) created , 1954, с.
207 , § 16 ; effective July 1, 1954)

SANITATION DISTRICTS
220.010 [2062g - 1 ] Definitions for KRS
220.020 to 220.540. As used in KRS
220.020 to 220.540 , unless the context
otherwise requires :
(1) " Sanitary works , " " improve-
ments , " " sanitary system " or " sanitary
sewers " means any works constructed by
a sanitation district in accordance with
the purposes of KRS 220.010 to 220.540 ,
as set forth in KRS 220.030 .
(2 ) " Person " means any person , firm ,
copartnership , association or corporation
other than a public corporation .
( 3 ) " Public corporation " means any
county , city , school district , water district
or drainage district , and any other govern-
mental agency or political subdivision
clothed with the power of levying general
or special taxes or issuing bonds payable
from special funds .
(4) " Land " or " property " means real
property .
(5 ) " Board of directors , " " directors "
or " board " means the governing body of
a sanitation district .
( 6) " Sanitation commissioner " or
" commissioner ” means the commissioner
of sanitation districts , as provided for in
KRS 220.020 .
220.020 [ 2062g -2 ] Commissioner of sani-
tation districts ; power to establish dis-
tricts . The director of the Division of
Sanitary Engineering of the Department
of Health shall , in addition to his other
duties , act as commissioner of sanitation
districts , and is vested with jurisdiction ,
power and authority , when the conditions
set forth in KRS 220.010 to 220.520 and
certified to by the county board of health
are found to exist , to establish sanitation
districts within any county of the Com-
monwealth . ( 1962 , c . 103 )
Sanitation districts , provisions for establishment
of in counties containing city of first class ,
KRS 76.505 to 76.585
Sewer construction districts in counties having
metropolitan sewer districts , KRS 76.300 to76.380

proval of metropolitan sewer district board
forbidden in county containing metropolitan

220.030 [ 2062g -3 ] Purposes for which
sanitation district may be established .
Sanitation districts may be established for
any of the following purposes :
( 1 ) To prevent and correct the pollu-
tion of streams .
(2) To regulate the flow of streams for
sanitary purposes .
(3) To clean and improve stream chan-
nels for sanitary purposes .
(4) To provide for the collection and
disposal of sewage and other liquid wastes
produced within the district ; and incident
to such purposes and to enable their ac-
complishment , to construct , with all ap-
purtenances thereto , laterals , trunk sew-
ers , intercepting sewers , siphons , pumping
stations , treatment and disposal works , to
maintain , operate and repair same , and
do all other things necessary for the ful-
fillment of the purposes of KRS 220.010 to
220.520 .
220.040 [ 2062g-4 ] Petition necessary to
establish district ; requisites . ( 1 ) Before
the Commissioner of sanitation districts
shall establish a district , a petition , ap-
proved as to propriety and necessity by
the county board of health of each county
affected , shall be filed with the commis-
sioner , signed by sixty percent of those in
possession claiming as freeholders within
the limits of the terrtiory proposed to be
organized into a district .
(2) In determining when sixty percent
of the landowners have signed the peti-
tion , the commissioner shall be bound by
the names as they appear upon the county
tax duplicate , which for all purposes of
KRS 220.010 to 220.520 shall be prima
facie evidence of such ownership . The
commissioner shall further require evi-
dence to determine the fact that all sig-
natures on the petition are genuine , and a
notification by ordinary mail sent to the

and subscribe to an oath that he will hon-
estly, faithfully and impartially perform
the duties of his office , and that he will
not be interested in any contract let for
the purpose of carrying out any of the
provisions of KRS 220.010 to 220.520 .
The oath shall be filed with the clerk of
the county court .
(2 ) Each director shall give a good and
sufficient bond , to be approved by the
county judge appointing him, for the faith-
ful and honest performance of his duties
and as security for all moneys coming
into his hands or under his control . The
cost of the bond shall be paid by the
district .
220.160 [2062g- 17 ] Quorum ; action to
be by resolution ; votes to be recorded . A
majority of the directors shall constitute
a quorum , and concurrence of the major-
ity in any matter within the duties of the
board shall be sufficient for its determina-
tion . All actions taken by the directors
shall be by resolution, and in each instance
the name and vote of each director shall
be recorded in the minutes .
220.170 [ 2062g -16 ; 2062g - 18 ; 2062g -19 ;
2062g -20 ] Officers of board ; seal ; records ;
powers ; compensation ; regulations . ( 1 )
The board of directors shall , upon taking
oath , elect one of their members as presi-
dent of the board , and shall select some
suitable person as secretary , who need
not be a member of the board . The secre-
tary shall serve as treasurer of the district ,
or the board may select a treasurer . The
selection of the secretary and treasurer
shall be evidenced on the minutes of the
board, with their compensation . They shall
serve at the pleasure of the board .
(2) The board shall adopt a seal, and
shall keep in a wellbound book a record
of all proceedings , minutes of meetings ,
certificates, contracts , bonds given by
employes and all corporate acts , which
shall be open to the inspection of any
owner of property in the district as well
as all other interested persons .
(3) The board of directors shall be the
governing body of the sanitation district ,
and shall exercise all the powers and
manage and control all the affairs and
property of the district .
(4) The board of directors may pre-
scribe the duties and fix the compensation
of all the officers of the district , subject to
the limits fixed in this section . No mem-
ber of the board of directors , except the
chairman , shall receive more than twenty-
four hundred dollars per annum . The
chairman shall receive not more than six
hundred dollars per annum in addition to
his compensation as a director . The treas-
urer shall receive not more than nine
hundred dollars and the secretary not
more than three hundred dollars per an-
num .
(5) The board may adopt all necessary
rules and regulations for the proper man-
agement and conduct of the business of
the board and of the corporation, and for
carrying into effect the other objects for
which the district was formed. All such
rules or regulations shall become effec-
tive only upon posting in at least ten
public places within the district . (1956 , с .
211 ; effective May 18 , 1956 )
220.180 [2062g- 18 ] Duties of secretary ;
certified copies of records . The secretary
shall be the custodian of the seal , minutes
and records of the district , and shall assist
the board of directors in such particulars
as the board directs in the performance
of its duties . The secretary shall attest ,
under the seal of the district , such records
as are required of him by the provisions
of KRS 220.010 to 220.520 , or by any per-
son ordering the same , and shall receive
for such transcription the same compen-
sation allowed county clerks for copying
records . Any portion of the record so
certified and attested shall prima facie
import verity.
Fees of county court clerks , KRS 64.010
220.190 [ 2062g - 19 ] Duties of treasurer ;
145

§ 220.200 SANITATION DISTRICTS AND WATER POLLUTION CONTROL
bond . The treasurer shall , keep an accu-
rate account of all money received or dis-
bursed by the district , make periodic ac-
countings and reports thereof as directed
by the board of directors , make regular
semiannual accountings , assist any au-
ditors employed by the board to check the
financial records of the district , sign all
checks or vouchers of the district , deposit
all funds received in any bank selected by
the board, and perform all other functions
as chief financial officer of the district as
directed by the board . No check or
voucher shall be valid until countersigned
by the president of the board. The treas-
urer shall give a good and sufficient bond
to the board for the faithful and honest
performance of his duties and as security
for all money coming into his hands or
under his control . The cost of the bond
shall be paid by the district .
220.200 [ 2062g - 16 ; 2062g -20 ] Em-
ployes of district ; supplies and equipment .
The board of directors may employ an at-
torney and a chief engineer for the dis-
trict, who shall hold office at the pleasure
of the board and who shall give such bond
as is required by the board. The board
may employ such other engineers , attor-
neys . agents and assistants as may be
needed , and may prescribe the duties and
fix the compensation of all the employes
of the district . The board may maintain ,
furnish and equip an office or offices , and
purchase such office supplies , equipment ,
apparatus , appliances , instruments and
tools as are necessary , which , with all
other necessary expenditures , shall be
taken as a part of the cost of the improve-
ment . The employment of the engineer ,
attorney and other personnel for the dis-
trict shall be evidenced on the minutes of
the board , with their compensation , and
their term of employment shall be at the
pleasure of the board .
220.210 [ 2062g -20 ; 2062g - 21 ] Qualifica-
tions and duties of chief engineer . ( 1 )
The chief engineer shall be a registered
professional engineer under the laws of
this state , and may be an individual , part-
nership or corporation . Before a chief
engineer may be employed his qualifica-
tions must be approved in writing by the
State Board of Health .
(2 ) The chief engineer shall be superin-
tendent of all the works and improve-
ments , and shall make a full report to the
board of directors each year, or oftener
if required . He shall make such sugges-
tions and recommendations to the board
as he deems proper , and shall advise the
board concerning matters pertaining to
engineering , design , construction and
operation of the sanitation system and
district . A copy of his reports shall also
be filed with the commissioner of sanita-
tion districts .
Professional Engineers , Ch . 322
220.220 [ 2062g-22 ] Board to prepare
plan for improvements . The board of di-
rectors shall cause to be prepared a plan
for the improvements for which the dis-
trict was created . The plan shall include
such maps , profiles , plans and other data
and descriptions as are necessary to set
forth properly the location and character
of the work , with estimates of cost and of
the property benefited , taken or damaged .
In the preparation of the plan , the board
may recognize the necessity of future ex-
tensions and enlargements that may result
from enlargements of the area of the dis-
trict , in order that the district improve-
ments may be designed to meet properly
such increased demands .
220.230 [ 2062g -23 ] Other surveys may
be used . If the board of directors finds
that any former survey made by any
other district or in any other manner is
useful for the purposes of the district , the
board may take over the data secured for
such survey, or such other proceedings
as may be useful to it , and if required may
pay the reasonable value thereof , which
shall not exceed the cost of obtaining the
information independently .
220.240 [ 2062g-24 ] Plan must be ap-
proved by State Board of Health . Upon
the completion of the plan the board of
directors shall submit it to the State
Board of Health for approval . If the
State Board of Health refers the plan back
for amendment , the board of directors
shall prepare and submit an amended
plan . If the State Board of Health rejects
the plan , the board of directors shall pro-
ceed as provided in KRS 220.220 to pre-
pare another plan . If the State Board of
Health approves the plan , a copy of the
action of the State Board of Health shall
be filed with the secretary of the board of
directors and by him incorporated into
the records of the district .
146

SANITATION DISTRICTS AND WATER POLLUTION CONTROL § 220.280
220.250 [ 2062g -25 ] Plan to be accepted
and financing fixed by resolution ; altera-
tions . Upon the approval of the official
plan by the State Board of Health the
board of directors shall pass a resolution
accepting the official plan as final and fix-
ing all financing , taxes and rentals as au-
thorized by KRS 220.010 to 220.520 neces-
sary for the construction, maintenance ,
and operation required to place the plan
in effect . After final resolutions are
passed fixing the rates and rentals , no
alterations of the plan shall be made ex-
cept as provided herein and as approved
by the State Board of Health .
220.260 [ 2062g -26 ] Other persons not
to install sanitation improvements with-
out approval ; violation is nuisance . After
the establishment of the district and the
organization of the board of directors , no
person or public corporation shall install
within the district any laterals , trunk
lines , interceptors for the collection or
discharge of sewage or other liquid waste ,
treatment or disposal works , until the
plans therefor have been submitted to and
approved by the board of directors of the
district and the State Board of Health .
Any installation contrary to the provisions
of this section shall constitute a nuisance
and shall be abated by injunction upon
proper application by anyone aggrieved ,
including the district , the commissioner ,
the State Board of Health , or the county
board of health .
220.270 [ 2062g -27 ] Power to carry out
plan by direct labor or contract . The
board of directors may devise , prepare for ,
execute , maintain and operate any or all
works or improvements necessary or de-
sirable to complete , maintain , operate and
protect the works provided for by the
official plan . They may secure and use
men and equipment under the supervision
of the chief engineer or other agents , or
they may let contracts for such works ,
either as a whole or in part .
220.280 [ 2062g - 28 ] Powers of board
limited ; private connections ; type of im-
provements authorized ; rights as to public
corporations ; annual audit . ( 1) The pow-
ers of the board of directors shall be lim-
ited to the construction, maintenance and
operation of such works as are necessary
to carry out the purposes of the district in
improvement of sanitation, as set forth in
KRS 220.030 . The board shall not permit
house and users ' connections at the cost of
the district , and every connection shall be
made under the supervision of the district .
No house or users ' connection shall be
made unless and until the house or user is
provided with an adequate water supply .
(2) In order to effect the proper collec-
tion and disposal of sewage and other liq-
uid wastes produced within the district , to
promote the public health , comfort , con-
venience and welfare , and to accomplish
all other purposes of the district , the board
may clean out , straighten, alter , deepen or
otherwise improve any stream , water-
course or body of water receiving sewage
or other liquid wastes and located in or
out of the district ; fill up any abandoned
or altered stream , watercourse or body of
water located in or out of the district ;
construct and maintain laterals , trunk
sewers , intercepting sewers , siphons ,
pumping stations , treatment and disposal
works and improvements deemed neces-
sary to accomplish the purposes of the dis-
trict and construct , preserve , operate or
maintain such works in or out of the dis-
trict ; construct connections to the works
of the district for the delivery thereto of
sewage and other liquid wastes ; incorpo-
rate with the works of the district or oth-
erwise utilize any public sewers , drains or
other sewerage improvements either with-
out modifications or with repairs , modifi-
cations or changes deemed necessary ; con-
struct any and all of the works and im-
provements across or through any public
or private property in or out of the dis-
trict ; hold , encumber , control , acquire by
donation , purchase or condemnation , and
construct, own , lease , use and sell, any
real or personal property , or any easement
necessary for right of ways or locations
for the works and improvements of the
district , or for any necessary purpose , or
for obtaining or storing material to be used
in constructing and maintaining the works
and improvements .
(3 ) KRS 220.010 to 220.540 shall not
limit or interfere with the right of public
corporations to install , maintain and oper-
ate sewerage systems as otherwise per-
mitted by law , but the board of directors
shall have full power and authority in the
construction and maintenance of improve-
ments for the purposes of the district to
serve the area included within the district ,
and the board of directors may require the
147

§ 220.285 SANITATION DISTRICTS AND WATER POLLUTION CONTROL
use of the improvements of the district by
persons and public corporations included
within the district and for which the im-
provements were installed .
(4) The board of directors shall have an
annual audit made by a certified public ac-
countant , copies of which shall be filed
with the Secretary of State and with the
county judge of the county or counties in
which the sanitation district is located .
220.285 Power to provide sewer service
outside district . The board of directors
of a sanitation district may make con-
tracts or other arrangements to provide
for the collection , disposal and treatment
of sewage and other liquid wastes pro-
duced outside of the district , as the board
of directors may determine ; provided ,
however , that this does not authorize a
sanitation district to provide sewer service
within a city of the first class except with
permission of the board of aldermen of
such city . ( 1960 , c . 71 ; effective June 16 ,
1960)
220.290 [ 2062g -30 ] Contracts for work ,
material and supplies ; how let. All con-
tracts for work , material or supplies that
may exceed five hundred dollars shall be
advertised for bids , not less than seven
days before bids are to be received , by
notice published once a week for two con-
secutive weeks (publication being com-
plete on date of last publication ) in at
least one newspaper of general circulation
within the district where the work is to be
done or the materials or supplies used .
The contract shall be let to the lowest and
best bidder who shall give bond with ap-
proved and ample surety for the faithful
performance of the contract. The notice
shall specify the general nature of the bid
sought , the time and date bids are to be
received , and where and how more specific
information can be obtained . The con-
tract shall be in writing , in duplicate , and
shall be accompanied by or refer to plans
and specifications for the work to be done ,
prepared by the chief engineer . The plans
and specifications shall be made and con-
sidered a part of the contract . The con-
tract shall be adopted by the board and
signed by its president and by the con-
tractor, and shall become a part of the
records of the district .
220.300 [ 2062g -31 ] Real property may
be acquired by district . The district may
acquire by gift , devise , bequest , grant or
purchase solely from the funds provided
under the authority of KRS 220.010 to
220.520 , such real property and interests
therein as are necessary to accomplish its
purpose , upon terms , privies or considera-
tions deemed reasonable by the board of
directors and agreed upon by the board
and the owner . Title to all property ac-
quired by the board shall be taken in the
corporate name of the district .
220.310 [ 2062g -32 ] Condemnation . The
board of directors may , by resolution re-
citing the need , order the condemnation
for the district of any real property or
interest therein that may , in the opinion
of the board, be necessary for the pro-
posed construction of any structure au-
thorized by KRS 220.010 to 220.520 , and
any property taken for a public use may
again be taken by the district if necessary .
Proceedings for condemnation shall be
conducted in the manner prescribed in
KRS 416.010 to 416.080 . No payment or
award in any condemnation proceedings or
for the cost or expense of such proceedings
shall be made except from the funds pro-
vided under the authority of KRS 220.010
to 220.520 .
220.320 [2062g-33 ] Regulations as to use
and construction of works and improve-
ments ; enforcement ; penalties and dam-
ages . Where necessary in order to secure
the best results from the construction , op-
eration and maintenance of the works and
improvements of the district and to pre-
vent their damage from misuse , the board
of directors may make and enforce regula-
tions pertaining to the use by persons and
public corporations of the works and im-
provements of the district . Such regula-
tions may prescribe the design , construc-
tion and use of sewers within the district
and the manner in which connections to
laterals , trunk sewers , intercepting sewers
and to other works of the district shall be
made , may prevent the unnecessaryunnec pollu .
tion of any watercourse or supply within
the district and may prohibit the discharge
into such sewers of any wastes deemed
detrimental to the works and improve-
ments of the district . Such regulations
shall have no effect until approved by the
State Board of Health . The board may re-
cover by civil action from any person or
public corporation violating such regula-
tions , a sum not less than one hundred
148

SANITATION DISTRICTS AND WATER POLLUTION CONTROL § 220.370
dollars nor more than one thousand dollars
for each offense , together with costs . The
directors may enforce by mandamus or
otherwise all necessary and authorized
regulations made by them , and may re-
move any improper construction or close
any connections made improperly or in
violation of the regulations . Any person
or public corporation willfully failing to
comply with the regulations shall be liable
for damages caused by such failure and
for the cost of renewing any construction
damaged or destroyed .
220.330 [2062g-34 ] Removal or adjust-
ment of obstructions . Any person or pub-
lic corporation having buildings , struc-
tures , works , conduits , mains , pipes , tracks
or other physical obstructions in , over or
upon the public streets , lanes , alleys or
highways that interfere with or impede
the progress of construction , maintenance
or repair of the works of the district shall ,
upon reasonable notice from the board of
directors , promptly shift , adjust , accom-
modate or remove the obstruction to meet
the exigencies occasioning such action .
Upon failure of any person or public cor-
poration to make such changes the boardmay do so . The reasonable cost and ex-
pense of such damage shall be met by the
district .
220.340 [2062g-35 ] Stream and rain
gages; surveys and scientific examina-
tions . The board of directors may estab-
lish and maintain stream gages and rain
gagés , and may make such surveys and ex-
aminations of rainfall, stream flow and
other scientific and engineering subjects
as are necessary and proper for the pur-
pose of the district . The board may issue
reports of its findings .
220.350 [ 2062g-36 ] Contracts for assist-
ance with governments , governmental
agencies and others ; operations in adjoin-
ing states . The board of directors may
enter into contracts or other arrangements
with the United States Government or any
department thereof , with persons and pub-
lic corporations , with the government of
this or any other state and with sewerage ,
drainage , conservation , conservancy or
other improvement districts in this or any
other state , for cooperation or assistance
in constructing , maintaining , using and
operating the works of the district or the
waters thereof , or for making surveys and
investigations or reports thereon . The
board may purchase , lease or acquire land
or other property in adjoining states in
order to secure outlets or for other pur-
poses of KRS 220.010 to 220.520 , and may
let contracts or spend money for securing
such outlets or other works in adjoining
states .
220.360 [ 2062g -37 ] Taxes for prelimi-
nary expense . ( 1 ) The board of directors ,
as soon as duly appointed and qualified ,
may levy one, two, or three annual taxes ,
which taxes need not be in successive years ,
or not more than fifteen cents upon each
one hundred dollars of assessed valuation of
property within the district , to be used
for the purpose of paying the expenses of
organization , surveys and plans , and for
other incidental expenses that may be
necessary up to the time money is received
from the sale of bonds . This tax shall be
certified to the auditors of the various
counties and by them to the respective
treasurers of their counties . The tax shall
be based upon the last preceding assess-
ment for state and county purposes , its
collection shall conform to the collection of
taxes for counties , and the same provisions
concerning the nonpayment of taxes shall
apply . The tax shall be added by the county
clerk to the next state and county tax bill
following the levy of the tax by the board
of directors , and shall be collected concur-
rently with the state and county taxes .
Neither the county tax commissioner nor
the county court clerk shall be entitled to
any additional compensation for the serv-
ices rendered in connection with the listing
of the property for taxation nor shall the
sheriff receive any additional compensation
for the collection of the tax . ( 1948 , с. 116 ;
1954 , c . 3, § 1 ; effective June 17, 1954)
220.370 [ 2062g -38 ] Borrowing money
for preliminary expense . To facilitate the
preliminary work and payment therefor ,
the board of directors may borrow money
for the district at a rate of interest not ex-
ceeding six percent per annum , and may
issue and sell, or deliver directly to its
creditors , negotiable warrants signed for
the district by the members of the board
of directors . The board may pledge the
preliminary tax authorized by KRS
220.360 , less a reasonable allowance for
interest , after the tax has been levied , for
the repayment of the warrants . If any
warrant is presented for payment and is
not paid for want of funds in the treasury ,
149

§ 220.380 SANITATION DISTRICTS AND WATER POLLUTION CONTROL
that fact , with the date of refusal to pay ,
shall be endorsed on the back of the war-
rant , and the warrant shall thereafter
draw interest at the rate of six percent per
annum until there is money on hand, de-
rived from the tax levy , sufficient to pay
the amount of the warrant with interest .
220.380 [ 2062g -1 ; 2062g -39 ] Bonds may
be issued by district . To defray the cost
of organizing the district and the pre-
liminary expense after organization , and
for the purpose of acquiring any sanitary
works authorized by KRS 220.010 to
220.520 , and any appurtenances or exten-
sions thereto , either by purchase or con-
struction , the district through its board of
directors may borrow money and issue
negotiable bonds , which shall be known as
" Sanitation District Bonds " and shall be
so engraved or printed on their face fol-
lowing the name of the issuing district .
No such bonds shall be issued until au-
thorized by resolution specifying the pur-
pose for which the funds are to be used
and the proposed undertaking , the amount
of bonds to be issued and the maximum
rate of interest the bonds are to bear .
The resolution shall further provide that
the sanitary works , appurtenances or ex-
tensions are to be acquired pursuant to the
provisions of KRS 220.010 to 220.520 .
220.390 [ 2062g -40 ] Interest rate on
bonds ; time and place of payment . Bonds
authorized under the provisions of KRS
220.380 to 220.520 may be issued bearing
interest at a rate not exceeding six percent
per annum , payable semiannually , and
shall be executed in such manner and be
payable at such time, not exceeding forty
years from the date thereof , and at such
place or places , as the board of directors
shall determine .
220.400 [ 2062g-41 ] Negotiability of
bonds ; tax exemption ; signatures ; how
sold ; maximum yield ; not a debt . Bonds
issued under the provisions of KRS 220.380
to 220.520 shall be negotiable and shall not
be subject to taxation . If any officer
whose signature appears on the bonds or
coupons ceases to be such officer before
delivery of the bonds , his signature shall
nevertheless be valid and sufficient for all
purposes the same as if he had remained in
office until delivery . The bonds shall be
sold in such manner and upon such terms
as the board of directors deem for the best
interest of the district . In no event shall
any bonds be sold on a basis to yield more
than six percent per annum . The bonds
shall be payable solely from the revenue
funds derived from the rentals from serv-
ices rendered by the district to the inhab-
itants thereof , as provided in KRS 220.510 ,
and shall not constitute an indebtedness of
the district within the meaning of the
Constitution . It shall be plainly stated on
the face of each bond that it has been is-
sued under the provisions of KRS 220.010
to 220.520 , and that it does not constitute
an indebtedness of the district within the
meaning of the Constitution .
220.410 [ 2062g -42 ] Use of proceeds of
bonds . All money received from any
bonds issued pursuant to KRS 220.380 to
220.520 shall be applied solely for the pur-
poses set forth in KRS 220.030 and 220.380 ,
except that the money may be used also to
advance the payment of interest on the
bonds during the first three years follow-
ing the date of the bonds .
220.420 [ 2062g -42 ; 2062g -43 ] Bond-
holders ' lien ; enforcement . There shall be
a statutory mortgage lien upon all prop-
erty of the sanitation district in favor of
the holders of the bonds and coupons , to
secure payment of the bonds and coupons .
The sanitary works constructed or ac-
quired by the board of directors , together
with any extensions and appurtenances
thereto , shall remain subject to the lien
until the payment in full of the principal
and interest of the bonds . Any holder of
the bonds or coupons may by proper pro-
ceedings protect and enforce the lien , and
may by proper proceedings , including
mandamus , compel the performance of all
duties required by KRS 220.010 to 220.520 ,
including the making and collection of
sufficient rates , the segregation of the in-
come and revenue and the application
thereof . The venue of such action shall be
in the county in which the district or its
largest area lies .
220.430 [ 2062g -44 ] Receiver in case of
default . If there is any default in the pay-
ment of the principal or interest of any
of the bonds , any court having general
equity jurisdiction may appoint a receiver
to administer the property of the district
on behalf of the district , with power to
charge and collect rates sufficient to pro-
vide for the payment of any of the bonds
150

SANITATION DISTRICTS AND WATER POLLUTION CONTROL § 220.500
T
or obligations outstanding against the
property and for the payment of the oper-
ating expenses , and to apply the income
and revenue in conformity with KRS
220.010 to 220.520 and the resolution re-
ferred to in KRS 220.380 .
220.440 [ 2062g -45 ] Sinking , operating
and depreciation funds to be provided . At
or before the issuance of the bonds , the
board of directors of the district shall , by
resolution , set aside and pledge the reve-
nue derived from services rendered by its
sanitary works into a separate and special
fund to be used and applied in the payment
of the cost , maintenance , operation and de-
preciation of the works . The resolution
shall definitely fix and determine the
amount of revenue necessary to be set
apart and applied to the payment of the
principal and interest of the bonds , and
the proportion of the balance of the reve-
nue to be set aside as a proper and ade-
quate depreciation fund . The remaining
portion of the balance shall be set aside for
reasonable and proper operation and main-
tenance of the works , except as provided
in KRS 220.450 . The rates to be charged
for the service from the works shall be
fixed and revised from time to time , in
accordance with the provisions of KRS
220.510 , so as to be sufficient to provide
for the payment of interest upon all bonds
and to create a sinking fund to pay the
principal due , to provide for the operation
and maintenance of the works , and to pro-
vide an adequate depreciation account .
220.450 [ 2062g -46 ] Use of surplus in op-
erating fund . If a surplus is accumulated
in the operation and maintenance fund
equal to the cost of maintaining and oper-
ating the works of the district during the
remainder of the calendar , operating or
fiscal year , as may be provided by the
resolution required by KRS 220.440 , and
the cost of maintaining and operating the
works during the succeeding like calendar ,
operating or fiscal year, any excess over
that amount may be transferred at any
time by the board of directors to the de-
preciation account to be used for improve-
ments , extensions or additions to the
works .
220.460 [2062g-47 ] Use of depreciation
fund ; investment . The funds accumulated
in the depreciation account shall be ex-
pended in balancing the depreciation in
the works of the district or in making new
construction , extensions or additions . Any
such accumulations may be invested by the
board of directors in the manner author-
ized by statute for other trust funds . The
income from such investments shall be
carried in the depreciation account .
Investment of trust funds , Ch . 386
220.470 [ 2062g-49 ] Refunding bonds .
The district may issue new bonds for the
purpose of providing funds for the pay-
ment of any outstanding bonds , in accord-
ance with the procedure prescribed for the
issuance of the original bonds . The re-
funding bonds shall be secured to the same
extent and shall have the same source of
payment as the bonds that are refunded.
220.480 [ 2062g -50 ] Additional bonds to
complete original project . If the board of
directors finds , prior to completion of the
sanitary works already begun , that the
bonds originally authorized will be insuffi-
cient to accomplish the purpose desired ,
additional bonds of like standing with the
original issue may be authorized and
is-
sued subject
tothe same procedure
.
220.490
[2062g
-
51] Original issue may
